description:

 connects elite creative and technical talent with leading AI research labs. Headquartered in San Francisco, our investors include

 Software Engineer
   

  

**Type:**

title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
**At a glance**
 Are you passionate about both advanced analytics and empowering others to grow in their analytical capabilities? Do you have the expertise to lead by example while fostering a culture of statistical thinking? If so, we have the perfect role for you!
   

  

 Channel Security is seeking a Senior Data Analyst to join our team; Fraud Monitoring. In this role, you will not only be a technical expert and a strong individual contributor but also a key leader in advancing the analytics maturity of a team with a business\-driven background. You will serve as a pillar for analytics standards and help shape the way we use data to make impactful decisions.
   

  

**Your job**
 As a Senior Data Analyst, in the Grid Channel Security, you will work together with fraud experts, data scientists and machine learning engineers to safeguard our customers from criminals. This includes threats such as phishing, account takeover, lost or stolen debit cards, e\-commerce fraud, and more.
   

  

 As a pivotal member of our team, you will dive into diverse and complex datasets to extract actionable insights that drive our fight against financial fraud. Your work will involve understanding fraud trends across various payment channels, assessing the potential of various data fields to enhance detection capabilities, recognizing seasonal patterns, and creating reliable forecasts. You will be a vital bridge between our fraud experts and data science team, with your insights acting as catalysts for advancing our fraud detection strategies. In this role, you’ll also be responsible for educating fraud experts on the data analysis domain, empowering them with the knowledge and tools they need to stay ahead of emerging threats. Alongside focused analysis, you will be collaborating with colleagues to brainstorm how innovative data science solutions can help us stay one step ahead of criminals in the ongoing cat\-and\-mouse game.
   

  

 ABN AMRO is a future driven bank that is working continuously on unlocking the power of data: you will be part of this transformation!
   

  

**Working environment**
 Grid Channel Security within Corporate Information Security Office (CISO) is responsible for the prevention and detection of payment fraud, and deciding the policy and strategy in the area of fraud. The Fraud Monitoring team that you are going to be part of, is a motivated multidisciplinary group of approximately 25 colleagues working with a focus on protecting customers. We work in a hybrid way and our day to day work is organized by the agile and scrum principles. CISO is recognised as having an informal work atmosphere where everyone has a heart for their job and is prepared to help others.
   

  

**Your profile**
 You are a proactive self\-starter with a keen eye for data analysis questions. You don’t need to be told what to do. Instead, you’ll look for insights people haven’t thought of yet before or didn’t have the time or skills (yet) to succeed. You are able to present the insights and have a strong capability of mentoring your colleagues in doing the same.
   

  

*Key Responsibilities* 
 :
   

  

* Conduct hands\-on data analysis to support business as usual activities, track key departmental KPIs and to develop new insight into fraud patterns.
* Design and deliver training sessions, workshops, and one\-on\-one coaching to enhance analytical skills across the team.
* Partner with leadership to identify opportunities for leveraging data to achieve strategic goals.
* Partner with data scientists to transform insights into hypotheses and experiments to improve fraud detection. Fostering the collaboration between data scientists and business experts.
* Proactively look for and drive improvements in our analytics. Including; reporting, dashboarding and data driven decision making.


*Qualifications and technical skills* 
 :
   

  

* A master’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Econometrics, Engineering, Physics, Mathematics, or similar).
* At least 3 years’ experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar role.
* Proficiency in data analysis tools and software, experience and exposure to at least 4 from the list below:
* SQL (must have)
* Python (must have)
* Databricks / Snowflake, or other Cloud analytics environments.
* MS Power Bi, Tableau, Google Looker
* Pyspark and Apache Hive
* No\-SQL technologies (Mongo DB, ElasticSearch or similar).
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


*About you* 
 :
   

  

* You are an energetic Firestarter, proactively looking for and finding relevant data analysis questions
* A Technical Expert with strong analytical skills and experience in applying statistical techniques to real\-world business problems.
* A Teacher and Mentor with a deep passion for upskilling and helping your fellow team members grow their analytical capabilities, especially guiding them to adopt statistical rigor during their analytical work.
* A Problem Solver who thrives on tackling complex challenges and delivering actionable insights.
* Experience and understanding of Scrum and Agile methodologies, with the ability to apply these frameworks effectively in project management and team collaboration.
* Ability to maintain accuracy and thoroughness in work, ensuring that documentation, data analysis, and project deliverables are thoroughly reviewed and free of errors.


**We are offering**
* Flexibility in a hybrid work environment to support strong work\-life balance;
* An industry\-competitive salary based on a 36 or 40\-hour work week;
* A supplementary benefit budget of 11%, which you can spend on pre\-tax fringe benefits;
* A personal development budget of EUR 1\.000 per year;
* An annual public transportation pass ;
* A solid pension plan.


**Interested?**
 Does this opportunity sound like something you’d be interested in? Please send us your application as soon as possible. We look forward to meeting you.
   

  

**Your Future: Inclusive, Innovative, Sustainable**
 At ABN AMRO, we believe in "
 **Banking for better, for generations to come.** 
 " Equal opportunities for everyone are a crucial foundation, as we strive for an inclusive culture where all employees feel seen, heard, and valued. Our vision of
 **being a personal bank in the digital age** 
 aligns perfectly with the demand for surprising insights and innovative solutions, born from a diverse interplay of cultures and experiences. We focus on customer experience, sustainability, and building a future\-proof bank, conducting annual reviews to ensure equal pay for equal work. Join a bank that embraces ingenuity and ambition, and make an impact with us for a better future.
   

  

*If you want to apply for the B\-Able or Reboot program, make sure to mention it both in the title and the content of your resume.*
**Disclaimer external recruitment agencies**
 External recruitment agencies need to have a signed agreement with ABN AMRO BANK N.V., executed by a Talent Acquisition Specialist, when submitting a resume to a vacancy. No unsolicited services or offers, please.

title: AI Automation Engineer
description:
**About Samotics**


At Samotics, we monitor industrial equipment through Electrical Signature Analysis (ESA), using advanced AI to analyse the electrical “heartbeat” of motors, pumps, and drives.


Our SAM4 platform detects failures from inside the motor control cabinet, without installing sensors on the asset itself. Today, we help industrial companies across water, chemicals, mining, and oil \& gas improve reliability, reduce downtime, and use energy more efficiently.


As we scale globally, we are investing in the next layer of leverage: internal AI and automation systems that help our teams move faster, work smarter, and operate at higher quality.

**The Role**


We are looking for an AI Automation Engineer to build the internal systems and workflows that make Samotics more effective.


In this role, you will identify high\-impact opportunities for automation across the business and turn them into scalable solutions using GenAI, APIs, internal tooling, and lightweight software. You will work across teams to improve how work gets done, from reducing repetitive manual tasks to building AI\-powered workflows that enhance speed, consistency, and decision\-making.


This is a hands\-on builder role for someone who combines strong technical fundamentals with a very strong and demonstrable affinity for GenAI. You are excited by the practical side of AI, not just experimenting, but turning ideas into reliable systems that deliver real value.

**Your Challenge**

**1\. Build AI\-powered workflows**  

 Design and implement GenAI\-enabled workflows that improve how teams operate on a day\-to\-day basis. This can include research support, drafting assistance, knowledge workflows, internal copilots, or other AI applications that create clear business impact.

**2\. Automate business processes end\-to\-end**  

 Translate manual, repetitive, or fragmented processes into scalable automations. You connect systems, data, and actions across teams using APIs, orchestration tools, scripts, and sound automation logic.

**3\. Turn GenAI use cases into production\-ready solutions**  

 Prototype, test, and improve practical GenAI applications. You know how to improve output quality, structure context, and apply the right level of validation to make AI useful in real workflows.

**4\. Build maintainable internal tooling**  

 Create solutions that are not only fast to launch, but also reliable and maintainable. You document what you build, improve existing workflows over time, and contribute to a strong technical foundation for internal automation.

**Example Tools \& Stack**


You may work with tools such as:

* LLM APIs and GenAI platforms
* Python or TypeScript
* Workflow automation platforms
* REST APIs and webhooks
* Internal data sources and lightweight databases
* Custom scripts and internal tooling


The exact stack will evolve, and you will help shape it.

 **What You Bring**

* 2–4 years of relevant work experience in a technical role such as automation engineering, software engineering, AI engineering, solutions engineering, or similar
* Strong preference for a technical academic background, such as computer science, AI, engineering, mathematics, physics, or a related field
* Hands\-on experience building automations, integrations, scripts, or internal tools
* A very strong and demonstrable affinity with GenAI and LLM\-based workflows
* Strong technical curiosity and the ability to quickly learn new tools and approaches
* Experience working with APIs, structured data, and system logic
* A practical mindset: you like building things that actually get used
* Strong problem\-solving skills and the ability to work independently
* Clear communication skills and the ability to work with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders

 **What Makes This Role Unique**


This role sits at the intersection of AI, automation, and business impact.


You will help define how Samotics uses GenAI internally, work on high\-leverage problems across teams, and build solutions that directly improve how the company operates. It is a role with a lot of ownership, a lot of room to build, and a direct path from idea to implementation.

**What We Offer**

* Stock Appreciation Rights (SAR) scheme
* Competitive compensation
* A high\-autonomy environment with real ownership
* The opportunity to work on applied AI in a fast\-growing industrial tech company
* A product that solves real\-world reliability and energy efficiency challenges
* A culture built on freedom and responsibility

**Interested?**  

We’d love to meet you.

title: IT Software Engineer (m/w/d)
description:
**Company Description**  

Everience is an international consulting group delivering AI\-augmented digital services and placing people at the heart of the AI revolution.


With a presence in Europe, Africa, Asia and America, Everience offers its 4,000\-strong workforce the most demanding and stimulating environment in which to transform and develop their skills, learning about new AI\-based roles and building their future employability.


Through its Symbiotic Academy the group offers a unique hub for training, practical application and exchange where everyone can experiment, learn, and progress in the fields of artificial intelligence and data.


In accordance with its core purpose of orchestrating the symbiotic relationship between humans and AI in the workplace, Everience is making the augmented employee the driving force of a “symbiotic age”, where AI enhances talents and opens up new career opportunities.

 **Job Description**  

We are seeking a skilled IT Software Engineer with experience in the oil and gas industry to design, develop, and maintain software solutions that support operational efficiency, data management, and digital transformation initiatives. The ideal candidate will have a strong technical background and a solid understanding of industry\-specific systems, including real\-time data environments and industrial operations.

 **Qualifications** **Key Responsibilities:**

* Design, develop, and maintain scalable software applications supporting upstream, midstream, or downstream operations
* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams (engineering, operations, and data teams) to deliver integrated technical solutions
* Develop and manage APIs, backend services, and data pipelines for real\-time and historical data processing
* Support integration of IT systems with operational technologies (OT), including SCADA and industrial control systems
* Analyze large datasets to improve operational performance, efficiency, and decision\-making
* Ensure system reliability, performance optimization, and cybersecurity compliance
* Participate in troubleshooting, debugging, and resolving production issues
* Contribute to system architecture design and continuous improvement initiatives

**Requirements :**

* Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field
* Proven experience as a Software Engineer or similar role
* Prior experience working within the oil and gas or energy sector is required
* Strong proficiency in programming languages such as Python, Java, C\#, or similar
* Experience with cloud platforms, databases, and distributed systems
* Familiarity with real\-time data systems, automation, or industrial integrations
* Understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC) and agile methodologies
* Strong problem\-solving and analytical skills
* Fluent in **Dutch\& English**

 **Additional Information** **Preferred Qualifications:**  

* Experience with SCADA systems, IoT platforms, or industrial data systems
* Knowledge of data engineering, ETL processes, or time\-series data management
* Familiarity with industry regulations, safety standards, and operational workflows
* Experience working with geographically distributed teams or field operations
*All our positions are open to both women and men and are, of course, open to people with disabilities.*

title: Supplier Quality Engineer
description:
**Please note: we can’t support with relocation \& visa support for this role.**

**About Vitestro**


Founded in 2017 in Utrecht, Vitestro is pioneering the future of blood collection with the Aletta® Autonomous Robotic Phlebotomy Device™ (ARPD™). This groundbreaking medical device combines advanced multi\-modal imaging (near\-infrared, ultrasound, and Doppler ultrasound) with robotics and AI to perform the entire diagnostic blood draw procedure autonomously.


By addressing critical healthcare staffing shortages and improving patient experience, Vitestro is transforming one of the most common and essential medical procedures. With more than 90 team members and growing rapidly, we are scaling our impact. As we placed our first devices with customers, we are now expanding our team to ensure successful implementation and long\-term reliability.


At Vitestro, we are committed to continuous innovation and improvement.

**Let's transform healthcare together and join us as our new Supplier Quality Engineer!**

**About the role**


As our Supplier Quality Engineer, you will play a key role in building and strengthening the supplier quality foundation behind our autonomous blood collection technology. You operate at the intersection of Operations, Quality, Engineering, and Supply Chain, ensuring that externally manufactured parts and subassemblies meet the quality standards required for a reliable and scalable medical device.


You will work closely with suppliers as well as internal stakeholders to understand manufacturing processes, identify quality risks, and drive structural improvements. In this role, you combine technical understanding with a hands\-on quality mindset. You know how to translate supplier performance, production realities, and regulatory expectations into practical actions that improve product quality, supplier accountability, and long\-term operational robustness.

**This is what your success looks like**


After 12 months, supplier scorecards are in place, supplier improvement plans are actively running, and there is a clearer and more consistent way of managing supplier quality performance across the supply base. Critical process steps at appointed suppliers are mapped and understood, and the most important quality gaps towards ISO 13485 have been identified and translated into concrete actions.


You have helped Vitestro build stronger supplier partnerships, with suppliers that take ownership of quality and recognize Vitestro as a professional and preferred customer. Internally, you are seen as a trusted quality partner who helps connect operational realities, supplier capabilities, and product quality requirements in a pragmatic and structured way.

**What you’ll be doing**

* Understand and assess the manufacturing processes of appointed suppliers, including critical process steps and quality control methods.
* Build, implement, and continuously improve supplier quality processes, standards, and ways of working.
* Identify gaps towards ISO 13485 and support the organization in strengthening supplier\-related quality controls.
* Develop supplier scorecards and quality KPIs to monitor supplier performance and drive accountability.
* Define, coordinate, and follow up on supplier improvement plans.
* Support supplier qualification activities and evaluate new suppliers from a quality and manufacturability perspective.
* Investigate supplier non\-conformities and drive root cause analysis and corrective actions in collaboration with suppliers and internal teams.
* Partner with Operations, Engineering, and Quality colleagues to improve incoming quality, process robustness, and long\-term supplier performance.
* Contribute to building a scalable supplier quality approach that supports Vitestro’s growth and product reliability ambitions

 **What you’ll bring**

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Manufacturing Engineering, or a related technical field.
* Experience with manufacturing processes and quality controls in a high\-complexity, low\-volume supply chain environment.
* Solid understanding of process improvement methodologies such as Kaizen, Lean, or 7\-step problem solving / 8D.
* Basic to solid knowledge of quality systems and structured quality improvement methods.
* Experience working with external suppliers in a manufacturing, quality, or supplier quality role.
* Strong analytical skills and the ability to understand how supplier processes impact product quality and operational performance.
* A structured, pragmatic, and hands\-on working style.
* Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to work cross\-functionally and externally with suppliers.
* Good command of English.

**Nice to have**

* Broad understanding of manufacturing processes such as machining, sheet metal, PCBA, plastic injection moulding, and test.
* Experience at a first\-tier supplier in a high\-complexity / low\-volume supply chain, supplying OEM customers (such as Philips, ASML, Elekta, or Thermo Fisher).
* Exposure to or understanding of ISO 13485\.
* Experience in MedTech, high\-tech systems, or another regulated manufacturing environment.

**What we offer**

* Competitive salary including pension plan.
* 25 days of annual leave based on a full\-time position.
* Hybrid work model.
* Working together in a high\-skilled team with our home base in Utrecht.
* Frequent out\-of\-work activities with our team, and annual company weekend away.
* Annual training budget of €2000\.\-
* Opportunity to be key shaper of a new global industry niche: autonomous medical robotics.

  

We offer an environment where you can make a lot of impact, as you are joining a growing scale\-up in MedTech. Our team of driven and talented colleagues enjoys solving the challenges in developing, manufacturing, and completing our breakthrough medical device. While working in an open culture, we strive for excellence in all domains. We believe in taking courage and like to challenge each other. We learn and teach, regardless of age, nationality or gender.

***For recruitment agencies – we are currently happy with the way we manage our process and fill our vacancies with our own efforts. Thank you.***

title: Postdoc SmartWeave: A textile Sensor for the Monitoring of Sweat Metabolites
description:
**Postdoc SmartWeave: A textile Sensor for the Monitoring of Sweat Metabolites**
================================================================================

***Imagine being able to enhance the quality of life of people with bipolar disorder. You can make it happen. Join our project Wearable sensors for monitoring of sweat metabolites.***

 **Job description**



Patients with chronic conditions regularly need to visit the hospital for check\-up and the taking of a blood sample. This is not only burdensome for the patient but the lab analyses are time consuming and expensive. In this project we develop a smart armband which analyses sweat continuously and unobtrusively during everyday activities. This can be achieved by weaving the sensors into the material and using a small clip\-on electronics unit. With that we can help patients with a bipolar disorder taking lithium as medication but it can also be used to monitor other metabolites like glucose or to detect dehydration.

  



As the post\-doc you will work at the faculty of EEMCS in collaboration with a PhD student at the faculty of Industrial Design Engineering of TU Delft. You will be working on the sensing system in the armband. This will include the sensing yarn, a microfluidics system, electronicis and communication. The main aim of this project is to detect lithium in sweat. However, other ions will be considered at a later stage of the project. You will furthermore integrate these yarns in a textile structure that takes care of the sweat absorption and transport and protects the sensors.

  



In the final year of the project, the sensor bands will undergo testing with patients in collaboration with the Leiden University Medical Center (LUMC). You will work in close cooperation with a postdoc at the Bioelectronics section of the Electrical Engineering faculty at TU Delft.

  


**Job requirements**


* You hold a PhD degree in chemical engineering, electrical engineering, or another relevant field related to electrochemical sensors and human health monitoring.
* You have the social skills to deal with many stakeholders, from fellow academics to patients.
* You have experience with analysis of sensor data.
* You have an affinity for patient care and are able to communicate with patients.
* You have a good command of written and spoken English.

  



**TU Delft (Delft University of Technology)**



Delft University of Technology is built on strong foundations. As creators of the world\-famous Dutch waterworks and pioneers in biotech, TU Delft is a top international university combining science, engineering and design. It delivers world class results in education, research and innovation to address challenges in the areas of energy, climate, mobility, health and digital society. For generations, our engineers have proven to be entrepreneurial problem\-solvers, both in business and in a social context.

  



At TU Delft we embrace diversity as one of our core values and we actively engage to be a university where you feel at home and can flourish. We value different perspectives and qualities. We believe this makes our work more innovative, the TU Delft community more vibrant and the world more just. Together, we imagine, invent and create solutions using technology to have a positive impact on a global scale. That is why we invite you to apply. Your application will receive fair consideration.

  



Challenge. Change. Impact!

  




**Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Mathematics and Computer Science**



The Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Mathematics and Computer Science (EEMCS) brings together three scientific disciplines. Combined, they reinforce each other and are the driving force behind the technology we all use in our daily lives. Technology such as the electricity grid, which our faculty is helping to make completely sustainable and future\-proof. At the same time, we are developing the chips and sensors of the future, whilst also setting the foundations for the software technologies to run on this new generation of equipment – which of course includes AI. Meanwhile we are pushing the limits of applied mathematics, for example mapping out disease processes using single cell data, and using mathematics to simulate gigantic ash plumes after a volcanic eruption. In other words: there is plenty of room at the faculty for ground\-breaking research. We educate innovative engineers and have excellent labs and facilities that underline our strong international position. In total, more than 1000 employees and 4,000 students work and study in this innovative environment.

  



, Mathematics and Computer Science.

  


**Conditions of employment**


* Duration of contract is 1 year with option to extend another 2 years (total three years)
* A job of 36\-40 hours per week.
* Salary and benefits are in accordance with the Collective Labour Agreement for Dutch Universities.
* An excellent pension scheme via the ABP.
* The possibility to compile an individual employment package every year.
* Discount with health insurers on supplemental packages.
* Flexible working week.
* Every year, 232 leave hours (at 38 hours). You can also sell or buy additional leave hours via the individual choice budget.
* Plenty of opportunities for education, training and courses.
* Partially paid parental leave
* Attention for working healthy and energetically with the vitality program.

  

  

Will you need to relocate to the Netherlands for this job? TU Delft is committed to make your move as smooth as possible! The HR unit, Coming to Delft Service, offers information on their website to help you prepare your relocation. In addition, Coming to Delft Service organises events to help you settle in the Netherlands, and expand your (social) network in Delft. A Dual Career Programme is available, to support your accompanying partner with their job search in the Netherlands. .

  


**Additional information**  

If you would like more information about this vacancy or the selection procedure, please contact prof. Paddy French, via p.j.french@tudelft.nl.

  

**Application procedure**  

Are you interested in this vacancy? Please apply no later than **4 May 2026** upload the following documents:


* CV
* Motivational letter


You can address your application to prof. Paddy French.  

  

**Please note:**


* You can apply online. We will not process applications sent by email and/or post.
* As part of knowledge security, TU Delft conducts a risk assessment during the recruitment of personnel. We do this, among other things, to prevent the unwanted transfer of sensitive knowledge and technology. The assessment is based on information provided by the candidates themselves, such as their motivation letter and CV, and takes place at the final stages of the selection process. When the outcome of the assessment is negative, the candidate will be informed. The processing of personal data in the context of the risk assessment is carried out on the legal basis of the GDPR: performing a public task in the public interest. You can find more information about this assessment on our website about knowledge security.
* Please do not contact us for unsolicited services.

title: Data Analyst / Data Visualization Expert (F/M/X)
description:
Job description
  
  

Amaris Consulting is looking for a Data Visualization \& Analytics Specialist who is passionate about turning data into clear, actionable insights. In this role, you will analyze complex datasets, build impactful dashboards, and help stakeholders make data\-driven decisions across the organization. This position is ideal for a data professional who combines strong analytical skills with a talent for storytelling and visual communication, and who enjoys working at the intersection of data, technology, and business. The role will support a key client of ours.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
  

As a Data Visualization \& Analytics Specialist, your role may include:
  
  

Analyzing datasets to identify trends, correlations, and patterns
  
Designing and developing dashboards and reports using tools such as Power BI, Tableau, QlikSense, or QuickSight
  
Translating stakeholder requirements into actionable data insights
  
Communicating findings clearly to both technical and non\-technical audiences
  
Ensuring data quality and integrity through continuous auditing and validation
  
Collaborating with cross\-functional teams to embed data\-driven insights into business strategies
  
Providing guidance on best practices in data visualization and data storytelling
  
Applying machine learning techniques to enhance predictive analytics and uncover deeper patterns in data
  
Staying up to date with emerging trends and techniques in analytics, machine learning, and visualization
  
Your Profile
  
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field
  
3\+ years of experience in data analysis and data visualization
  
Strong experience with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, QlikSense, QuickSight, or similar)
  
Solid analytical skills with hands\-on experience in SQL, Python, and/or R
  
Hands\-on experience with machine learning — and a genuine enthusiasm for applying it to real\-world data challenges
  
Experience with Java for data processing or system integration
  
Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear insights
  
Detail\-oriented, structured, and quality\-driven
  
  

**Nice to have:**  

Experience in Dutch
  
Why Join Us?
  
Contribute to impactful and innovative engineering projects across the Netherlands.
  
Join an international consulting environment focused on growth, digital transformation, and continuous learning.
  
Collaborate with major global industry leaders across sectors such as technology, manufacturing, and life sciences.
  
Enjoy a hybrid work model with a blend of office and remote work.
  
Work within a diverse, international network supported by global hubs and centers of excellence.
  
Benefit from additional perks such as performance bonuses and mobility options.
  
  

Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. For this reason, we welcome applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics
  
  

Who are we?
  
Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System \& Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success.
  
  

At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience. We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like:
  
  

**Brief Call:** Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!
  
  

Interviews (the average number of interviews is 3 \- the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities!
  
  

**Case study:** Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem\-solving scenario, etc.
  
  

As you know, every person is different and so is every role in a company. That is why we have to adapt accordingly, and the process may differ slightly at times. However, please know that we always put ourselves in the candidate's shoes to ensure they have the best possible experience.
  
We look forward to meeting you!

title: AI Automation Engineer
description:
**Job Description**
**About Samotics 🏢**
 At Samotics, we monitor industrial equipment through Electrical Signature Analysis (ESA), using advanced AI to analyse the electrical “heartbeat” of motors, pumps, and drives.
   

  

 Our SAM4 platform detects failures from inside the motor control cabinet, without installing sensors on the asset itself. Today, we help industrial companies across water, chemicals, mining, and oil \& gas improve reliability, reduce downtime, and use energy more efficiently.
   

  

 As we scale globally, we are investing in the next layer of leverage: internal AI and automation systems that help our teams move faster, work smarter, and operate at higher quality.
   

  

**The Role 👥**
 We are looking for an AI Automation Engineer to build the internal systems and workflows that make Samotics more effective.
   

  

 In this role, you will identify high\-impact opportunities for automation across the business and turn them into scalable solutions using GenAI, APIs, internal tooling, and lightweight software. You will work across teams to improve how work gets done, from reducing repetitive manual tasks to building AI\-powered workflows that enhance speed, consistency, and decision\-making.
   

  

 This is a hands\-on builder role for someone who combines strong technical fundamentals with a very strong and demonstrable affinity for GenAI. You are excited by the practical side of AI, not just experimenting, but turning ideas into reliable systems that deliver real value.
   

  

**Your Challenge 🎯**
* Build AI\-powered workflows Design and implement GenAI\-enabled workflows that improve how teams operate on a day\-to\-day basis. This can include research support, drafting assistance, knowledge workflows, internal copilots, or other AI applications that create clear business impact.
* Automate business processes end\-to\-end Translate manual, repetitive, or fragmented processes into scalable automations. You connect systems, data, and actions across teams using APIs, orchestration tools, scripts, and sound automation logic.
* Turn GenAI use cases into production\-ready solutions Prototype, test, and improve practical GenAI applications. You know how to improve output quality, structure context, and apply the right level of validation to make AI useful in real workflows.
* Build maintainable internal tooling Create solutions that are not only fast to launch, but also reliable and maintainable. You document what you build, improve existing workflows over time, and contribute to a strong technical foundation for internal automation.


**Example Tools \& Stack**
**You May Work With Tools Such As**
* LLM APIs and GenAI platforms
* Python or TypeScript
* Workflow automation platforms
* REST APIs and webhooks
* Internal data sources and lightweight databases
* Custom scripts and internal tooling


 The exact stack will evolve, and you will help shape it.
   

  

**Job Requirements**
**What You Bring ⭐**
* 2–4 years of relevant work experience in a technical role such as automation engineering, software engineering, AI engineering, solutions engineering, or similar
* Strong preference for a technical academic background, such as computer science, AI, engineering, mathematics, physics, or a related field
* Hands\-on experience building automations, integrations, scripts, or internal tools
* A very strong and demonstrable affinity with GenAI and LLM\-based workflows
* Strong technical curiosity and the ability to quickly learn new tools and approaches
* Experience working with APIs, structured data, and system logic
* A practical mindset: you like building things that actually get used
* Strong problem\-solving skills and the ability to work independently
* Clear communication skills and the ability to work with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders


**What Makes This Role Unique**
 This role sits at the intersection of AI, automation, and business impact.
   

  

 You will help define how Samotics uses GenAI internally, work on high\-leverage problems across teams, and build solutions that directly improve how the company operates. It is a role with a lot of ownership, a lot of room to build, and a direct path from idea to implementation.
   

  

**What We Offer 🚀**
* Stock Appreciation Rights (SAR) scheme
* Competitive compensation
* A high\-autonomy environment with real ownership
* The opportunity to work on applied AI in a fast\-growing industrial tech company
* A product that solves real\-world reliability and energy efficiency challenges
* A culture built on freedom and responsibility


**Interested?**
 We’d love to meet you.

title: Technical Co-Founder & Architect - AI Native Spatial Design Platform
description:
**Vibalist** 
 is building the "Spotify for interior design"—a generative platform for physical spaces that merges spatial logic with aesthetic intelligence.
 



  
 



 We have developed a
 **breakthrough UX** 
 that delivers magical ideation \+ deterministic control, making users feel like visual creators rather than prompt engineers. At the core of our vision, we are architecting a
 **proprietary taste engine** 
 : a sophisticated recommendation system that suggests professional\-grade compositions based on curated design principles, not random generation.
 



  
 



 We are an Amsterdam\-based (creative \+ marketing) founding team seeking a
 **Technical Co\-founder \& Architect** 
 to co\-envision the product and lead the development. We are looking for a true partner to complete our founding trio as the technical lead, offering substantial
 **founding equity** 
 and a full seat at the table.
 



  
 



 Our use cases scale from individual decor item discovery to complete home transformations \- serving everyone from design beginners to discerning professionals.
 



  
 



**The Technical Challenge** 




 You will be the primary architect of a multi\-modal ecosystem designed to bridge the gap between aesthetic data and structured 3D environments. This involves:
 


* **The Taste Engine:** 
 Building a recommendation system (RecSys) that uses knowledge graphs and semantic search to navigate a large library of real\-world assets.
* **Discovery Architecture:** 
 Developing "fuzzy logic" and personalization algorithms where "taste" is a quantifiable data point learned from editorial curation and user interactions.
* **3D Scene Logic:** 
 Scene graph architecture, spatial reasoning, and layout constraint systems.
* **Visual Pipeline:** 
 Integrating 2D diffusion models (e.g., ControlNet, Flux) with a Three.js/WebGPU\-based proxy geometry pipeline for real\-time, browser\-based interaction.
* **Computer Vision \& Spatial Intelligence** 
 : Architect a system for high\-fidelity scene segmentation \- leveraging frameworks like Meta’s SAM (3D) \- to automate tagging and taxonomy across a vast library of interior design and art.



  
 



**Ideal Background** 




 We are looking for a "Product Architect" with platform\-level engineering skills and solid architectural instincts who has deep expertise in either 3D Graphics or Applied AI, with the architectural range to lead the integration of both.
 



  
 



 You might be a fit if you are expert\-level in at least one of these areas specifically and eager to architect the connections between them:
 



  
 


* **Discovery/RecSys:** 
 Building recommendation engines, discovery platforms, or large\-scale personalization systems.
* **Creative Tools:** 
 Shipping complex Three.js / WebGPU applications or building scene editors/game engines.
* **Knowledge Engineering:** 
 Working with and optimizing vector databases (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate), Graph\-RAG, or ontologies.
* **AI Integration:** 
 Experience in multi\-modal AI (CLIP/Multimodal Embeddings) or world model integration is a massive plus.



  
 



**Personal Profile** 



* Hands\-on builder mindset and appetite for risk, ownership, and speed.
* Comfortable with early\-stage ambiguity.
* Entrepreneurial drive to shape both technology and business.
* Interest in creativity tools, design software, or spatial computing.



  
 



**What you’ll get** 



* Co\-founder equity reflecting your foundational role.
* Competitive salary following our upcoming Pre\-Seed round.
* The chance to define a genuinely novel technical architecture at the convergence of AI, RecSys, and visual design.
* Build in one of Europe's most vibrant tech and design ecosystems.



  
 



**NL / Amsterdam preferred.** 




  
 



 If this sounds interesting, please apply and we’ll share more details and the persona demo videos.

title: Senior AI/ML Engineer
description:
**How do you make our customers happy?**
 At bol, we handle a lot of customer service cases most of which are resolved via our call center. Customers prefer self\-service, and enabling this could make life of our customers significant easier. We believe Agentic AI, combined with robust business process management, can transform these workflows by autonomously resolving cases, complying with policies, and delivering personalized responses. This is a pioneering opportunity to apply cutting\-edge AI at scale in retail, creating structural impact and redefining customer experience.
   

  

**The biggest challenge**
 Automating processes for customer service by combining AI usage and workflow processes in a fast‑changing environment with a strong focus on happy customers. While building reliable solutions you’ll foster trust, using best practices, and balance innovation speed with bol’s high standards for security, compliance, and architectural integrity. You operate in a domain where technology, policies, and expectations shift quickly. This requires curiosity, flexibility, and a mindset focused on establishing structure where there currently is none.
   

  

**What You’ll Do As Senior AI/ML Engineer**
 We are seeking an experienced AI/ML Engineer to join our Core Service department. Working in our team, you can really make an impact on our customers as we are talking with them, every day.
   

  

 You, and the team you’ll work with, will be responsible for designing and implementing the automated workflows to solve customer problems, integrating Agentic AI in combination with policies and procedures improvements. This will create the framework and core guidelines for future scalability, which also means you’ll possess great communication skills, able to present learnings to others, share best practices, and coach/mentor other team members.
   

  

 We expect you to be an experienced AI/ML Engineer, already having seen millions of lines of code. Next to that we expect someone that has an open engineering mind, making their decisions based on programming’s best practices. As a Senior Engineer you should have good understanding of system design, system architecture, distributed systems, AI evaluation models, design patterns and integration patterns, and keep security in the back of your mind when developing new models.
   

  

**Technology/experience Needed**
* Strong Python skills\& hands\-on experience with LLMs and GenAI (e.g. Google GenAI/Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, open\-source models via Hugging Face), including prompting, fine\-tuning or adapter\-based methods. We are framework\- agnostic, experience with any major ML/LLM framework is welcome, none is a hard requirement.
* Practical knowledge of RAG / grounding: embeddings, vector databases (e.g. Pinecone, ChromaDB, Weaviate, Qdrant, pgvector etc.) and retrieval pipelines for question\-answering use cases.
* Familiarity agent frameworks (Google agentic frameworks such as Google ADK / Vertex AI Agent Builder, or equivalent frameworks (e.g. LangChain, LlamaIndex)
* Experience with the full model lifecycle, including dataset preparation, training pipelines, offline/online evaluation, deployment strategies, model registry, automated retraining, rollback, and retirement of production models.
* Experience monitoring production ML/LLM systems for quality, safety, and reliability, including drift, data quality, retrieval effectiveness, hallucination/groundedness, latency, throughput, failures, cost, and guardrail effectiveness such as input/output filtering, policy enforcement, fallback handling, and human\-in\-the\-loop escalation where needed.
* Experience deploying models and LLM\-backed services to production (e.g. Docker, cloud platforms, CI/CD), and exposing them via APIs (FastAPI, REST/gRPC). GCP experience is a strong plus (e.g. Vertex AI, Cloud Run, GKE), but experience with other major clouds is also valued.


**Why you can make difference**
 You combine AI or ML engineering experience with a solid software engineering foundation. You’ve worked hands\-on with cloud platforms (preferably GCP) before and understand the complexity and pitfalls of reliable and secure automation. Familiarity with NotebookLM, Agentspace, or similar collaborative AI environments is obviously a plus. Beyond the technical, you truly care about customer and user impact, enjoy collaborating with cross\-functional teams, and have a knack for explaining technical concepts lucidly.
   

  

**3 reasons why this is (not) for you** 
**Switch to find out**
* \- You like to only be told what to do, instead of challenging yourself with thinking of improvements No, if you rather be comfortable with only taking the next task assigned to you
* \- Being adaptable is not fun for you No, if you rather stay within your comfort zone
* \- Speed comes before quality for you No, if you just want to get things done and get frustrated with thinking things through before jumping into the code.
* \+ Solid engineering experience Yes, if you have a solid software engineering background and have delivered enterprise applications of great quality.
* \+ You love solving problems Yes, if you like diving into code, logs and data to find why situations are the way they are and orient yourself towards solutions.
* \+ Constant improvement is in your DNA Yes, if you can’t relax when you see something that could be done better being overlooked and you’re not afraid to share your ideas and feedback.


**This is where you’ll work**
**Core Service**
 At the leading e\-commerce tech platform in the Netherlands and Belgium. Bol is where 13 million customers shop and 49,000 partners sell. Our Core Service domain is essential to supporting and innovating the after sales journey. Our innovation belief is that every customer should be able to solve every question through self service, but human assistance should never be more than one click away. With a team of over 60 engineers, product analysts, designers and product managers, we work every day to minimize the service effort of our customers.
   

  

 Next to the hard work there’s also room for fun. With the occasional board game lunches with colleagues or the fun events organized by our department there’s enough to do to get to know your colleagues on a more personal level as well.
   

  

**We take pride in our B Corp certification and strive for continuous improvement every day. Our annual bonus is tied to sustainability goals, and we are committed to equality and equal opportunities for all.**

title: Junior Data & Analytics Consultant
description:
Data is key to driving progress, but to fully unlock its potential, companies need a robust and innovative approach. As a
 **Junior Data \& Analytics Consultant** 
 at Rockfeather, you guide our customers in shaping their data strategy and analytics solutions to help them make smarter, faster, and better decisions.
   

  

 As a Junior Data \& Analytics Consultant, you advise clients on their data and analytics goals, translating their challenges into tangible, actionable solutions.
   

  

 Your primary focus is on helping businesses unlock the value in their data by designing intuitive, reliable, and insightful data models and reports. With know\-how in tools like
 **Power BI** 
 and/or
 **Tableau,** 
 you help businesses drive decisions through data\-driven insights.
   

  

 At Rockfeather, we are committed to staying ahead of the curve with the latest technologies. As a Junior Data \& Analytics Consultant, you will be part of a team that actively shares knowledge and expertise across Data \& Analytics, Data Engineering, and Low\-Code to deliver the most impactful solutions.
   

  

**Job Requirements**
 You bring a blend of expertise, energy, and passion for data to your role. With 0\-2 years of experience in Data \& Analytics and a foundation in business intelligence tools, you thrive on delivering impactful, customer\-centric solutions.
   

  

**On a typical day, you:**
* Advise clients on data analytics journeys, translating their needs into robust data solutions.
* Develop data models and create visually appealing, intuitive dashboards.
* Work with a wide range of tools such as Power BI and Tableau.
* Approach challenges proactively, ensuring solutions are both scalable and future\-proof.
* Always prioritize quality and customer needs in your work.
* Communicate fluently in English and preferably in Dutch, sharing knowledge and insights.
* Approach your work with a proactive, results\-driven mindset.
* Travel to the office or client locations as part of your regular routine.


**What We Offer:**
* Contract: 9\-month temporary contract with the intention of transitioning to a permanent role.
* Hours: 40 hours per week.
* Salary: €2,700 \- €3,250 (based on experience).
* Holiday Pay: 8% holiday allowance.
* End\-of\-Year Bonus: Paid out in March\-April, based on company performance and FTE.
* Pension Plan: Fully covered by Rockfeather.
* Vacation Days: 25 days.
* Mobility Budget: Choose between a lease car or a public transport travel card.
* Work Abroad: Opportunity to work remotely for up to 2 months per year.
* Learning \& Development: Freedom to choose your learning path, with internal and external coaching available.
* Flexible Working: Flexible hours and hybrid working options.
* Rockfeather Community: Frequent social (sport) activities.
* Company Certificates: Option to buy certificates in the company.


**About Rockfeather**
 Rockfeather is a young and ambitious consultancy company specialized in
 **Data Visualization, Data Engineering, and Low\-Code** 
 . Our work is guided by five core values:
   

  

* Together: We collaborate, share knowledge, and build lasting partnerships.
* We Own It: We take responsibility and deliver on our promises.
* We Fix It: We go the extra mile to find smart, reliable solutions.
* We Love It: We bring passion, energy, and optimism to everything we do.
* No Bullshit: We are open, honest, and focused on what really matters.


 We don’t claim to be the perfect place for everyone. If you’re looking for rigid structures and lots of hierarchy, we’re not your match. But if you want to shape impactful solutions, grow fast, and have fun while doing it, you’ll feel at home here.
   

  

**Application Procedure**
 Ready to make an impact as our next Junior Data \& Analytics Consultant? Send us your CV and your motivation in any form you prefer, be it a video, letter, report, or something else.
   

  

 Our process typically has three steps, including two interviews \- one with our internal recruiter and another with your potential team lead \- along with a technical assessment. You’ll also have the opportunity to meet one of your future team members for an honest look at what it's really like working with us 😉.
   

  

**Application Deadline**
**You can apply until the 1st of May 2026\.** 
 Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so don’t wait too long to send in yours.

title: Legal Counsel Finance
description:
*We are looking for a talented legal professional who enjoys working in an ambitious organisation in the scale\-up phase and who has an affinity with the purpose of Invest International. This is a great opportunity if you are eager to really be part of deal team as a legal counsel and contribute to making (inter)national impact!* 




  





**Invest International \- Dutch Solutions for Global Challenges** 




 All over the world, the UN’s Sustainable Development Goals must be achieved. These global challenges call for innovation, private entrepreneurship, and public leadership. We believe Dutch (linked) businesses can offer solutions to make the world a better place. It is our purpose to foster these solutions and make the necessary investments feasible.
 



  





 We are Invest International,
 **we invest in Dutch solutions for global challenges** 
 . Nice to meet you!
 



  





 Our hybrid business model enables us to support both governments and businesses by providing capital as well as project development capacity. We help to make the more difficult investments in innovative solutions financeable. Yet, in everything we do we live ‘impact first’.
 



  





**Organization** 




 Invest International opened its doors in October 2021, in The Hague. We are a state\-owned company set up by the Dutch government. A private company funded with public capital. We are a new organization and are continuously developing. Our people are aligned with our mission, and our 7 people drivers guide the way we work together.
 



  





**Ready for the future \- Invest\-NL and Invest International join forces** 




 With a targeted date of 1 January 2028, Invest\-NL and Invest International will integrate to form a full\-fledged National Promotional Institution. This integration increases the effectiveness of public investments and international cooperation, improves access to financing for entrepreneurs, and strengthens the economic resilience of the Netherlands. The new organization will become a strategic player that enables innovation and future\-oriented growth – with impact both domestically and internationally. For more information please visit:
 **https://investinternational.nl/news\-items/invest\-nl\-and\-invest\-international\-join\-forces/** 




  





**The position** 




 The
 **Legal Counsel Finance** 
 will provide legal advice within the organisation with a strong focus on the execution of new transactions within the Structured Finance team (Invest International’s Core Capital) as well as SME/Midcorp transactions. You will also advise on – the legal structuring and aspects of – envisaged new projects in early phase and be a sparring partner for the (Senior) Investment Officers and other commercial deal team members. In addition, the Legal Counsel Finance will be in close contact and manage external (foreign) legal counsel, if engaged. Further, you will be involved in relation to the existing investment portfolio of Structured Finance and Dutch SMEs/MidCorps.
 



  





 The Legal Counsel Finance will be part of the Legal \& Procurement Department. The Legal team currently consists of 8 lawyers covering a wide variety of legal matters (such as transaction advice on debt and equity transactions, corporate law, procurement law, administrative law, privacy, sanction and anti\-money laundering rules, state aid and corporate governance matters) The reporting line is into the Director Legal \& Procurement, who directly reports to the CFRO. The CFRO together with the CEO and CIO constitute the Management Board of Invest International.
 



  





**Responsibilities** 



* Focus on providing in\-house legal advice on and manage the execution of new transactions being e.g. export credit facilities, investment loans and project finance (including reviewing, drafting and negotiating (LMA based) loan and security documentation).
* Review and advise on other contracts that need to be reviewed, such as NDAs, Term Sheets, LoIs and MoUs.
* Coordinating the closing of transactions and legal topics with external parties and law firms.
* Pro\-actively monitor and advise on legal developments, jurisprudence and/or new legislation/ regulations relevant to Invest International’s activities.
* Maintain and further develop the know\-how and templates for the loan documentation.
* Develop and provide trainings on legal topics for the business and other teams within the organisation.
* Monitor the quality of the state aid analyses made by the front office on a transaction basis.
* When potential legal issues arise, provide legal advice within the organisation including financial restructuring and recovery processes.
* Advice on other legal matters or projects that fall within the scope of the Legal team; and
* Safeguard Invest International’s values at the core of its activities.



  





**Requirements** 



* Strong affinity with the mission and vision of Invest International;
* University law degree (LLM);
* At least 3\-5 years of solid background in banking and finance (transaction practice), as a lawyer in private practice (
 *advocaat* 
 ) or in\-house legal counsel;
* Experience with typical LMA based loan agreements and related security and guarantee documentation is a must and experience with the export and/or project finance is highly appreciated;
* Solid knowledge of private law in general, and more specifically in up\-to\-date in\-depth knowledge of contract / banking \& finance law, Dutch Civil Law and/or English law;
* Experience in drafting legal documents independently and effectively managing and monitoring legal (framework) conditions;
* Ability to understand new environments swiftly, how it actually works including the interrelationships, and adapt to ‘get things done’;
* You place value in processes and procedures yet aim at results that favour the business in the short and long term;
* Flexibility in dealing with ambiguity and resilient with adversity;
* Strong networking and relation\-building skills taking into account different needs, interests, and cultural backgrounds;
* Analytical and persuasive, creative/pragmatic, decisive, result\-driven, eye for detail;
* Excellent communication skills in at least English (written and verbal). Dutch language is appreciated.
* *It is a prerequisite that you already live in, and are allowed to work in The Netherlands* 
 .



  





*Invest International appreciates diversity. Different perspectives, backgrounds and insights increase the brainpower and perspectives for action within our organization, so that we optimally meet the needs of our clients. It doesn't matter where you were born, what you believe in, how old you are, and who you love. We are especially curious about your drive for social impact.* 




  





**What we offer** 



* An innovative, dynamic, and inspiring working environment.
* 12 months contract for 40 hours per week with potential to extend afterwards.
* Salary scale 17\. This indicates a monthly salary range between EUR 6\.415 and EUR 7\.618, excluding holiday allowance and 13th month based on a 40\-hour workweek.
* Also, we offer a contribution\-free pension plan, NS business card 1st class (which also can be used for private travel), laptop, and phone.



  





**Apply for the job** 




 Would you like to join our team as our new
 **Legal Counsel Finance** 
 ? Then we'd love to hear from you! Please share your CV and cover letter noting that the
 
 application process closes on 12 April 2026
 
 .

title: AI Engineer
description:
**About MarvelX** 




  





 MarvelX is building highly specialized AI agents for the most complex, heavily regulated industries. Think insurance claims, compliance, support. Automated by AI that actually works in the real world. The opportunity is massive, and we’re moving fast.
 



  





 We’re looking for a AI Engineer to help us push the boundaries of what’s possible with AI in production. Someone who thrives at the intersection of cutting\-edge research and shipping real products. Someone who can turn ideas into working systems, fast. You’ll work directly with the founders and tech lead to design, build, and deploy AI agents that solve mission\-critical problems for our clients.
 



  





 This isn’t a typical engineering role. It’s an early seat on the rocket ship.
 



 If that sounds like your kind of challenge, let’s talk.
 



  





**Mission** 




 As a founding AI Engineer, you’ll be at the core of MarvelX’s product and tech evolution. You’ll take ownership of designing and building agentic systems that can handle high\-stakes, high\-complexity workflows. You’ll explore the edge of LLMs, reasoning engines, and orchestration frameworks, and bring them to life in production environments where reliability, compliance, and explainability matter.
 



  





**What you'll be doing** 



* Build and deploy AI agents that handle real\-world workflows in insurance, banking and other financial services domains.
* Experiment with LLMs, retrieval\-augmented generation (RAG), tool\-use, and multi\-agent orchestration to push performance.
* Design pipelines that ensure explainability, traceability, and auditability for every decision the AI makes.
* Collaborate directly with product, design, and GTM to rapidly iterate on customer feedback.
* Optimize models and systems for production: latency, accuracy, scalability, and security.
* Stay ahead of the curve: explore new architectures, open\-source tools, and research to keep MarvelX at the frontier.



  





**What you'll need** 



* 4–6 years of hands\-on experience building AI/ML systems (startups, scale\-ups, or research labs).
* Strong background in Python, ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow), and modern AI stacks (OpenAI APIs, vector DBs, etc.).
* Experience with LLMs and agentic architectures \- you’ve built with them, broken them, and made them better
* Solid understanding of data engineering: pipelines, preprocessing, and deploying models in production.
* Proactive, scrappy, and able to ship fast without compromising on quality.
* Excited by solving hard problems where there’s no clear playbook \- and you create the playbook.



  





**Bonus:** 
 exposure to compliance, fintech, or other regulated domains where explainability is critical.

title: Data Scientist
description:
Do you want your work to directly contribute to fighting financial crime at scale? At Rabobank, you will turn complex transaction data into actionable insights that help detect money laundering and terrorist financing across more than 8 million customers.
 



 In this role, you sit at the heart of the Transaction Monitoring domain, combining data analysis, engineering, and visualization to improve how financial crime is detected. Your work enables operational teams to focus on real risks by making detection systems smarter, more efficient and more transparent.
 



  





**Your impact** 



* Transform complex transaction data into clear, actionable insights that improve financial crime detection.
* Enable analysts to focus on true positive alerts by optimizing how monitoring systems perform.
* Contribute directly to strengthening the bank’s fight against money laundering and terrorist financing.



  





**Your responsibilities** 



* Build and improve data pipelines that support tuning, lookbacks, and impact analysis of transaction monitoring models.
* Perform end to end analyses from problem intake to coding, visualization and stakeholder presentation.
* Design, develop and deliver production ready dashboards that support rule tuning, rule development and system insights.
* Combine multiple complex model components into efficient and scalable analytical workflows.
* Collaborate with platform and engineering teams to bring solutions into production environments.
* Translate data into meaningful insights that support data driven decision making across stakeholders.



  





**What you will work on** 



* Estimating the impact of changes in transaction monitoring model parameters on detection performance.
* Optimizing workflows to allow more efficient and flexible model tuning.
* Building dashboards such as rule performance dashboards, system overview dashboards and transaction level insights tools.



  





 Requirements
 



**Hard skills** 



* Master’s degree in a technical or quantitative field.
* 5 or more years of experience in data analysis using Python.
* Hands on experience with PySpark and Databricks.
* Experience building dashboards and visualizations using Power BI.



**Soft skills** 



* Experience delivering end to end solutions including version control, pipelines and testing frameworks.
* Strong ability to understand underlying business questions and communicate insights effectively.
* Structured, precise, proactive, and results driven mindset.
* Ability to guide junior and medior colleagues and share technical direction.
* Capability to see the bigger picture and anticipate future insights and improvements.



  





 Benefits
 


* Salary indication: € 5\.500,00 \- € 6\.925,00\.
* Extra: A 13th month and pension contribution.
* Duration of 1 year with possibility of extension.
* 36 to 40 hours per week.
* Fixed office days on Monday and Thursday.
