In this role, you'll architect and implement robust data platforms and solutions leveraging Databricks and Azure, enabling improved analytics, operational efficiency, and business growth. The ideal candidate will possess strong technical expertise with a strategic, hands\-on approach to designing and delivering scalable data systems.

**What You Will Do**
--------------------

* Design, build, and optimize scalable data architectures leveraging Databricks on Azure.
* Develop and maintain robust data pipelines, models, and warehousing structures using industry best practices.
* Collaborate closely with engineering, analytics, and business stakeholders to translate requirements into technical designs.
* Lead the implementation of ETL/ELT frameworks, ensuring data quality, security, and compliance.
* Guide the integration of new data sources and deployment of new capabilities.
* Define and enforce data governance, data modeling, and management standards.
* Troubleshoot data and platform issues to ensure reliable and high\-performing solutions.
* Provide technical mentorship to junior team members on best practices and emerging tools.

**Requirements**
----------------

* Proven experience as a Data Architect, Data Engineer, or similar role.
* Extensive hands\-on experience with Databricks and Azure data services (e.g., Azure Synapse, Azure Data Lake, Azure Data Factory).
* Expertise designing and implementing data pipelines, data models, and analytics architectures in cloud environments.
* Strong proficiency in SQL, Python, and/or Scala for data engineering.
* Familiarity with data governance, security, access control, and compliance in Azure.
* Solid understanding of data warehouse concepts, dimensional modeling, and distributed systems.
* Experience collaborating with cross\-functional and non\-technical teams.
* Excellent problem\-solving, communication, and documentation skills.
* Fluent English

**Working Conditions \& Benefits**
----------------------------------

* Individual Contract or B2B Contract
* PTO
* Completely remote wok

**Our values**


We are a company that seeks the best for both our employees and clients, reaching beyond expectations in turning dreams into reality. Our way of working is rooted in our core values (Integrity, Excellence, Proactivity, Innovation, and People), with an expectation that our future colleagues will make these their second nature in their everyday work and life. We don’t ask for perfection, but we do appreciate people motivated to better themselves in every conceivable aspect.

**About IT Labs**


Founded in 2005, IT Labs is an international software tech company, specializing in purpose and process\-driven teams for high\-performance, innovation, transformation, and efficiency. Our HQ is in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida, and we have teams around the world \- the UK, the Netherlands, Brazil, Argentina, Serbia, Croatia, BIH, Montenegro, North Macedonia, and the Middle East. We are constantly growing, and we would love for you to become part of our team!

title: Medior Data Scientist
description:
**Company Description**  

Vattenfall is one of the largest energy companies in Europe. We make electricity and heat and supply energy to millions of customers. We do this in Sweden, Germany, the United Kingdom, France, Denmark, Finland and the Netherlands.


With around 21,000 employees, we are enabling a future in which we are less dependent on the use of fossil fuels for our energy. We are at the forefront of the transition to a sustainable energy system. To achieve this ambitious goal, we are looking for talented individuals who, in addition to their passion for their own role, also have a strong team spirit and want to contribute to supporting a meaningful company mission.

 **Job Description** **Do you want to contribute to the energy transition and a sustainable world? And does developing data driven Business solutions give you energy? Then join our Data \& Analytics team as a Data Scientist!**

**Your position**


As a **Data Scientist** you will develop data science solutions with a strong impact on Vattenfall Business to Consumer (BtC) ambitions regarding both purpose and profitability. This is also reflected in our Data Strategy mission “A sustainable step ahead through data”!


The Data \& Analytics team is responsible for the development of data driven solutions for marketing \& sales, customer journeys, service and digitization, proposition development and sustainability advice to our customers.


Solving complex business challenges using data and machine learning will be the core of your work. From the first idea, thinking of possible solutions and methods. Through the actual model development using a diverse amount of data sources on our Vattenfall Analytics Platform. To implementing a data science solution so it can be used in either our channels or internal systems.


Throughout the data science workflow you remain in close collaboration with our internal stakeholders to make sure your solutions match their needs.


So as a data scientist you will…

* Develop data science solutions from idea to implementation
* Contribute to complex business challenges in one of the 3 business domains

**Where will you work?**


The Vattenfall BTC Data \& Analytics department develops data driven solutions for our customers and customer processes. We do this by creating and implementing machine learning models, strategic analysis, reporting and a self\-service data model.


The department is spread out in 3 business domain teams; where data experts and business experts collaborate closely to realize valuable data solutions. Each Data \& Analytics domain team consists of an Analytics Translator, Analytics engineers and Data Scientists, and is supported by Data\- \& ML Engineers from IT.


We value joint development of ways of working and best practices. This is facilitated through our Data Science Community of Practice

  

**Location**


Amsterdam

 **Qualifications** **Your profile**


Being a solution\-orientated team player with a critical, pro\-active mindset is key for the role of a Data Scientist in our team. As you collaborate closely with colleagues from other disciplines, you are able to explain in a simple way how your models work and how they contribute to solving a business problem. Also, you dare to challenge current solutions and come up with improvements. We would love to talk with you, if you recognize yourself in the following:

* You have an academic degree with a strong quantitative component (Mathematics, Physics, Econometrics, or similar);
* At least 4 years of professional experience in a data scientist role (or similar);
* You have excellent verbal and written communication skills; in English;
* Working experience in an agile environment.


 Your technical skills include:

* Writing production worthy Python code, with knowledge of different programming paradigms;
* Extensive knowledge of a variety of machine learning techniques and their application in the real world;
* A solid foundation in probability theory and statistics;
* Working with large and complex data sets, with an understanding of distributed compute and query optimization;
* Experience with DevOps practices (CICD, code reviews, git, etc.).

  

**Additional Information** **Our offer**  

The height of your salary is based on your education and relevant work experience. The salary for this position ranges from €4600 to €5900 gross per month, excluding holiday allowance, based on 38 hours per week. In addition to your salary, we offer a comprehensive secondary benefits package, the most important components of which are:

* Based on your annual salary: 8% holiday allowance, 4,5% end of year bonus and a profit sharing of up to 3\.5% based on the results of Vattenfall Netherlands (RDR).
* NS Business card for commuting or a travel allowance of €0\.23 per km up to a maximum of 50 km one way and a home office contribution of €3,05 per day
* 20 statutory vacation days, 5 additional days through My Budget My Choice, 2 extra non\-statutory days, 3\.5 vitality days, andif you work a 40\-hour week on a 38\-hour contract 13 extra ADV days.
* My Budget My Choice budget, which allows you to purchase or sell extra vacation days, buy a e\-bicycle, or simply receive it as addition pay.
* Pensionplan at ABP: 70% by employer and 30% by you
* Collective health insurance by Zilveren Kruis Achmea.
* 4 hours of leave to perform volunteer work through the Vattenfall Foundation
* Education budget, because we believe it is important that you can continue to develop yourself.


At Vattenfall, there is a close team culture while also providing ample room for individual initiative.


At our company, hybrid working is the norm, meaning you will work at the office at least 50% of the time. Most of your colleagues prefer to work the remaining 50% of their time from home, but the choice is entirely yours. At Vattenfall, we highly value a good work\-life balance and continuously seek ways in which we can contribute to this as an employer.  

  

**More Information**  

We welcome your application in English no later than April 10h. We kindly request that you submit your application exclusively via our website, as we cannot guarantee the processing of applications submitted through other channels.


Please note that we have already started conducting first\- and second‑round interviews. Therefore, we encourage you not to wait too long to apply — if we receive a sufficient number of strong applications, we may close the vacancy earlier than expected.  

  

For more information about the recruitment process you are welcome to contact our recruiter Alisa Roo, via mail: alisa.roo@vattenfall.com


At Vattenfall we are convinced that diversity contributes to build a more profitable and attractive company and we strive to be good role model regarding diversity. Vattenfall works actively for all employees to have the same opportunities and rights regardless of gender, ethnicity, age, transgender identity or expression, religion or other belief, disability or sexual orientation. en int


The security of Vattenfall and its employees is essential. For that reason, a pre\-employment screening will be part of your recruitment process. The screening is based on the role you will fulfill within Vattenfall. The screening will be performed by a third party, DISA Global Solutions AB.  

  

**We look forward to receiving your application!**

title: Senior RAM System Engineer
description:
Location: Hengelo, Netherlands**Senior RAM System Engineer**
==============================


At the headquarters in Hengelo, the System Engineering department is responsible for the design of new products and for directing further development by, among others, software and hardware engineers. Within the System Engineering department, the Specialty Engineering team (which consists of about 20 engineers in total) handles the specification, analysis, and design of system aspects. To this end, the Specialty Engineering team is composed of four areas of expertise: reliability/availability/maintainability (RAM), human factors (HF), diagnostics, data analytics \& maintenance intelligence (MINT), and electromagnetic compatibility (EMC).


For the RAM team (which will consist of 5 engineers after this vacancy is filled) within Specialty Engineering, Thales is looking for an **experienced/senior reliability engineer**.


As a reliability engineer, you contribute significantly to the operational availability of the advanced radar and combat management systems developed at Thales.

* You play a key role in the design process concerning reliability, availability, and maintainability, where high availability requirements apply. During the design phase of the systems, you model the solution approach, analyze design alternatives, and assess them for their reliability and maintainability.
* Where appropriate, you provide advice on alternative solutions that could offer a better contribution. In doing so, you can make a well\-balanced consideration between effort, risks, other interests, and the result.
* You will frequently consult with other system engineers as well as engineers from other disciplines such as design, testing, logistics, and component teams.
* You will deepen your understanding of the theoretical and practical failure behavior of the systems at the level where the system components are integrated. Reliability analyses of the system components developed by Thales Netherlands are performed at the printed circuit board level through reliability prediction.
* You will contribute to this and provide insights to the engineers involved. In addition, you will take note of practical knowledge gained regarding reliability and maintainability and incorporate this into future analyses and recommendations. As an expert, you are expected to keep up with developments in the field and to broaden and share your experience with software tools and knowledge about modeling techniques.

**About you**

* Degree in technical study, either on academic (WO) or HBO level.
* Affinity with hardware and electronics.
* Proven experience or affinity with reliability engineering, preferably using military standards (MIL)
* Proven experience with DFR in product development
* and/or on printed circuit board (PCB) level.
* Ability to calculate failure rates, mean time between failure (MTBF) and other RAM metrics using information provided by design engineers and/or external suppliers.
* Proven Experience with RAM analysis \& simulation tools such as windchill risk and reliability (WRR).
* Experience with (Design) Failure Mode Effects Analyses (FMEA and DFMEA).
* Data modelling skills, Python/Matlab, etc.
* Ability to quickly adapt to different environments, both in collaboration with others (team player and ability to work individually) and on technical level (keeping an overview as well as the ability to focus on technical level).
* Strong communication skills and outgoing pro\-active attitude to support collaboration with other departments and external customers.
* Proficiency in English (speaking, reading and writing) and preferably also good communication skills in Dutch.

**What's in it for you ?**

* A challenging job in a great team within our high\-tech, innovative work environment. Includes the freedom for initiative and good ideas.
* Room for personal and professional growth within our organization, for example in regards to technical, commercial or international skills.
* Valuable international collaboration with coworkers from 68 different countries around the globe.
* A comfortable work space in a modern environment, with a focus on flexibility and the right work life balance.
* You can usually work where and whenever you want. Within Thales, we aim for a 60/40 balance, where you work from home at least two days a week, when your role allows this.
* A good salary, fitting travel allowance, and the ability to personalize your employment conditions: for instance, you can trade PTO for internet costs, our bicycle plan, company fitness and relocation costs.
* 40 days off per year (PTO).


Interested?


Apply now! Click on the button below to upload your profile and show your interest.


To ensure that we are a good match, we may ask you to participate in an assessment.


A government clearance will be requested as part of standard Thales procedures.

*Diversity Statement*


We actively support a working pattern that suits your lifestyle and helps you reach your ambitions. That means that equal opportunities, inclusion and an informal culture are integral to our success. It also means that your well\-being and happiness matter to us! That’s why we offer you the flexibility to do what’s important to you; whether that’s part time hours, job sharing, remote working, or the ability to flex your start and finish times.

*Security Requirements*


In line with Thales' Baseline Security requirements, candidates will be asked to provide evidence of identity, eligibility to work in The Netherlands and employment and/or education history. This vacancy requires a security clearance which can require further evidence to be provided. For further details of the evidence required to apply for security clearance please refer to the General Intelligence and Security Service.

title: UI Designer - Mobile Apps
description:
We are seeking a talented UI Designer to join our mobile app team. You will craft visually  

compelling, consistent, and user\-friendly interfaces across Android and iOS products. As part  

of a global UX organization, you will collaborate closely with product managers, UX designers,  

and engineers to bring polished, high\-quality designs to life—simplifying navigation and in\-car  

experiences for millions of users.
### **What you’ll do**

* Design clear, attractive, and user\-friendly visual interfaces for mobile experiences.
* Implement and maintain a consistent visual language across products, ensuring design quality and coherence.
* Create UI components, mock\-ups, layouts, high\-fidelity screens, design documentation, specifications, and UI assets (icons, imagery, animations).
* Contribute to our design system, supporting the evolution of UI components and visual foundations.
* Work closely with UX designers to translate interaction flows into refined visual layouts.
* Communicate design rationale clearly to cross\-functional partners.
* Present work, incorporate feedback, and participate in design reviews.
* Collaborate with product and engineering teams to ensure high\-quality implementation of UI assets.

### **What you’ll need**


* 3–5 years of experience designing consumer\-facing mobile applications, demonstrated through a strong visual/UI portfolio.
* Proven experience designing for Android and iOS, using components, visual styles, and design tokens effectively.
* Strong visual design skills: typography, layout, color, spacing, hierarchy, and motion principles.
* Proficiency in UI and prototyping tools (Figma essential).
* Experience working with design systems and scalable UI libraries.
* A detail\-oriented mindset with a passion for polished, user\-centered visual experiences.
* Ability to collaborate effectively within cross\-functional product teams.
* Strong communication skills and openness to feedback.
* Passion for automotive or in\-vehicle experiences; must have a driver’s license.
* A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in UI Design, Visual Communication, Graphic Design, or related fields is a plus
**What we offer**  

A competitive compensation package, of course.  

Time and resources to grow and develop, including a personal development budget and paid leave for learning days, as well as paid access to e\-learning resources such as O’Reilly and LinkedIn Learning.  

Time to support life outside of work, with enhanced parental leave plus paid leave to care for loved ones and volunteer in local communities.  

  

Work flexibility, where TomTom’ers, in agreement with their manager and team, use both the office and home to focus, collaborate, learn and socialize. It’s all about getting the best out of both worlds – we ask TomTom’ers to come to the office two days a week, and the remaining three are free to be worked in either location.  

Improve your home office with a setup budget and get extra support with a monthly allowance.  

Enjoy options to work from your home country and abroad for a set number of days each year, to visit family and friends, or to simply explore the world we’re mapping.  

Take the holidays you want with a competitive holiday plan, plus an extra day off to celebrate your birthday.  

Join annual events like our Hackathon and DevDays to bring your ideas to life with talented teammates from around the world.  

Become a part of our inclusive global culture and have the chance to collaborate with a diverse community – we have over 80 nationalities at TomTom!  

Find out more about our global benefits and enjoy additional local benefits tailored to your location. **Meet your team**  

We’re the User Experience Design team. Fueled by user insights and data analytics, our mission is to create powerful, easy\-to\-use products that delight our customers, from millions of drivers to automotive customers who embed our technologies in their vehicles. We deliver the design specifications for all TomTom’s products. Joining us, you'll be part of a team that brings together design disciplines from user research to interaction, concept and visual design. Together, we'll continue to develop a map that people trust all around the world.  

  

  

**At TomTom...**  

You’ll help people find their way in the world. In 2004, TomTom revolutionized how the world moves with the introduction of the first portable navigation device. Now, we intend to do it again by engineering the first\-ever real\-time map, the smartest and most useful map on the planet.  

Work with a team of 3,600\+ unique, curious and passionate problem\-solvers. Together, we’ll open up a world of possibilities for car manufacturers, enterprises and developers to help people understand and get closer to the world around them. **After you apply**  

Our recruitment team will work hard to give you a meaningful experience throughout your journey with us, no matter the outcome. Your application will be screened closely and you can rest assured that all follow\-up actions will be thorough, from assessments and interviews all the way through onboarding. To find out more about our application process, check out our hiring FAQs. **TomTom is an equal opportunity employer**  

TomTom is where you can find your place in the world. Every day we welcome, nurture and celebrate differences. Why? Because your uniqueness is what makes you, *you*. No matter your culture or background, you’ll find your impact at TomTom. Research also shows that sometimes women and underrepresented communities can be hesitant to apply for positions unless they believe they meet 100% of the criteria. If you can relate to this, please know that we’d love to hear from you.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: MLOps Platform Engineer - Team Data & Analytics & AI Platform
description:
As an MLOps Engineer, you develop the AI and MLOps platform that enables our data scientists to bring models to production faster, smarter and more reliably.  

  

**Uitdagingen**  


As an MLOps Engineer, you help build and run our central Data Science \& AI platform. You work on project provisioning flows, Python packages and integrations with Databricks. You create clear, reusable project templates for data science workloads, AI models and agent‑based solutions. These templates help teams start quickly and work in a consistent way.


You play an important role in the migration from AWS‑based solutions to Databricks. You define standards for this migration, support data scientists in their transition and help test, deploy and improve their workloads. You work closely with teams to train them in MLflow, LLMOps and our broader MLOps practices. You also explore how AI coding skills and coding assistants can help our data scientists work faster, cleaner and more reliably.  



  

  

**Je verantwoordelijkheden**  

**Visible work**: You help build our Data Science \& AI platform that many of our data scientists and AI engineers use every day. You work on project provisioning, create standard project templates and support the development of agent‑based and AI use cases. This gives your work direct impact on projects in logistics, sorting, customer contact and planning.
  

**Challenge**: You work in a dynamic environment where you help shape our new Databricks platform while also supporting our existing AWS solutions. You coordinate the migration, guide teams through the transition and define standards for MLflow, LLMOps and broader MLOps practices. You also explore new Databricks features such as Databricks Apps, Agent Bricks and agent development. This gives you room to experiment, learn and contribute to the future of our AI platform.
  

**Good atmosphere**: You work with colleagues who are eager to improve, help each other and enjoy trying new things. You can bring your ideas, learn from others and grow in a supportive team.
  

**Flexibility**: You work hybrid: partly from home and partly from our modern office in the historic Stationspostgebouw next to The Hague Hollands Spoor. This gives you freedom to organise your week in a way that works for you.
  

**Responsibility**: You get real ownership. You help shape how we build and use our AI platform and how our growing AI community works with data, models and agent‑based technologies.
  

**Development opportunities**: You keep developing yourself through the PostNL Academy, by learning from colleagues and by working with modern tools such as Databricks, MLflow, LLMOps, agent‑based development and AI coding tools that support data scientists in writing better and faster code.
  

  

**Wat breng jij mee?**  


You enjoy working with others and like turning complex topics into clear and simple solutions. You take initiative, think ahead and help your colleagues move forward. You communicate easily with people who have different levels of technical knowledge. You are curious, open to feedback and eager to learn, because of the rapidly changing field of AI and MLOps.


You also recognise yourself in the following:


* You have a bachelor’s or master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, AI or a similar field.
* You have experience with MLOps principles, such as model development, testing, deployment and monitoring.
* You are skilled in Python and version control.
* Preferably, you have AWS CDK and typescript experience.
* You have experience with CI/CD tools, such as GitHub Actions or a similar setup.
* You have worked with a cloud platform, such as AWS and/or Databricks.

  

**Wat bezorgen we jou?**  


A role full of innovation and work that you see reflected throughout the Netherlands. You help shape the logistics e‑commerce platform of the future.
  

  

You can count on a strong package of employment benefits.


* Salary between €4,588\.87 and €6,498\.75 gross per month based on 37 hours (32 hours is also possible).
* Hybrid working and flexibility in organising your time. If you work from home two or more days a week, you receive a €45 net monthly allowance.
* 8% holiday allowance and 25 vacation days based on full‑time employment.
* Commuting reimbursement through NS Business Card or kilometre allowance.
* A good pension scheme through the PostNL pension fund.
* Laptop (Mac or Windows) and smartphone.
* Training and development options via the PostNL Academy.
* Discounts on travel, events and products.

title: Artificial Intelligence Engineer
description:
An Agentic AI Engineer designs, builds, and deploys autonomous AI systems that do not just generate text (like traditional chatbots) but rather reason, plan, use tools, and take actions to achieve multi\-step goals with minimal human oversight. This role focuses on behavior, tool\-use orchestration, and workflow state management.
 



 LinkedIn \+1
 



 Key Responsibilities
 


* **Design Agentic Architectures:** 
 Implement multi\-step planners, ReAct loops, state machines, and long\-term memory systems (e.g., LangGraph, AutoGen).
* **Tool\-Use Orchestration:** 
 Integrate LLMs with external tools, APIs, databases, and enterprise systems to enable autonomous actions.
* **RAG \& Context Management:** 
 Develop Retrieval\-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines and manage agent context to ground decisions in real\-time data.
* **Production Deployment:** 
 Create evaluation frameworks for reliability, accuracy, cost, and latency, including guardrails and human\-in\-the\-loop controls.
* **Multi\-Agent Systems:** 
 Build systems where specialized agents collaborate to complete complex tasks.
* Siemens \+3



 Required Skills \& Qualifications
 


* **Programming:** 
 Strong proficiency in Python (essential) and familiarity with TypeScript/Node.js.
* **AI Frameworks:** 
 Hands\-on experience with LangChain, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, or similar.
* **LLMs \& Tools:** 
 Deep understanding of OpenAI APIs, Claude, Llama, and function calling.
* **Data \& Backend:** 
 Proficiency with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate), RAG, REST APIs, and SQL.
* **DevOps:** 
 Experience with Docker, Kubernetes, and CI/CD pipelines.
* **Experience:** 
 Generally 2–5\+ years in software engineering, with 1\-2 years focused on LLM/agentic systems.
* BMW Group Careers \+7



 Core Differences from Traditional AI Roles
 


* **Behavior \> Prediction:** 
 Focuses on what the agent
 *does* 
 (action) rather than just what it predicts.
* **Autonomous End\-to\-End:** 
 Shifts from one\-shot prompting to persistent, goal\-driven agents that maintain state.
* **Tool Integration:** 
 Focuses heavily on enabling AI to interact with outside software (APIs/Databases).
* LinkedIn \+4



 Example Job Titles
 


* Senior Agentic AI Engineer
* Principal AI Engineer – Agentic Systems
* Agentic AI Developer
* Generative AI / Agentic AI Senior Engineer

title: Machine Learning Engineer
description:
You will be joining a global leader in the biotechnology and pharmaceutical industry, focused on developing innovative therapies for serious diseases. The organization operates at the forefront of advanced manufacturing, where digitalization, data, and automation play a key role in ensuring high\-quality and efficient production processes.
   

  

 Within this environment, there is a strong focus on Smart Digital Manufacturing, leveraging data and machine learning to improve equipment reliability, optimize performance, and enable predictive maintenance strategies.
   

  

**About the role** 
  

 As a Machine Learning Engineer, you will be part of the Equipment Engineering team, contributing to the development of data\-driven solutions that enhance manufacturing reliability and efficiency.
   

 You will work with complex datasets from facility and manufacturing systems (such as cooling systems, compressors, pumps, and production lines) to generate actionable insights into equipment health and performance. By developing and deploying machine learning models in production environments, you will play a key role in enabling predictive maintenance and reducing downtime.
   

 As the program evolves, you may also contribute to advanced industrial AI applications, including computer vision solutions (e.g. assisted line clearance) and Digital Twin models for simulation, monitoring, and optimization of manufacturing processes.
   

**Key responsibilities** 
* Manage the full machine learning lifecycle: data collection, preprocessing, model development, validation, deployment, and continuous improvement
* Analyze data from industrial systems such as compressors, pumps, and manufacturing equipment
* Interpret complex sensor data (e.g. vibration, frequency, temperature, force/position, and image data)
* Research and implement suitable machine learning and deep learning models
* Develop models for predictive maintenance, anomaly detection, and condition\-based monitoring
* Build scalable, production\-level Python pipelines for model training and deployment
* Design and implement dashboards (e.g. Grafana) to monitor data streams and model performance
* Perform model validation and monitoring to ensure robustness and long\-term reliability
* Detect and respond to data drift and model performance issues
* Collaborate with engineers, technical leads, vendors, and global data science teams


**Your Profile:
   

 Education \& experience**
* MSc in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Mechatronics, or a related field \+ 3\+ years of relevant experience
   

**or**
* BSc in a similar field \+ 5\+ years of relevant experience


**Technical skills**
* Strong Python skills (pandas, scikit\-learn, PyTorch and/or TensorFlow)
* Experience with Databricks
* Solid understanding of machine learning and deep learning (e.g. anomaly detection, probabilistic models)
* Experience deploying models into production environments


**Systems knowledge**
* Ability to interpret physical machine behavior using sensor data


**Software engineering \& DevOps**
* Experience writing clean, scalable, production\-level code
* Proficiency with Git
* Familiarity with Docker, CI/CD, and DevOps practices


**Other**
* Ability to independently design and deliver end\-to\-end solutions
* Fluent in English (written and spoken)


**Nice to have** 
* Experience with predictive maintenance or industrial data use cases
* Experience in regulated environments (e.g. GMP)
* Experience with Grafana dashboards
* Knowledge of computer vision (CNNs, Vision Transformers, PatchCore, PaDiM)
* Familiarity with AWS (SageMaker, S3\)

title: Senior Data Scientist (GenAI) DFC
description:
**At a glance**
 Are you ready to join one of ABN AMRO's largest and fastest\-growing data science teams dedicated to Detecting Financial Crime (DFC)? As a Data Scientist at DFC you’ll work on socially relevant projects with significant impact on the financial system's stability and security. All the while you get the chance to work on the frontier of Data Science innovation.
   

  

**Your job**
 You'll be joining a vibrant group of about 35 Data Science Experts in the Innovation team of our Detecting Financial Crime in Amsterdam. You’ll collaborate closely with DFC teams and other departments, bringing fresh perspectives and energy to our innovative projects.
   

  

 In this role you’ll be transforming traditional transaction monitoring and Know\-Your\-Customer (KYC) processes into state\-of\-the\-art machine learning and genAI technologies, such as NLP and LLM. This responsibility extends to continuously tailoring models adapting them to business needs and emerging financial crime threats.
   

  

 Here’s some insights in your new role:
   

  

* Design and develop genAI models for transaction monitoring and KYC processes.
* Collaborate with business units, Compliance, and DFC analysts to enhance financial crime detection.
* Tailor data\-driven models to evolving business needs and financial crime threats.
* Interpret model results and effectively communicate findings to stakeholders.


**Working environment**
 The Detecting Financial Crime – Data Science Innovation Team at ABN AMRO is a key player in the fight against financial crime, including activities such as money laundering, terrorist financing, and tax evasion. As one of the largest and fastest\-growing data science teams within the organization, our department is dedicated to developing innovative solutions that maintain the integrity of the financial system. Situated in Amsterdam, this vibrant group of over 35 Data Science Experts collaborates closely with various departments and DFC teams. We strive for growth and improvement, offering an exciting environment for innovation.
   

  

 Our work is highly visible and impactful, drawing attention from senior management, national regulators, and society, underscoring their crucial role as gatekeepers of financial stability. By harnessing a combination of expertise and innovative approaches, we try to ensure that we detect all perpetrators in the banking system. Through this work, we align the interests of clients, the bank, and society, making its mission both professionally rewarding and socially relevant.
   

  

**Your profile**
* You have a master’s degree or PhD in computer science/data science or related field and you have at least 5 years work experience as a Data Scientist and at least 2 years work experience with GenAI.
* You have experience with working with unstructured data and transforming that data for GenAI application design.
* You have experience with Azure AI services and trade\-offs of different GenAI models.
* You have experience with exposure to frameworks and concepts relevant to GenAI or Natural language processing (any experience in e.g. RAG, DSPy, langchain, agents, NLP classification problems, topic modeling etc.).

* Having working experience within the KYC/CDD/AML domain is beneficial.
* Located in the Netherlands.


**Characteristics:**
* You have a sense for business, and understand how Data Science and genAI specifically can be used to deliver value.

* You are pragmatic and value\-focussed.
* You are able to communicate effectively with technical and business counterparts.
* You are a teamplayer that requires minimal steering to be productive.
* You love grow individually \& as a team.
* You have a pro\-active mindset and are a self\-starter.


 Of course, this is our view on the matter! If you don’t check all the boxes, but are confident you are a great fit tell us why!
   

  

**We are offering**
 At DFC, you’ll be working in a driven and dynamic environment, where people work hard based on a shared vision and objective. The DFC department is characterised by a non\-hierarchical structure, short lines of communication, a focus on deliverables and close teamwork with other departments.
   

  

 Although we make high demands of our work, we never lose sight of the human dimension because we believe that the success of our mission depends on it. In this work context, we’ll offer you the opportunity to work on your professional and personal development offering custom growth for people of all seniority.
   

  

 Apart from that we offer:
   

  

* The gross monthly salary displayed above is based on a 36\-hour work week, including vacation pay and benefit budget.
* The Benefit Budget is 11% of your salary. The Benefit Budget allows you to acquire additional employment benefits. If you make no purchases or reservations in the Benefit Shop in a given month, you are paid one twelfth of your Benefit Budget that month.
* Five weeks of vacation per year. You have the option to purchase an additional four weeks per year.
* Personal development Budget of € 1,000 per year, which you can accumulate up to € 3,000\.
* A hybrid way of working with on average only two work days in the office.
* An annual public transport pass with free public transportation throughout the Netherlands.
* A more than excellent pension scheme.

Your responses will help us understand your experience and how you drive and lead projects to create recognizable impact for our customers and employees:

 We look forward to your application! Any question around the vacancy, please reach\-out to the assigned recruiter.

title: Information Retrieval Algorithm Engineer
description:
**Team Introduction:**
 Huawei Amsterdam Research Center is at the forefront of innovation in artificial intelligence and machine learning. We are dedicated to developing cutting\-edge technologies that revolutionize how people interact with information and enhance productivity across various sectors. Our team of experts is committed to pushing the boundaries of what's possible in AI, and we are looking for talented individuals to join us on this exciting journey.
   

  

**Position Overview:**
 We are seeking a highly skilled and motivated Research Engineer to join our team, focusing on building advanced AI systems based on Large Language Models (LLMs) enhanced with Reinforcement Learning (RL). The ideal candidate has hands\-on experience applying RL techniques to train and align large language models, with a particular emphasis on agentic applications such as deep research and automated code generation.
   

  

 In this role, you will be responsible for designing and developing cutting\-edge
 **LLM\-powered Multi\-Agent systems** 
 trained and aligned using Reinforcement Learning, targeting complex agentic scenarios including deep research and automated code generation. You will work closely with our team of researchers, data scientists, and engineers to push the frontier of what LLM\-based agents can achieve.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
* Design and develop LLM\-based multi\-agent AI systems, applying Reinforcement Learning (e.g., RLHF, RLAIF, PPO, GRPO) to train, align, and optimize agents for complex agentic tasks such as deep research and automated code generation.
* Utilize RL training frameworks (e.g., verl, OpenRLHF, TRL) and agent orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex) to build, train, test, and deploy autonomous AI agents at scale.
* Research and implement RL\-based training pipelines and reward modeling strategies to improve LLM agent performance on long\-horizon tasks including deep research, multi\-step reasoning, and code generation.
* Develop and integrate advanced knowledge management and Retrieval\-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems to provide AI agents with accurate and timely information, reducing model hallucinations.
* Stay up\-to\-date with and contribute to the latest research in Large Language Models, Reinforcement Learning for LLMs (RLHF, RLAIF, process reward models), agentic AI systems, and domain\-specific applications such as deep research automation and AI\-driven code generation.
* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to integrate the AI solutions you develop into existing products and services.
* Evaluate and benchmark the performance of AI Agent systems and perform iterative optimization.


**Qualifications:**
* Master's or Ph.D. in Computer Science with a focus on NLP, ML, or AI.
* Hands\-on experience applying Reinforcement Learning to train and align Large Language Models (e.g., RLHF, RLAIF, PPO, GRPO) and building LLM\-based agent or multi\-agent systems; this is a core requirement for the position.
* Strong programming skills in Python and familiarity with at least one major machine learning framework such as PyTorch.
* Solid understanding of deep learning architectures, including Transformers, BERT, GPT, etc.
* Proven track record of research excellence, demonstrated through publications in relevant conferences and journals.
* Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team.
* Excellent problem\-solving skills and attention to detail.


**Preferred Qualifications:**
* Proficiency with RL training frameworks such as verl, OpenRLHF, TRL, or similar; experience with reward modeling, process reward models (PRM), and RLVR is a strong plus.
* Practical experience building deep research systems or AI coding agents (e.g., search\-augmented reasoning, code execution agents, test\-driven code generation); familiarity with evaluation benchmarks such as SWE\-bench, HumanEval, GAIA, or BrowseComp.
* In\-depth experience with agent orchestration frameworks such as LangChain, LlamaIndex, combined with advanced RAG techniques for grounding agents in factual knowledge.
* Background in software development and engineering best practices.

title: Data Scientist
description:
**Overview**
**Company Description**
 VPR Energy BV is part of the Vitol Group, a global leader in the energy and commodities sector. With key operations in Houston, London, Singapore, Rotterdam, and 40 other offices worldwide, Vitol serves the world’s energy markets. Vitol invests globally in traditional and sustainable energy assets, including 18 million mm³ of storage, 850\+ kbpd of refining capacity, and more than 7,000 service stations.
   

  

**VPR Energy**
 Located in the Europoort\-Rotterdam area, VPR Energy BV operates the most dynamic refinery owned by the world’s largest independent energy and commodities trading company. Our highly efficient and flexible 140 kbpd topping refinery processes gas condensate and crude oil into naphtha, jet fuel, gasoil, and residual fuel. These valuable products serve as feedstock in the petrochemical industry or as energy to heat homes and fuel marine vessels.
   

  

 Every day, we leverage our expertise in refining crude and condensate blends, striving to be best in class in terms of energy reductions and sustainability targets. Our operations are managed from our office in Europoort Rotterdam by a small, highly engaged team with extensive knowledge and experience in processing, operations, and logistics. Our people build our reputation as a leading player in the energy markets through their creativity, commitment, professionalism, and entrepreneurial spirit.
   

  

 As VPR Energy continues to grow steadily, we are seeking the next member to join our innovative team.
   

  

**Job Description**
**Position Summary**
 The Data Scientist will act as an important support for the wider VPR performance team, using expertise to improve and enhance business activities. Core responsibilities include:
   

  

* Writing and implementing algorithms to extract, structure, and analyze data
* Applying existing software and tools, or developing new ones (including new data science methods and techniques) to transform unstructured, raw data into usable business information
* Translating insights obtained from data into concrete recommendations for business operations in written reports, dashboards, or presentations
* Managing and enhancing data streams and analyses
* Automating tasks such as data collection


**Qualifications**
**Qualifications and Experience**
* Minimum Bachelor’s degree in a STEM field (for example econometrics, engineering, or mathematics)
* 2 – 5 years of relevant working experience
* Strong database programming skills (such as Python and SQL) and excellent knowledge of Excel
* Experience with CI/CD processes (e.g., Git)
* Ability to convey complex analysis in a concise and understandable manner
* Enjoys working in a dynamic environment
* A good command of the Dutch and English language
* No 9\-to\-5 mentality


**Additional Information**
**Additional Information**
* Contribute directly to a large variety of projects
* Be part of a small and flat organization with lots of creative freedom
* Free in\-house lunch, freshly prepared and catered daily
* Potential for travel to our Geneva and London office
* Competitive compensation and benefits package

title: Personal Assistant - Global Infrastructure
description:
We are looking for a Personal Assistant who can support the management team (MT) members of the different tribes within Global Infrastructure to stay organized, prepared, and able to focus on what matters most. You will manage the agenda, support key meetings and events, and help the Management Team work smoothly together. This role suits someone who enjoys structure, responsibility, and being a trusted point of coordination.
   

  

**About Global Infrastructure And The Management Team**
 Global Infrastructure is responsible for delivering and managing a cohesive and resilient Global Infrastructure ecosystem that encompasses Hybrid Cloud (Private/Public), Data Centers, Networking, and Middleware capabilities, enabling seamless integration and optimized performance to support the organization’s strategic goals and operational needs.
   

  

 You will collaborate closely with Business Managers and the other (Executive) Personal Assistants in Global Infrastructure, to ensure good coordination, smooth planning, and high standards across the team.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Take ownership of complex agenda management. You plan ahead, understand priorities, and know the purpose and sensitivity of each appointment.
* Prepare client meetings, offsites, Town Halls, and other internal or external events. This includes logistics and following up on action points.
* Take care of administrative tasks such as maintaining accurate records, managing expenses, handling purchase orders, and processing approvals.
* Build strong relationships with colleagues and external contacts. You understand how to navigate the organization effectively.
* Arrange business travel and accommodation while balancing quality, timing, and cost.
* Handle information and communication carefully. You draft clear internal notes and client\-ready emails in both Dutch and English.


**How To Succeed**
 You are a strong match if you:
   

  

  

* Have experience supporting a senior leader in a complex or international organization, including advanced calendar and meeting management.
* Write clear and professional communication and can easily adjust your tone to the situation.
* Have strong stakeholder management skills
* Build trust quickly and feel confident giving constructive input, even without formal authority.
* Are confident using Outlook, Teams, and Excel, and can work comfortably with tools like Workday and I\-buy (E\-Profit).
* Stay calm and organized when priorities shift. You maintain high quality and pay close attention to detail.
* Handle sensitive and confidential information with professionalism and good judgment.


 Think Forward! Our goal is to help people advance in life and business. We are a well\-established brand with strong financials, global reach, and omni\-channel strategies. If you value innovation, agile principles balanced with compliance and quality, reliable service, and a practical work approach, this is the place for you.
   

  

**What department?**
 The Analytics Engineering Center of Excellence team, with over 50 members and expanding, aims to make ING data\-driven by leveraging Big Data and Data Science to deliver impactful solutions. We foster a collaborative and creative work environment focused on supporting our people and projects.
   

  

**Role Description**
 Your passion is to work with the latest and greatest technologies in the field of Machine Learning Engineering.
   

  

**Do You Recognize Yourself In This Profile**
* Experience working on machine learning projects
* Practical experience developing with Python
* Experience designing distributed software with cloud\-based managed services (preferably GCP)
* Familiarity with machine learning technologies and frameworks, such as sklearn, MLFlow, TensorFlow (including those available on GCP)
* Understanding of MLOps architecture and methodologies
* Knowledge of software engineering practices including version control, testing, documentation, and code review
* Experience building data pipelines, including ETL processes and data processing frameworks like pySpark
* Proficiency in data manipulation and transformation using tools such as SQL
* Background in monitoring and observability with tools like the ELK stack
* Experience implementing deployment and provisioning automation tools including Docker, Kubernetes, Openshift, and CI/CD


**Nice to have (but Not Required):** 
 Even if you don't meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply. Experience in these areas is a bonus:
   

  

  

* Proficiency in Bash scripting and Linux systems administration
* Experience in developing and maintaining high load APIs
* Experience with distributed systems and clusters for batch and streaming data (S3/Spark/Kafka/Flink)
* Experience working within an agile/scrum framework
* Willingness or ability to use multiple programming languages, such as statically typed languages
* Contribution to open\-source projects is considered advantageous
* Involvement in the implementation and deployment of cloud solutions incorporating Generative AI and LLM technologies


**Rewards And Benefits**
 We want to make sure that it’s possible for you to strike the right balance between your career and your private life. https://careers.ing.com/en/benefits\-NL
   

  

**The Benefits Of Working With Us At ING Include**
* 25\-28 vacation days depending on contract
* Pension scheme
* 13th month salary
* 8% Holiday payment
* Hybrid working
* Personal growth and challenging work with endless possibilities
* An informal working environment with innovative colleagues


**About Us**
 Curious about how ING empowers people and businesses to move forward? https://careers.ing.com/en/working\-in\-the\-netherlands
   

  

**Questions?**
 Contact the recruiter attached to the advertisement. Want to apply directly? Please upload your CV and motivation letter by clicking the ‘Apply’ button.

title: Data Architect
description:
Sia is a next\-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today, our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity, and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change, helping clients initiate, navigate, and benefit from transformation. Optimism is our force multiplier, mitigating downside and maximizing opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results; our optimism transforms outcomes.
   

  

  

  




**Job Description**

 To support our growth, we are recruiting Senior Consultants specializing in Data Architect solutions. You will lead client engagements, co\-design data engineering solutions through close collaboration with clients, manage stakeholders, and ensure impactful project delivery from strategy through execution.
   

  





**Responsibilities:**



* **Data Architecture \& Pipeline Design:** 
 Building robust, scalable data pipelines and architectures to enable efficient ingestion, processing, and delivery of large volumes of structured and unstructured data.


* **Cloud \& Distributed Systems:** 
 Designing and implementing data solutions leveraging modern cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP) and distributed computing frameworks (e.g., Spark, Kafka).


* **Database Design \& Data Modeling:** 
 Developing logical and physical data models and optimizing database solutions for various data types and workloads, including relational, NoSQL, time\-series, and graph databases.


* **Data Integration \& Loading Patterns:** 
 Implementing best practices for batch, streaming, and real\-time data integration, adapting to structured, semi\-structured, and unstructured data sources.


* **CI/CD \& DataOps Practices:** 
 Enabling continuous integration and deployment of data services and infrastructure, using modern DevOps tooling to ensure reliability, versioning, and automation in data workflows.



**Within Sia itself you will:**



* Develop and enhance our Data Engineering offerings through internal training sessions, working groups, and internal/external publications.


* Represent Sia at professional events, contributing actively to thought leadership initiatives (articles, white papers, webinars).


* Drive business development by crafting compelling proposals, identifying new collaboration opportunities, and fostering long\-term client relationships.


* Manage client engagements, serving as a trusted advisor and subject matter expert to middle and senior\-level management.


* Supervise and mentor junior to medior consultants, guiding their professional growth, reviewing deliverables, and facilitating the development of technical and interpersonal skills.



**Your profile**



* Analytical thinker with a structured and proactive approach.


* Experienced communicator capable of clearly articulating complex technical ideas to varied audiences.


* Effective stakeholder manager, comfortable guiding teams through project delivery.
* Demonstrated ability to mentor colleagues and facilitate professional development.


**Qualifications**
* Completed WO Master's degree in Data Science, Econometrics, Applied Mathematics, Computer Science, (Energy) Engineering, or a related technical field involving substantial programming.
* The specific stack that you will work with will depend on the client but, as an indication, we commonly work with Databricks, Snowflake and Azure.
* 3\-6 years of relevant professional experience (or equivalent academic research).
* Strong analytical mindset with systematic problem\-solving abilities.
* A fast learner who demonstrates strong communication skills, adaptability, and entrepreneurial drive.
* You have a strong interest in data and AI\-related topics, the ability to quickly build expertise in these areas, and you’re excited about helping our clients seize the opportunities they offer.
* Fluency in Dutch, with excellent communication and presentation abilities. German or French fluency is a plus.
* Proven interest in Data Engineering.



**What do we offer?**



* Competitive salary package, including annual salary increase, bonuses, and NS Business Card.
* Tailored training and development opportunities to position you as an expert in your field.
* Flexible working arrangements (office, home, client sites) and international project exposure.
* Collaborative global environment fostering professional growth and knowledge sharing.


**Additional Information**

 Unfortunately, we are unable to provide a work permit for this position.
   

  





 Sia is an equal opportunity employer. Employment decisions—including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or disciplinary actions—are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, and business needs.
   

  





  

  





 Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

title: Head of Influencer EMEA
description:
*DEPT® is a Growth Invention company built to help the world’s most ambitious brands grow faster. Operating at the intersection of technology and marketing, our 4,000\+ specialists deliver growth invention services across Brand \& Media, Experience, Commerce, CRM, and Technology \& Data. We’re 50\|50 tech and marketing, partner\-led, and first to move. Clients include Google, Lufthansa, Meta, eBay, and OpenAI. We have been certified B Corp and Climate Neutral since 2021\.*
**Job Purpose**
 Are you a visionary Influencer leader ready to move beyond tactical execution and shape the future of the creator economy? We are seeking a dynamic
 **Head of Influencer** 
 to transform our EMEA discipline into a powerhouse of
 **operational excellence, strategic innovation, and consistent client impact.**
 You will serve as a thought leader, shaping a forward\-thinking vision for the Influencer craft across EMEA. You won't just follow social trends; you will proactively integrate
 **AI\-driven talent discovery, automated reporting, workflows and Influencer\-first creative frameworks** 
 into our daily operations. Your goal is to redefine what 'performance' looks like when creators are embedded at the heart of the media mix.
   

  

 You will lead a growing team across EMEA, removing blockers and ensuring they have access to the right tools and knowledge. You will empower every lead and consultant to deliver creatively brave, culturally relevant, and consistently excellent work that drives long\-term client success.
   

  

**WHAT YOU’LL DO:**
* Strategic \& Operational Leadership: Take the strategic lead on the performance, profitability, and long\-term growth of the Influencer discipline across EMEA. Establish the right organizational setup to ensure quality and standardization across all markets.
* Innovation \& AI Integration: Accelerate the transition from manual outreach to AI\-augmented influencer management. Proactively integrate AI tools for talent vetting, trend forecasting, and automated campaign analysis to redefine efficiency.
* Craft Excellence: Define and evolve the DEPT® influencer proposition, setting the standard for creator strategy, platform usage, and cultural relevance. Ensure influencer is embedded "upstream" in client roadmaps rather than as a tactical bolt\-on.
* Client Impact \& Growth: Oversee our Top 20 Media and Influencer accounts, driving innovation and excellence in delivery to ensure high levels of satisfaction, account retention, and organic growth.
* People Development: Coach and mentor Influencer Leads across EMEA. Identify skills gaps and foster a culture of People Happiness, giving team members the confidence to deliver high\-impact, business\-driven projects.
* Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Enable x\-craft collaboration, bringing Influencer activities closer to adjacent crafts such as Paid Social, Content, Data, and AI to ensure an integrated Growth Invention approach for our clients.
* Tech \& Tooling: Drive the adoption of the right creator tech and internal tooling, ensuring a data\-led approach to talent selection and ROI measurement across all accounts.
* Knowledge Sharing: Champion the influencer craft internally and externally. Encourage knowledge sharing between EMEA markets and ensure we are first to move in adopting new platform features and creator formats.


**What You Bring**
* Experience: Have at least 10 years of experience in Influencer Marketing and Creator Strategy, with a proven track record of leading large agency teams and managing global client portfolios.
* Strategic Visionary: You have a deep understanding of the creator economy and how to translate brand objectives into creator\-led platforms and ideas that earn attention.
* Operational Mindset: Highly autonomous and proactive, with the ability to own complex regional projects and drive operational improvements that improve margins and delivery.
* AI \& Tech Curiosity: You don't just follow trends; you understand how to apply AI and automation to enhance the influencer workflow and provide deeper insights to clients.
* Leadership: Genuinely gain energy from guiding others and leading leaders. You are a natural mentor who prioritizes team engagement and professional growth.
* Communication: A confident, dynamic communicator able to work across functions and regions to bring a unified influencer vision to life.
* Commercial: has built and grown profitable teams in other businesses, including heavy involvement in pitches.
* Integrated Knowledge: Have broader digital marketing knowledge (Paid Social, Organic social, content and Data) to ensure influencer strategy is perfectly aligned with the wider media mix.


**WE OFFER**
*Please note that benefits may vary by location. Feel free to reach out to your recruiter for more details.* 
  

  

* 
+ Hybrid working policy
+ Stay happy and healthy with a contribution to your health through the OpenUp platform, and a multisport programme;
+ A reputation for doing good. DEPT® has been a Certified B Corp® since 2021\.
+ Awesome clients. Whether big, small, local or global — at DEPT® you’ll get the opportunity to work with all of them. And we celebrate all of our successes together!
+ The opportunity for possibility. We want to enable you to do what you do best and help you develop your skills further with training, development and certifications.
+ Global annual DEPT® Cares Month in which employees come together and donate their skills to support local charities.


**WHY DEPT®?**
 We are a Growth Invention company built to help the world’s most ambitious brands grow faster. Operating at the intersection of technology and marketing, we create what is next by pioneering ideas, acting fast, and moving further because standing still just is not in our DNA.
   

  

 We are drawn to people who stay curious, move with intent, and never stop inventing. Our culture runs on three values: better together, relentlessly curious, and get sh\*t done. It is how we work, how we grow, and how we make things that matter.
   

  

 At DEPT®, you will find the freedom to explore, the space to collaborate, and the trust to make a real impact for our clients, for each other, and for the world we are helping to build.
   

  

**DIVERSITY, EQUITY \& INCLUSION**
 At DEPT®, we take pride in creating an inclusive workplace where everyone has an equal opportunity to thrive. We actively seek to recruit, develop, nurture, and retain talented individuals from diverse backgrounds, with varying skills and perspectives.
   

  

 Not sure you meet all qualifications? Apply, and let us decide! Research shows that women and members of underrepresented groups tend not to apply for jobs when they think they may not meet every requirement, when in fact they do. We believe in giving everyone a fair chance to shine.
   

  

 We also encourage you to reach out to us and discuss any reasonable adjustments we can make to support you throughout the recruitment process and your time with us.
   

  

 Want to know more about our dedication to diversity, equity, and inclusion? Check out our efforts here.

title: AI Engineer
description:
Within the Data Office, we have a team responsible for building impactful AI solutions. With these AI solutions, we help Non\-Life (Schade \& Inkomen) to digitize important processes. An example of this is the digitization of our Claims processes. Your main goal is to create AI solutions for operational teams such as Underwriting and Claims together with your colleagues.
   

  

**What are you going to do**
 You understand how to help your colleagues from the business translate their wishes into AI solutions, and you take pride in transforming these into robust, production\-grade AI solutions. With your solution\-oriented and proactive mindset, you design the best AI solution for business problems together with your team, and you realize this using our AI toolkit. We work with colleagues from the business on projects that often span multiple months.
   

  

 Specifically, you bring AI solutions into production so that your solutions truly create value for our company tomorrow. Researching and applying new technology plays an important role. You use state\-of\-the\-art techniques such as AI as a Service and LLMs, like Cognitive Services and OpenAI. Additionally, you actively contribute to innovation within NN. During our monthly innovation day, you and your colleagues explore which new techniques are applicable to our company.
   

  

 If you enjoy diving into the latest AI trends to find solutions for business problems, then this job is for you.
   

  

 Key responsibilities include:
   

  

* Develop scalable AI applications and components using LLMs and agentic frameworks.
* Design AI components with solid engineering rigor, clean code, proven design patterns, while applying NN’s standards into the codebase.
* Bring AI models to production as APIs and integrate them into the NN landscape.
* Support the team by promoting engineering best practices, conducting code reviews, and maintaining technical documentation.
* Continuously contribute to innovation initiatives and explore new technologies for assetization and reuse.
* Translate business requirements into reusable AI solutions and present them to stakeholders.


**What we offer you**
 NN invests in an inclusive, inspiring work environment and in skills and competences for the future. We match this with employee benefits that are in line with what is needed today and in the future. This way, we offer our employees the opportunity to get the best out of themselves. We offer you:
   

  

* Salary between €4,414 and €7,438 depending on your knowledge and experience
* 13th month and holiday allowance are paid with your monthly salary
* 27 vacation days for a 5\-day working week and one Diversity Day
* A modern pension administered by BeFrank
* Plenty of training and learning opportunities
* NS Business Card 2nd class, which gives you unlimited travel, also privately. Do you prefer to travel with your own transport? Then you can declare the kilometres travelled
* Allowances for setting up your home office and for internet use


 We work hybrid at NN. Together with your team, you decide where, how and when you work. The official location of this job is The Hague.
   

  

**Who you are**
 You are a versatile engineer with a strong software engineering background, who is? results\-oriented and enjoys turning business needs into robust software. You are especially motivated to drive the adoption of our AI solutions. Your open attitude and clear communication helps you collaborate effectively with stakeholders and teammates. Additionally, you have:
   

  

* Proficiency in Python
* Strong grasp of software design and architecture (SOLID, Clean code, design patterns), testing, observability and reliability
* A plus if you have experience in designing AI solutions or applications using Gen AI (e.g. LLM Workflows, agentic frameworks) based on business requirements is a plus
* A plus if you have experience with RAG systems, LLMOps (e.g. prompt management, evaluations)
* A plus if you have experience building reusable Python libraries/SDKs for other teams: clear interfaces, semantic versioning, and packaging.


**Who you will work with**
 The team you work with on a daily basis consists of a group of AI Engineers/Data Scientists, supported by a team of ML engineers and software engineers who help the team put the work into production, and a program manager who frames our activities. Of course, you work with many colleagues from the business. Together with these colleagues, we work daily with great enthusiasm and dedication to optimize and innovate customer service, processes, and quality, in close collaboration with the operational teams.
   

  

 Additionally, you are a member of the guild. Here, you exchange knowledge and experiences with peers. We believe that every AI Engineer can learn something from peers and vice versa can teach something to others in the community. This way, you accelerate your career and deliver maximum value with our AI solutions.

 If you have any questions about the job, you can reach out via email to Joost Stroes (Recruiter) \- Joost.Stroes1@nn\-group.com.

* 27 vakantiedagen en 1 Diversiteitsdag
* 13e maand en vakantiegeld
* Hybride werken

title: AI Builder
job_function: General Business, Management, and Business Development
description:
**The short version**
 Most AI projects fail not because the technology doesn’t work, but because they solve the wrong problem, which usually means no one took the time to understand what is actually slowing a team down or what would genuinely improve how they work.
   

  

 This role is about fixing that: identifying the real bottleneck in a client’s content workflow, designing something people will actually use, and building it fast enough to have an impact.
   

  

 You’ll work with enterprise marketing teams on how they brief, produce, localise, and publish content, and your job is to spot where time is lost, where quality drops, and where AI or automation can remove friction.
   

  

 Your value isn’t the tools you know, but how you decide what’s worth fixing, what isn’t, and what the simplest solution is that already improves the situation.
   

  

 Then you build it yourself — usually as an automated process, an AI agent, or a small internal tool.
   

  

 You’ll work directly with our AI Lead and CPO, who are currently building faster than they can scale, which means there’s more work than they can take on.
   

  

 If you prefer building real AI solutions over talking about them, this is the job.
   

  

**Welcome to Contentoo**
 Contentoo is a content company based in Amsterdam, working with 300\+ enterprise clients such as Riverty, Action, and Team.blue, and supporting marketing teams in producing content across multiple markets and languages.
   

  

 We combine AI agents with expert freelancers to manage the full workflow, from brief to delivery, while keeping quality consistent.
   

  

 We already use AI in our own workflows and build AI solutions for clients, and we’re expanding that across more of the business. In practice, that means:
   

  

* We build AI agents that help our own team work faster
* We build AI solutions for clients that reduce manual work
* What works for one client becomes something we can reuse for others


 Our view is simple: generic AI produces generic content, and the advantage lies in knowing what “good” looks like for each client and in building systems that deliver it consistently.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
 You own the full cycle, from first conversation to a working solution that is actually used, without handoffs between thinking and building.
   

  

 You join client calls, identify the real bottleneck (not just what’s being said), decide what’s worth solving, and build the solution yourself as an automation, agent, or small tool.
   

  

 Once something is live, you make sure people actually use it, and you look for patterns that can be reused across clients so successful solutions don’t stay isolated.
   

  

 You also improve internal AI tools and work with Product and Engineering to turn proven solutions into platform features.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 We’re looking for two things in one person.
   

  

 First, judgment: you quickly understand what’s really going on in a client situation, form clear opinions about what should be built, and explain those decisions to both business and technical stakeholders.
   

  

 Second, the ability to build: you don’t wait for specs, but use tools like GPT or Claude APIs, automation platforms such as n8n, and rapid prototyping to create working solutions yourself.
   

  

 Most people are strong in one of these areas, but this role requires both.
   

  

 Your background could be in strategy, product, digital transformation, or technical implementation, or you might have become the AI person in your last company and want to focus on it full\-time.
   

  

**Why Contentoo, why now**
 You’ll be building real things that are used by real teams, both internally and by clients, rather than working on abstract plans or long roadmaps.
   

  

 There is no established playbook for AI\-driven content operations yet, which means the way you approach problems and the solutions you build will directly shape how this part of the business develops.
   

  

 You’re not starting from scratch: we already have 300\+ clients, hundreds of thousands of completed projects, and real demand for better ways of working with AI.
   

  

**Interested?**
 Apply via the link. No cover letter needed — just show us something you built that you’re proud of.
