title: Data Integration Engineer
description:
Do you want to fight for equality? Tackling inequality at its roots, together with colleagues, our partner organizations and activists in more than 90 countries? To change the systems behind it, once and for all!  



Oxfam Novib stands behind the people who stand up for equality. Together, we fight for a world where everyone has equal opportunities, regardless of religion, ethnicity, sex, disability, sexual orientation and gender identity. A world where everyone feels safe; where income gaps are shrinking instead of growing, where climate change is tackled firmly.  


Equality is the common thread in our work, both internally and externally. We recognize that our team of colleagues can become even more diverse, and that only with a diverse team can we gain a broad perspective on the world. Therefore, we want to encourage everyone who is interested to apply. Our aim is to create an environment where colleagues feel welcome and diversity is embraced and celebrated.

Our aim is to build a team that offers a rich variety of perspectives, enabling us to achieve an inclusive and inspiring work environment.  


**Apply and become part of Oxfam Novib!** **Build the digital Backbone for Impact**


Are you passionate about building reliable data flows and connecting systems so organizations can make better decisions? Do you enjoy designing scalable integrations and turning complex data landscapes into structured, reliable platforms? If so, we invite you to join our ICT Unit at Oxfam Novib.

  

**Your role**



As a **Data Integration Engineer**, you will play a key role in strengthening Oxfam Novib’s digital backbone. You will design and maintain the data flows and integrations that connect our core business systems such as ERP, program management platform, HR system, data lake, data warehouse and reporting tools.

Your work will ensure that data flows securely, reliably, and efficiently across the organizations supporting financial accountability, operational efficiency, and data\-driven decision\-making.

This role is central to Oxfam Novib’s **digital transformation, ERP implementation, and data platform development**, helping to replace fragmented integrations and manual data processes with scalable, governed solutions.  

  

**What you will do?**
* **Build Integration Pipelines**: Design, develop, and maintain ETL/ELT pipelines and integrations using Azure Fabric or similar technologies.
* **Connect Core Systems**: Implement and maintain integrations between ERP, finance, HR, program system, and reporting platform.
* **Strengthen Data Architecture**: Support the development of a structured data platform and integration layer that enables reliable data exchange and reporting.
* **Improve Data Quality**: Implement validation, monitoring, and transformation processes to ensure reliable and consistent data across systems.
* **Support ERP Implementation**: Contribute to data migration, integration design, and validation processes as part of the ERP transformation program.
* **Enable Data\-Driven Decisions**: Provide structured and scalable data pipelines that support dashboards, reporting, and analytics.
* **Replace Fragmented Integrations**: Consolidate ad\-hoc and point\-to\-point integrations into governed and standardized integration patterns.
* **Collaborate Across Teams**: Work closely with Solution Architects, Business Analysts, and reporting teams to ensure data solutions meet organizational needs.
* **Ensure Security and Compliance**: Apply secure data transfer, encryption, and access control standards in collaboration with Information Security teams.
  


**Your team**  

You’ll be joining the newly formed data team within the **ICT Unit**. As part of the **Finance and Operations Department**, we ensure smooth operations that help Oxfam Novib achieve its impact goals and meet regulatory requirements. You’ll report to the **Manager of ICT** and work closely with IT colleagues, internal staff, and external stakeholders across various projects. Become part of a team that makes a difference and contribute to innovative solutions that drive Oxfam Novib’s mission forward!  


**Who are we looking for?**  

You are a technically skilled and collaborative professional who enjoys building reliable data integrations and enabling data\-driven decision\-making. You are comfortable working in a complex IT landscape and translating organizational data needs into scalable technical solutions.


**We are looking for someone who has:**


* A **Bachelor’s or Master’s degree** in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field
* **3– 4 years of experience** in data engineering, system integration, or ETL development
* Experience with **Azure Fabric, Data Factory or similar ETL/integration tools**
* Strong **SQL skills and understanding of data modelling and data pipelines**
* Experience working with **APIs, structured data formats (JSON/XML), and cloud data platforms**
* Knowledge of **data lake, data lake house, data warehouse, and modern data platforms**
* A structured and analytical mindset with strong **problem\-solving skills**
* The ability to collaborate with both **technical teams and business stakeholders**
* Professional proficiency in **English** (Dutch is an advantage)

title: Data & AI Architect
description:
**At a glance**
 Do you have a strong passion for data and do you agree that data is at the core of creating value for customers? Are you energized by tackling complex data\-related challenges? Do you enjoy collaborating with a team and engaging with stakeholders across the bank? If your answer is yes, we have an exciting opportunity for you in our Domain Architecture organization.
   

  

**Your job**
 You will work in the Domain Architecture Team of Daily Banking Grid in Transaction Banking value cluster. The architecture team is pivotal in defining and executing architectural frameworks, and cutting\-edge technologies to ensure our IT assets are strategically aligned with business objectives and maximize organizational value.
   

  

 As a Data Architect, you will be responsible for:
   

  

* Develop and maintain the data architecture for Transaction Banking value cluster, ensuring alignment with business goals and architecture vision of ABN AMRO.
* Advocate for data lake house architecture and promote its values.
* Author and maintain architectural documents like HLD, LLD and DFD.
* Collaborate with the central data office and business stakeholders to understand data requirements and translate them into technical solutions.
* Collaborate with IT, business units, and external partners to ensure data architecture aligns with business needs.
* Define and establish data management KPIs, OKRs and procedures to ensure data quality, consistency, and security.
* Design and develop conceptual, logical, and physical data models supporting integration, warehousing, and analytics initiatives.
* Simplify and socialize data governance policies to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and data privacy standards.
* Stay informed about emerging trends and technologies in data architecture and management.
* Monitor and optimize data architecture performance, scalability, and efficiency.
* Work with cross\-functional teams to establish roles and responsibilities across central and domain teams.


**Working environment**
 As a Data Architect, you will play a crucial role in shaping the future landscape of data domain within the Transaction Banking cluster. Your expertise in Azure architecture or similar hyper\-scaler cloud platforms will be essential as you design and implement robust, scalable data solutions. You'll leverage Databricks or equivalent data engineering platforms to streamline data processing and analytics, ensuring our systems are both efficient and future ready.
   

  

 Your enthusiasm for emerging technologies like Microsoft Fabric, serverless architectures etc. will help drive innovation within our data integration processes, fostering agile and interconnected data environments. As a Generative AI practitioner, you will play a transformative role in advancing our data initiatives, exploring new methodologies for data analysis, reporting and insights generation.
   

  

 Key Deliverables:
   

  

* Strategic Data Architecture Design
* Technology Leadership
* Collaboration and Influence
* Regulatory Compliance and Governance
* Innovation and Thought Leadership
* Scalable Solutions Development


**Your profile**
 Must haves:
   

  

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field.

* Expertise in Data Engineering, data modeling, and software design.
* Familiarity with industry leading frameworks like DAMA\-DMBOK, DCAM etc.

* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to work in a fast\-paced environment.
* Generative AI practitioner with knowledge of AI registry, EU Act, cognitive architecture, RAG agents, and XOps (AIOps).
* Knowledge of banking and financial services industry\-specific data regulations and compliance requirements.
* Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
* Collaborative, approachable, and open to learning and educating.
* Accountable and ambitious, with a drive for quality and a pace for execution delivery.
* Ability to zoom out and zoom in based on problem statements and audience mix.
* You are fluent in English and located in the Netherlands.


 Good to haves:
   

  

* Togaf and Archimate certified


**We are offering**
* The gross monthly salary displayed above is based on a 36\-hour work week, including vacation pay and benefit budget.
* The Benefit Budget is 11% of your salary. The Benefit Budget allows you to acquire additional employment benefits. If you make no purchases or reservations in the Benefit Shop in a given month, you are paid one twelfth of your Benefit Budget that month.
* Five weeks of vacation per year. You have the option to purchase an additional four weeks per year.
* Personal development Budget of € 1,000 per year, which you can accumulate up to € 3,000\.
* Possibility to work from home (in consultation with your team and depending on your position).
* An annual public transport pass with free public transportation throughout the Netherlands.
* An excellent pension scheme.
* Banking for better days. These are days you can use for your personal development or on which you can contribute to society.


**Interested?**
 Are you interested? Then apply for this vacancy. For more information, contact Gagan Saxena via gagan.saxena@nl.abnamro.com. We look forward to meeting you!

title: Applied AI, Technical Lead, Forward Deployed AI Engineer - EMEA
description:
**About Mistral**
 At Mistral AI, we believe in the power of AI to simplify tasks, save time, and enhance learning and creativity. Our technology is designed to integrate seamlessly into daily working life.
   

  

 We democratize AI through high\-performance, optimized, open\-source and cutting\-edge models, products, and solutions. Our comprehensive AI platform is designed to meet enterprise needs, whether on\-premises or in cloud environments. Our offerings include le Chat, the AI assistant for life and work.
   

  

 We are a dynamic, collaborative team passionate about AI and its potential to transform society. Our diverse workforce thrives in competitive environments and is committed to driving innovation. Our teams are distributed between France, USA, UK, Germany, and Singapore. We are creative, low\-ego, and team\-spirited.
   

  

 Join us to be part of a pioneering company shaping the future of AI. Together, we can make a meaningful impact. See more about our culture on mistral.ai/careers.
   

  

**About The Job:**
 Mistral AI is seeking a Technical Lead, Applied AI to drive the technical strategy, execution, and delivery of complex AI solutions for our enterprise customers. In this role, you will lead a project teams of Applied AI Engineers, ensuring the successful deployment of Mistral AI products and the development of high\-impact, scalable AI use cases.
   

  

 You will act as the primary technical point of contact for our most strategic customers, guiding them through the entire lifecycle—from pre\-sales to post\-implementation—while collaborating closely with research, product, and engineering teams to shape the future of our offerings.
   

  

 As a Technical Lead, you will bridge the gap between cutting\-edge AI research and real\-world enterprise applications, ensuring our solutions are robust, scalable, and aligned with both customer needs and Mistral’s technological vision.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
* Deliver as an IC the critical lines of codes of our complex projects, you’ll be hands\-on and de\-risk the critical parts of our complex projects. You’ll stay deeply involved in coding, reviewing, and optimizing AI solutions
* Lead technical teams of Applied AI Engineers, providing mentorship, technical guidance, and best practices for deploying state\-of\-the\-art GenAI applications across industries
* Lead technical discussions during pre\-sales, translating customer requirements into actionable solutions and communicating Mistral’s technological advantages to diverse stakeholders
* Design and oversee the implementation of complex AI systems, including fine\-tuning, RAG, agentic workflows, and custom LLM applications, ensuring alignment with Mistral’s product roadmap and open\-source initiatives
* Drive innovation by identifying emerging trends in AI, evaluating new tools and methodologies, and championing best practices for fine\-tuning, inference, and deployment
* Work closely with product managers, researchers, and engineers to ensure seamless integration of customer feedback into Mistral’s product development cycle


**How We Work In Applied AI**
* We care about people and outputs
* What matters is what you ship, not the time you spend on it
* Bureaucracy is where urgency goes to vanish. You talk to whoever you need to talk to. The best idea wins, whether it comes from a principal engineer or someone in their first week
* Always ask why. The best solutions come from deep understanding, not from copying what worked before
* We say what we mean. Feedback is direct, timely, and given because we care
* No politics. Low ego, high standards
* We embrace an unstructured environment and find joy in it


**About You**
* You are fluent in English
* You hold a PhD or Master’s degree in AI, Machine Learning, Computer Science, or a related field
* You have 7/8\+ years of experience in AI/ML, with at least 2\+ years in a technical leadership role (e.g., Tech Lead, Engineering Manager, or Solutions Architect) focused on AI products or enterprise solutions
* You have a proven track record of leading teams to deliver complex AI projects, from prototyping to production, in industries such as tech, finance, healthcare, or industrial automation
* You possess deep expertise in fine\-tuning LLMs, advanced RAG, agentic systems, and deploying NLP applications at scale
* You are proficient in Python, PyTorch, and modern AI frameworks (e.g., LangChain, Hugging Face). Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and MLOps tools is a plus
* You have strong software engineering skills, including API design, backend/full\-stack development, and system architecture
* You excel in technical communication, with the ability to articulate complex concepts to both technical and non\-technical audiences, including executives and engineers
* You thrive in fast\-paced, collaborative environments and are passionate about mentoring and growing technical talent


**Ideally, you have:**
* Contributed to open\-source projects, particularly in the LLM or AI space
* Experience in customer\-facing roles (e.g., Solutions Architect, Customer Engineer, or Technical Product Manager) with a focus on enterprise AI adoption
* A track record of driving technical strategy and influencing product direction based on customer needs and market opportunities


**Why join us?** 
 You’ll have the opportunity to shape the future of AI adoption in enterprises, work with a world\-class team, and contribute to open\-source projects that impact millions. If you’re excited about leading technical innovation and solving real\-world challenges with AI, we’d love to hear from you!
   

  

**Why join us?**
 You’ll have the opportunity to shape the future of AI adoption in enterprises, work with a world\-class team, and contribute to open\-source projects that impact millions. If you’re excited about leading technical innovation and solving real\-world challenges with AI, we’d love to hear from you!

 to join our Commercial team in Amsterdam.
 




  






 This role is ideal for a recent graduate or early\-career professional with strong SQL skills and an interest in building data infrastructure and analytics tools used in global shipping operations.
 




  






 You will work closely with our Commercial and Data teams to develop the data systems, dashboards, and digital platforms that support commercial decision\-making across our tanker fleet and other vessel segments.
 




  






 The position also involves working with modern AI tools to improve data workflows, automate analytics processes, and enhance internal digital platforms.

* or another quantitative field



  





**Technical Skills:** 




  





**Strong foundation in:**

title: Data Scientist
description:
At de Bijenkorf, data plays a central role in how we operate and make decisions, and we continue to expand our capabilities every day. With millions of customers visiting our stores and e\-commerce platform, we manage large and rapidly growing datasets that we use to train machine learning models and generate insights. These insights help us make smarter decisions and create impact across all areas of our business. From customer experience to logistics, assortment, and marketing. Want to help shape what’s next?
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Data Scientist, you translate the richness of our customer data into tangible impact across our website, app, online marketing channels, stores, customer service, supply chain, and buying teams. You have the opportunity to improve the shopping experience of millions of customers and build the data products that power better decisions across the entire organization. You will:
   

  

* Ideate and develop Build scalable machine learning products across various domains, working directly with colleagues to explore and shape data\-driven solutions.
* Deploy and own Take models from development to production, owning the full ML ops lifecycle in close collaboration with our tech teams.
* Experiment and iterate Continuously improve production models, MLOps flows, and contribute to the broader data platform.


 We currently have a number of models in production across different business domains. Examples include recommendation systems, sales and returns forecasting, customer segmentation and lifetime value prediction, and marketing budget optimization. In our latest work, we built a recommender system using a LLM and a multimodal model that combines text and image features, allowing us to create more meaningful recommendations. Our work is powered by a modern tech stack including Python, GCP, BigQuery, dbt, git, Argo Workflows, Docker, Kubernetes and Airflow.
   

  

**About The Team**
 You will join a centralized Data \& Analytics team, working closely with data engineers, analysts, and tracking \& measurement experts. The team operates Agile, giving you substantial responsibility and the freedom to make impactful decisions from the start. You’ll be based at our Service Office, with flexible remote work options.
   

  

 The Data \& Analytics team supports a diverse range of organizational departments with their data needs, giving you the chance to work with a variety of data sources and use cases.
   

  

 Your Experience:
   

  

* Strong fundamental experience as a Data Scientist or Machine Learning Engineer (4\+ years of experience).
* Proficiency in Python for delivering production\-ready code.
* Proficiency in SQL, experience with dbt is a big plus.
* While hands\-on experience with GCP is a big plus, familiarity with a similar cloud platform is also valuable.


 Experience with other aspects of this role are great to have, but can also be learned on the job.
   

  

* Strong knowledge of CI/CD.
* Familiarity with Docker and Kubernetes.
* Experience with operationalizing machine learning models.


**Who you are:**
* Proactive and able to take ownership of projects end\-to\-end, from idea to production.
* You are able to identify opportunities where data and machine learning can have real business impact.
* You stay up\-to\-date with the latest industry trends and you are eager to implement your learnings.
* You are able to explain your solutions to a non\-technical audience
* Strong communication and collaboration skills to work effectively with cross\-functional teams.


 What we offer
   

  

* 30 vacation days
* An annual profit sharing (variable) bonus
* Vitality budget
* Contribution towards your pension
* Hybrid enviroment
* A great culture
* An employee discount on products purchased from de Bijenkorf

 Onsite roles require full\-time presence in the company’s facilities.
   

  

 Field roles are most effectively done outside of the company’s main facilities, generally at the customers’ or suppliers’ locations.

title: Senior Full-Stack Engineer
description:

We track citations, sentiment, and visibility across every major AI engine, processing billions of crawler logs and AI responses in real time. Thousands of companies use what we build to make decisions every day.
 



  





 Our stack: TypeScript monorepo (React, Next.js, Fastify, Prisma, tRPC), backed by Postgres, Elasticsearch, Redis, and ClickHouse, running on Google Cloud. Multiple services — API server, AI gateway, ingest pipelines, log processing — each with its own scaling challenges.
   




 What You'll Do

* Design and improve data pipelines that move millions of records through Postgres, Elasticsearch, Redis, and ClickHouse daily
* Build and operate microservices on Google Cloud (Cloud Run, Pub/Sub, CI/CD) — including keeping them observable with Sentry, structured logging, and custom dashboards
* Contribute to how we work: code review standards, testing practices (Vitest, Playwright), and technical direction
* Pair with the founding team directly — no layers of management between you and decisions.


 What We're Looking For
 * Strong experience with TypeScript, React, and Node.js (we use Fastify, but framework\-specific knowledge transfers)
* Comfort designing and querying relational databases (we use Postgres with Prisma)
* Experience building APIs — whether tRPC, REST, GraphQL, or a mix
* Familiarity with job queues, async processing, or event\-driven architectures
* A habit of writing tests and reviewing code thoughtfully


 Nice to Have

* Working with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google)
* Data visualization (Recharts, D3, or similar)
* Monorepo tooling (Turborepo, pnpm workspaces)
* Google Cloud Platform

**Direct influence on product and technical direction — this isn't a feature factory** 




**A codebase that's genuinely well\-maintained (TypeScript strict, Biome linting, comprehensive types)**

title: UX Writer
description:
TicketSwap is on a mission to become the world’s favorite marketplace to safely buy and sell tickets. With over 18 million fans across 42 countries, we’re building a platform that people love and trust.


On this journey, how we communicate with our fans is essential. We’re looking for a versatile UX Writer who will be part of our UX Writing team, collaborating daily with Product and other teams across the business, and reporting to our UX Writing \& Localization Manager. You’ll create UX copy for customer\-facing UX flows and content touchpoints for new features in our app and website.


We’d like to work with someone who's eager to deliver on their writing craft – with our fans, organizers and all walks of event goers in mind. Essentially, we’re looking for someone who knows how to solve user problems by transforming complex concepts into easily understandable pieces of copy. Right up your alley? Read on.


The UX Product Design team is a creative, fun and dedicated group of skilled UX Writers, Designers, and a UX Researcher. We’re driven and focused, but we also know how to encourage thinking outside of the box, always iterating to create fresh and engaging experiences that connect with our fans. Come join us!

### **What you’ll do**


As a UX Writer at TicketSwap, you’ll bring your content\-first perspective to designing our user experience. Here’s a glimpse of what that includes:

* Design content with the team for web and mobile, including microcopy, interaction flows, error messages, notifications, and in\-product messaging across our user experience
* Collaborate closely with UX Writers, Designers, Product Managers, Engineers, and our UX Researcher on the daily to shape the strategy and UX concepts from ideation to delivery
* Manage localization into 17 languages of all your content designs, along with our UX Writing \& Localization Manager, UX Writer, Junior UX Writer and external freelance team of skilled translators
* Keep a close eye on quality assurance of our content and localization in our TMS, connected CMS and localization management tools
* Shape your content using TicketSwap’s tone of voice to strike the right tone and bring our brand voice and consistency into your work
* Document and share processes, workflows, feedback history, and content strategy decisions for clear communication across teams
* Present your work at design and content critiques to actively give and receive feedback with UX Writers, Designers, UX Researcher and other stakeholders across the business
* Contribute to the UX Writing team’s roadmap and goals, such as content guidelines, glossaries, developing content patterns, integrating AI for increased efficiency, audits, Content Circle initiatives and more
* Support the Customer Experience, Marketing and Legal teams in optimizing and localizing content like FAQs, chatbot flows, SEO, in\-app wording and email/push notifications

**Required for your application:**


A portfolio with examples of your UX writing/content design skills. We’re looking for app content, website content designs, product marketing campaigns – anything that clearly shows off your love of words in the UX field so we can get a feel for your style.

### **About you**

* You’re a seasoned UX Writer with around 3\-5 years’ hands\-on experience in UX writing and localization management – you’ve got a solid point\-of\-view about writing, languages and user experience
* You’re available 40 hours per week, speak native (or C1\) English, and any additional languages are a plus
* You’re analytical and experimental, using data to back your decisions
* You prioritize your own work autonomously, are eager to collaborate, and actively reach out for support when needed
* You’re comfortable presenting your writing approach to stakeholders and find enjoyment in documenting your thinking, sharing your work, and getting feedback
* You have empathy, an appreciation for humor, and an affinity for the events scene
* You bring strong project management skills and are able to juggle cross\-team collaboration with your UX writing workload
* You’ve got experience with tools like Figma, Lokalise, Intercom, ChatGPT and/or other content management and design tools
* You’re based in The Netherlands, are an EU resident, or have an existing visa to work here (a relocation package isn’t available)

### **Why you’ll love it here**


At TicketSwap, you’ll join a creative, international team shaping how millions of fans experience live events. You’ll have the opportunity to:

* Craft meaningful product experiences that help fans buy and sell tickets with confidence
* Turn complex flows into simple, human, and engaging content across our app and website
* Work closely with Designers, Product Managers, Engineers, Researchers and more in cross\-functional teams
* See your work live quickly and used by millions of fans worldwide
* Take ownership of your craft, with space to experiment, learn, and improve how we communicate

### **Excited and ready to join our team?**


Research shows that women and those in traditionally underrepresented groups tend to apply only if they check all the boxes. That’s a shame, as we want to build a diverse and inclusive team. If you know you’ve got what it takes but don’t quite meet every requirement for the role, we still encourage you to apply.


Apply now and remember to include your portfolio. We'll take a look and get back to you as soon as possible!

title: AI Engineer (EdTech) - Student Worker
description:
Studocu helps millions of students find and share the study materials they need. We have 50M\+ monthly active users, and we're building Studocu AI: a set of AI\-powered features that make studying faster and more effective. Think course companions, diagnostic exams, and smart content recommendations, all powered by LLMs and agentic systems.

**The Role**


We're looking for a working student who wants to get their hands dirty building AI features that real students will use. You won't be fetching coffee or organizing spreadsheets. You'll be writing Python, working with LLMs/Langfuse/Langgraph and shipping code alongside our AI Engineering and Data Science team.


This is a role for someone who's already tinkering with AI on their own: building side projects, experimenting with APIs, breaking things and figuring out why. We care less about your grades and more about what you've built. This opening is strictly not an internship.

**What you'll actually do**


You'll work on real features within Studocu AI (studocu.com/ai). Depending on your skills and what's in flight, that could look like:

* Building and testing prompt chains and agent workflows in Langgraph
* Prototyping new AI features (evaluation tools, content processing pipelines, conversational flows)
* Running evaluations on model outputs writing scoring logic, comparing approaches, spotting failure modes
* Helping improve our document processing and retrieval pipelines
* Contributing to internal tools that help the team move faster


You'll be paired with a Senior AI engineer who will review your code, give you context, and make sure you're learning, but you'll be expected to own your work and push it forward.

 **Must\-haves:**

* Eligible to work in the Netherlands. We do not provide any sponsorships for this role.
* Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's programme (Computer Science, AI, Data Science, Math or similar)
* Comfortable writing code
* Familiar with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, or similar). You've called one from code, not just used ChatGPT
* You've built something with AI outside of coursework. A side project, a hackathon entry, a tool you made for yourself
* Available for at least 16 hours per week for a minimum of 6 months

 **Very nice\-to\-haves (hey, we have to dream):**

* Experience with frameworks like LangChain, LangGraph, or similar orchestration tools
* Familiarity with FastAPI or other async Python web frameworks
* Exposure to vector databases (Milvus, Pinecone, Weaviate, etc.)
* Basic understanding of evaluation and monitoring for LLM applications
* You've deployed something to production, even if it was small

The AFS Interest Rates Division has roots dating back 175 years and continues to expand in both its core established and new markets. In total AFS Group counts more than 175 employees, with ambitious growth plans centered on how to best serve our customers in this dynamic, ever\-changing, and fast\-paced markets. The AFS Interest Rates Division consists of a group of 40\+ ambitious and motivated professionals across Money \& Capital Markets and Fixed Income providing best in class solutions to serve a variety of public sector, corporate, banking, and institutional clients.


At AFS Group, our values drive how we work and grow together. We are committed to being knowledgeable in our field, collaborative in our approach, entrepreneurial in our mindset, accurate in our execution, and ethical in all our dealings.

  

We are seeking talented Brokers to help grow and deepen relationships with corporates, governments, and public institutions across the Asia. You’ll be supported by AFS’ robust product suite, as well as the expertise and network of our experienced colleagues. The Interest Asia desk specializes in brokering deposits, commercial paper (CP), certificates of deposit (CD), and other short\-term interest rate instruments. * Repurchase Agreements

* Exploring AI use cases within the business

* Building AI Solutions from idea to prototype, to a working solution

* We’re not looking for a traditional data scientist, but someone who builds AI tools and automation solutions

**Who we looking for**

* Strong interest in AI and automation
* Builder mindset
* Able to create prototypes quickly
* Works independently and proactively
* Quickly learn new tools
* Able to communicate effectively with non\-technical teams

**Technical skills**

* Able to read Python

* LLM APIs (OpenAI / Gemini / Anthropic)

**Nice to have**

**What we offer**

* A unique workplace on the Stock Exchange floor in Amsterdam.
* A diverse, international, and ever\-growing work environment with highly driven teams.
* Space for your refreshing and entrepreneurial ideas to help grow our business.
* Training sessions, coaching and development opportunities for your personal growth.
* Bi\-annual performance\-based bonus scheme.
* International events and congresses, to help you build your network and drive better sales.
* 27 paid days off per year.
* Depending on where you live, an NS business card or bike lease options.

 **Get in touch!**


Are you ready for a new challenge by becoming a member of this motivated team on the Amsterdam Stock Exchange? Please send your resumé through the application link. The Corporate Recruiter in charge is Saranya Nihot. If possible, we could prefer you to add a link to a portfolio website or github page.

2nd round: Job interview with Head of IT \& Infrastructure \& Head of Energy

title: Senior Fullstack Engineer
description:
**About Us**


Scaler is a sustainability data platform built for the real estate industry. We give real estate managers and investors the data they need to protect asset value, meet reporting obligations, and reduce long\-term risk. Our product is data\-heavy, increasingly built on LLMs, and operates in a domain where data errors carry real financial consequences.

**The role**


We're looking for a senior fullstack engineer who builds AI\-powered features in production. You'll own significant parts of the product end to end, from system design to the final interface, working directly with product and designers to turn complex data into tools clients actually use.


You'll also help raise the technical bar across the team through code reviews, architecture input, and engineering standards that keep the codebase clean as we grow.

**What you'll do**

* Design and ship full\-stack features across a data\-intensive platform used by real estate investment teams
* Build and maintain features that put LLMs to work in production, including RAG pipelines, LLM integrations, guardrails, and evaluation frameworks
* Lead architectural decisions for backend systems built to handle continued growth in data volume and client load
* Set engineering standards across code reviews, testing, monitoring, and observability

**What you'll bring**

* Minimum 5 years of fullstack engineering experience with a track record of shipping production systems
* Deep experience in Python and JavaScript/TypeScript with strong command of CSS
* Production experience with LLM integration, including prompt engineering, RAG pipelines, function calling, evals, or guardrails
* Experience with one or more: FastAPI, Laravel, Vue.js, Tailwind CSS, ECharts
* Proven ability to work with large, complex datasets and build systems that surface them clearly
* Experience with web metrics, monitoring, and observability tooling
* Strong fundamentals in data structures and algorithms, applied to real engineering constraints
* Works well in a small, fast\-moving team and owns outcomes, not just tickets

**Our tech stack**

* Front\-end: Vue.js, Pinia, Vite, Tailwind CSS, ECharts
* Back\-end: Laravel, Python, FastAPI
* Infrastructure: AWS, Kafka, Redis, Postgres, Nginx, Envoyer, Docker
* Tools: Sentry, Linear, GitHub, Notion

**Why Scaler?**
===============


When you join Scaler, you will collaborate with a team of curious, sharp, supportive, and genuinely enthusiastic individuals about Scaler's product and unique value. Our people are committed to making a positive impact on our customers' journey to achieving absolute zero carbon.


At Scaler, we’re committed to building a workplace where everyone belongs. Employment opportunities are open to all, regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law


Compensation Range: €75K \- €100K

Trusted by 20,000\+ property managers worldwide, Hostaway is an industry leading, AI\-powered vacation rental management platform designed for professional short\-term rental operators. Hostaway brings everything together for greater efficiency and faster growth.


Profitable, high\-growth, and the first short\-term rental PMS unicorn . Join us at our most exciting stage yet! Learn more about our recent valuation and story here.

### **About the role**


As a Senior Frontend Engineer at Hostaway, you'll take ownership of building and evolving the interfaces that our customers rely on every day. You'll be a key contributor to our frontend architecture, working in close collaboration with Product, Design, and Backend teams to deliver intuitive, high\-performance experiences at scale.


Your responsibility won't end at release \- you'll monitor software in production, troubleshoot issues, and drive ongoing improvements to performance, stability, and user experience. Beyond shipping features, you'll help raise the bar across the team through code reviews, mentorship, and technical leadership.

### **Responsibilities**

* Design, build, and maintain scalable, high\-quality UI components, features, and reusable libraries that support our design system.
* Develop intuitive, performant, accessible, and responsive user experiences across browsers and devices.
* Build and maintain frontend integrations with backend services, including REST APIs, webhooks, and WebSocket\-based real\-time functionality.
* Identify, troubleshoot, and resolve complex frontend issues, including memory leaks, performance bottlenecks, and production bugs.
* Define, present, and lead relevant technical initiatives with a clear understanding of the challenge, why it matters, and the impact the delivery brings.
* Contribute to testing, code reviews, architecture decisions, and engineering best practices to ensure quality and maintainability.
* Collaborate closely with cross\-functional teams and support the monitoring and reliability of released software in production.

  

* 6\+ years of frontend software development experience, with a strong track record of building scalable, maintainable applications.
* Strong proficiency in React, TypeScript, JavaScript, CSS and HTML.
* Experience with state management solutions such as Redux, Context API, or similar.
* Hands\-on experience integrating with backend systems using REST APIs, webhooks, and/or WebSockets.
* Strong ability to diagnose and fix memory leaks, frontend performance issues, and browser/runtime inefficiencies.
* Experience with testing frameworks such as Jest, Cypress, or Playwright,
* Proficiency monitoring and supporting production software.
* Demonstrated expertise in cloud\-native development, with a strong understanding of how to build and maintain resilient and scalable frontend architectures in a cloud environment.

**Bonus points for**

* Experience working on a mature SaaS product, with a focus on improving and extending the UI for a large user base.
* Experience with cloud\-native applications, particularly on AWS.

### **What we offer**

* **Competitive Compensation:** We offer competitive pay based on market rates in the country of the applicant.
* **100% Remote:** Enjoy the freedom to work from anywhere within your country of residence—be it a co\-working space, your home office, or even your dining room table. The choice is yours. Just don’t ask to work in our office (we don’t have one).
* **Equity:** Every role in our company comes with valuable stock options in a fast\-growing and profitable company. This ensures we all share in the company’s success.
* **Values\-Driven Leadership:** Our Core Values are not just words we’ve written to make us feel good. We leverage them daily when making strategic and tactical decisions.
* **Professional Growth:** Our rapid growth offers unparalleled learning and development opportunities, along with a multitude of career advancement opportunities.
* **Annual Paid Leave:** The specific amounts vary by country and are aligned with country and/or contract\-specific norms.
* **Geographic Specific Benefits:** As an international employer, we offer different country\-specific benefits such as Health Insurance and Pensions in countries where these perks are customary. The specifics depend on the country of the applicant.
* **Dynamic Team Culture:** As a global company with team members in over 40 countries, our diverse and international culture fuels our innovation and creativity, providing a key pillar to our success (and making it a lot of fun to work here).


Thank you for your interest. If you apply for this role, you will receive an email from our Talent Acquisition team after your application has been reviewed alongside the qualifications for this role and the qualifications of others who have applied.

* Build production\-grade AI infrastructure, design and implement the core services that make AI features reliable, versioned, observable, and safe to ship at scale.
* Drive the evaluation and quality story, establish quality gates, prompt comparison workflows, hallucination detection, and CI/CD integration so that every AI feature ships with measurable confidence.
* Shape the LLM gateway strategy, ensure provider failover, model routing, cost tracking, and rate limiting are handled centrally \- eliminating duplicated effort across teams.
* Champion cross\-team adoption, work with product teams across Hostaway to make the AI Platform easy to adopt. Publish internal blueprints, define integration patterns, and ensure teams can build specialized agents without deep platform knowledge.
* Influence engineering\-wide practices, drive best practices for prompt engineering, tool design, observability, and agent testing across the organization.
* Mentor and elevate engineers, raise the technical bar across the AI team and beyond \- through code review, architectural guidance, and hands\-on pairing.

  

* 8\+ years of software engineering experience, with at least 2 years operating at a Staff or equivalent level.
* Deep Python expertise. This platform is built in Python \- you should be highly proficient and opinionated about writing production\-grade Python services.
* Production experience with LLM\-based systems. You've built, shipped, and operated AI features or platforms that serve real users at scale. You understand the operational realities: prompt versioning, evaluation, cost management, observability, and failure modes.
* Strong architectural instincts. You can design systems that start simple and evolve gracefully. You know when to keep components in a single service and when to extract. You make pragmatic trade\-offs between speed and extensibility.
* Familiarity with the modern AI tooling ecosystem. Experience with frameworks and libraries such as PydanticAI, LangChain, LangGraph, LiteLLM, Langfuse, Phoenix, MLflow, or comparable tools. You don't need to know all of them, but you should be able to evaluate and integrate them with confidence.
* Multi\-agent architecture experience is a strong plus. If you've designed systems with agent orchestration, tool calling, structured output, and context management, you'll hit the ground running.
* Comfort working across a polyglot stack. The AI Platform is Python, but it doesn't exist in isolation. Hostaway's product includes Go services (including existing MCP servers), a PHP monolith, and a React frontend. You don't need to be proficient in all of these, but you should have the curiosity and adaptability to read, understand, and integrate with code across these technologies.
* Infrastructure fluency. Comfortable with AWS, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, and operating services in production.
* Cross\-team influence. You lead through clarity and credibility, not authority. You've successfully driven adoption of platforms, patterns, or standards beyond your immediate team.

### **What Success Looks Like:**

* The AI Platform is the trusted foundation for every AI feature at Hostaway \- teams build on it by default, not by exception.
* Prompt changes are versioned, tested, and rolled out with the same rigor as code deploys.
* AI features in production are observable, cost\-tracked, and recoverable \- when something breaks, the team knows within minutes, not days.
* Other engineering teams actively seek your guidance on building AI capabilities \- you're the go\-to technical authority for applied AI at Hostaway.
* The platform scales from the current set of use cases to a broader agent ecosystem without requiring fundamental rearchitecture.

title: Cloud Account Executive - Employee Services, ITSM - Amsterdam
description:
**Description**
---------------


* Job Description

**Do you have an entrepreneurial spirit but want to be part of the Salesforce Ohana?**


Salesforce.com is seeking talented, energetic, quota\-carrying sales professionals to help drive the growth of our **Employee Service solutions**—encompassing both HR Service and IT Service capabilities. The Employee Service Account Executive role focuses on selling our Employee Portal, HR Service Console, Prebuilt Integrations, and ITSM/ITOM capabilities that empower employees and IT teams with the right information, anytime, through a unified, AI\-powered platform.


As an Account Executive, you’ll formulate and execute a scalable sales strategy within a designated vertical—driving growth by penetrating our existing Salesforce customer base and attracting new customers.


You’ll help organisations **improve employee experience, HR efficiency, and IT service delivery with seamless self\-service portals, HR service management, and IT service management solutions.**


This role sits within the **Emerging Business organization**, contributing to Salesforce’s growth in a fast\-paced, collaborative, and innovative atmosphere that fosters product incubation—all while being a valued member of the Ohana.

**Your Role**


You will work with customers to demonstrate the value of Salesforce’s Employee Service suite, which unifies HR Service and IT Service into a single, AI\-driven platform. You’ll guide customers to the right solutions that transform HR and IT service operations, streamline processes, and improve both employee engagement and IT service delivery.

**You’ll be responsible for:**


	+ Prospecting, pipeline generation, and revenue attainment.
	+ Navigating medium to long sales cycles for strategic, high\-value deals.
	+ Ensuring customer satisfaction for early adopters and expansion customers**Your Responsibilities**


	+ Develop strong internal and customer relationships to create win\-win sales opportunities.
	+ Clearly communicate the value of Salesforce’s HR Service and IT Service/ITSM capabilities to potential customers.
	+ Drive revenue growth by closing new business and expanding existing accounts within your vertical.
	+ Develop and execute sales campaigns targeting employee service transformation across HR and IT.
	+ Generate and qualify leads, manage a robust pipeline, and assist core Account Executives in closing strategic deals.
	+ Engage with stakeholders across HR, IT, and operations to understand business challenges and develop tailored, outcome\-based solutions.
	+ Maintain accurate sales forecasting and CRM updates.
	+ Collaborate with solution engineers, marketing, and customer success teams to provide a seamless end\-to\-end client experience.**Your Qualifications**


	+ 6\+ years of quota\-carrying enterprise software or technology sales experience, ideally in SaaS, Employee Experience, HR Service Management, ITSM, or ITOM.
	+ Proven experience in solution selling and value\-based selling to multiple stakeholders.
	+ Strong knowledge of ITSM platforms, IT operations, and the ITIL framework.
	+ Familiarity with HR tech, employee experience platforms, and HCM systems (e.g., Workday) is a plus.
	+ Fluency in English.
	+ Bachelor’s degree strongly preferred.**Key Attributes We’re Looking For**


	+ Strong consultative selling skills with a track record of exceeding sales goals.
	+ Proven ability to navigate complex, multi\-stakeholder sales processes.
	+ Competitive spirit, resourcefulness, and ability to solve complex problems.
	+ Business acumen to articulate ROI across HR, IT, and operational use cases.
	+ Skilled in objection handling, negotiation, and closing high\-value deals.
	+ Ability to collaborate cross\-functionally and act as a trusted advisor.
	+ Knowledge of ITSM tools (e.g., ServiceNow, Atlassian), HCM systems, and employee service solutions.**Why Join Salesforce:**


	+ **Innovative Culture:** Be part of a company that's reshaping the future with advanced AI models and cloud solutions
	+ **Global Impact:** Contribute to products that are transforming industries worldwide
	+ **Career Growth:** Access to professional development opportunities, mentorship programs, and a clear path for advancement
	+ **Benefits \& Perks:** Check out our benefits site which explains our various benefits, including well\-being reimbursement and generous parental leave
Join us as part of the Salesforce Ohana and help organisations transform how they engage, support, and empower their people—across both HR Service and IT Service.

title: Solutions Architect - AI / ML - Training & GPU infra
description:
AI/ML Solutions Architect – Distributed Training \& GPU Infrastructure


**Company**


Join a fast\-moving AI infrastructure team working on the cutting edge of large\-scale ML workloads. This role is ideal for engineers who enjoy solving deep technical challenges in distributed training, multi\-GPU systems, and scalable AI inference infrastructure. You will work directly with AI\-focused clients, helping them get the most out of modern GPUs (H100, B200, etc.) and ML frameworks such as PyTorch (and JAX in some environments).


**Team \& Responsibilities**

Work alongside senior AI and infrastructure engineers building large\-scale GPU platforms. As part of the customer solutions team, you will:


* Design and validate production\-grade distributed training (primary) and largescale inference architectures on large GPU clusters, typically tens to thousands of GPUs
* Work hands\-on with customers to debug, optimize, and scale ML workloads across multi\-node GPU environments
* Act as a technical authority on GPU performance, networking, and schedulers, making trade\-offs at scale and translating customer needs into concrete platform requirements
* Collaborate closely with engineering, product, and R\&D to influence roadmap decisions based on real\-world ML workloads
* This is a hands\-on, technical role; you are expected to work directly in customer environments, not only advise at a high level

**Required skills and experience**

* Hands\-on experience designing and operating production\-grade, multi\-node GPU workloads for training or inference
* Strong background in distributed deep learning (PyTorch Distributed, DeepSpeed) on GPU clusters
* Deep understanding of GPU architecture and interconnects (H100/A100 class, NVLink, InfiniBand)
* Experience with Kubernetes or Slurm and performance tuning using GPU profiling and monitoring tools

This role is not a fit if your experience is limited to single\-node training, high\-level AI strategy, or non\-production research environments. We are looking for engineers and architects who thrive at the intersection of AI workloads and large\-scale infrastructure.


**What's offered**


Location: Remote from anywhere in Europe


Total compensation up to EU 300k (base \+ variable), depending on level and experience


  
Business unit
The Next Chapter W\&S

Europe, Amsterdam

Fully Remote

Yes, but only for candidates already based in Europe.
Is remote possible?
This role is open for both on\-site in The Netherlands as well as full\-remote

No, this is a permanent job with a regular contract of employment.

English

Full\-time, Regular \- indefinite, Regular \- temporary

title: Senior Platform Engineer (Infrastructure)
description:
**About Gelato**
----------------


Gelato has created the world’s largest global network for on\-demand production of custom products – from t\-shirts and mugs to books and wall art. We empower a new generation of ecommerce entrepreneurs to share their creativity worldwide while embracing local, sustainable production. By producing locally and perfectly matching supply with demand, we eliminate waste and reduce carbon emissions.


At Gelato, we don’t own production facilities – we build the software that connects them. With over 140 production partners in 32 countries, our network can deliver custom products to five billion people in just 72 hours. It’s smarter, faster, and greener.


With GelatoConnect, our SaaS platform, we’re driving innovation in print production. GelatoConnect simplifies operations for print producers by automating procurement, workflows, and logistics into one seamless system. By boosting efficiency and cutting costs, it helps businesses scale to meet market demands. With GelatoConnect, we’re reshaping the print industry to be more efficient, sustainable, and profitable.

**Platform**
------------

**Gelato** is an **AI\-first**, cloud\-native platform transforming local, on\-demand production and delivery — with zero physical assets. We power:

* **Global production orchestration**: connecting e\-commerce customers to 130\+ partners across 32 countries. From AI\-guided plate design to smart routing and logistics, we deliver to over 5 billion people in under 72 hours—faster, greener, smarter.
* **GelatoConnect**: our AI\-driven print production OS that helps producers master digital printing — from inventory to shipping — by automating every step: procurement, prepress, printing, packaging, and dispatch. It’s machine\-agnostic, real\-time, and built for uptime, precision, and profitability.

**AI at the Core**
------------------


We are not just early adopters of AI — we’re **AI\-native builders**. Across engineering, product, and operations, we embrace AI to accelerate innovation:

* **AI\-first development** using LLMs for code generation, testing, documentation, and design exploration.
* **Autonomous AI agents** driving platform resilience, observability, cost control, and internal workflow automation.
* **AI\-native product features** built into GelatoConnect and the core platform — improving routing, personalization, and production optimization.
* A culture of **continuous experimentation** with RAG, agent orchestration, prompt engineering, and model fine\-tuning.

**Responsibilities**
--------------------

* Ensure 24/7 platform operations, including on\-call support and incident response.
* Build and evolve **AI agents** that automate scaling, recovery, and diagnostics through control\-plane patterns and event\-driven logic.
* Manage **multi\-cloud infrastructure**, including serverless, through declarative, automated workflows.
* Collaborate with the **Delivery Platform** team to improve DevEx with intelligent internal tooling.
* Drive **reliability, observability, and cost\-efficiency** across Gelato’s global infrastructure.
* Enable engineers with **self\-service, secure, and scalable delivery pipelines**.

**Qualifications**
------------------

* Deep understanding of **Linux fundamentals** (processes, I/O, filesystems, networking, memory management, system internals).
* Deep experience with **AWS** (primary) **and GCP** (bonus), including cost optimizations, EKS, networking, S3, VPC peering, IAM, and related services.
* Strong understanding of **Kubernetes internals**, including networking, security, and cluster operations.
* Hands\-on experience with **GitOps workflows** (e.g. ArgoCD) and **Infrastructure as Code** using Terraform.
* Proficient in **Python, Go**, or other languages — with a willingness to learn Go if not already familiar.
* Comfortable using **AI development tools** like Cursor, Claude, and coding with LLMs.


Experience with or strong interest in building **AI agents and RAG workflows**, with a commitment to learn if needed.

**What it’s like to work at Gelato**
------------------------------------


We are a customer\-obsessed team with the ambition to change the world by connecting technology to the printing industry and making it much more sustainable. Everyone who joins our team must feel genuinely intrigued and motivated by our mission. We expect a lot. We are a driven team with big goals, so we seek individuals who are genuinely passionate about their work and possess an entrepreneurial spirit. Our culture is unique and we live by our values, so it's worth learning more about our culture and how we work before presenting your application.


At Gelato, we pride ourselves on our global presence with 14 offices worldwide, fostering a dynamic and diverse work environment. Rooted in a culture that values collaboration, creativity, and camaraderie, we actively cultivate a company culture that thrives on shared experiences. We encourage team members to embrace this culture by working from our inspiring office spaces at least three days a week, allowing for meaningful connections and collective growth.


Lastly, we ask that you please upload your CV in **English**, regardless of which country you are applying from.

title: Senior Cloud Engineer (Azure) – Enterprise Environment
description:
**Who we are**  

Since 1990, we’ve grown into a global leader in personalized medicine. Today, 5,000\+ colleagues in 34 countries work together to support over 200,000 customers worldwide.



From compounding and natural products to software, equipment, and genomics — we deliver smart, ready\-to\-use solutions that make healthcare better.



We’re passionate, science\-driven, and always looking for new ways to improve lives.



Explore your future at careers.fagron.com.

 **About the job**  

We are looking for a highly experienced Senior Cloud Engineer (Azure) to take end\-to\-end ownership of our global Azure platform, while actively enabling and supporting other teams in designing and implementing cloud solutions.



You operate in a global, enterprise\-scale environment, ensuring Azure is not only technically robust, but also secure, governed, cost\-efficient and automation\-driven. You act as a trusted advisor for product, application and platform teams, helping them adopt Azure in a compliant and scalable way.

  


**Key responsibilities**


**Azure platform \& architecture**: Design, build and operate a global Azure platform across multiple regions and subscriptions. Define and evolve enterprise\-grade Azure Landing Zones. Own subscription strategy, networking, RBAC and management group design. Drive standardization and automation using Infrastructure as Code (Bicep, Terraform, ARM)


**Cloud governance (ownership)**: Define and enforce governance frameworks, including policies, management groups, RBAC, naming standards, and tagging. Ensure security, compliance, and audit readiness. Embed controls into CI/CD pipelines.


**Cost management \& FinOps:** Lead Azure cost management practices, budgets, alerts, chargebacks, and optimization. Apply AI\-driven cost analytics and anomaly detection.


**Identity \& application integration:** Manage hybrid identity landscapes (Azure AD, Entra ID, ADFS, on\-prem AD). Support secure access patterns and implement Zero Trust principles.


**AI agents \& cloud automation:** Design and implement AI\-driven agents and intelligent automation using Azure OpenAI, Functions, Logic Apps and event\-driven architectures.


**Team collaboration:** Partner with product and DevOps teams, review architectures, and provide practical guidance on governance, security, and cost efficiency. Mentor teams across regions, drive alignment on platform standards, and act as a trusted advisor.

 **Set yourself apart**  

You possess the following qualifications, background and abilities:



 8\+ years in cloud/platform engineering with deep **Azure expertise**.



Hands\-on experience with:


* **Azure Governance** (Policy, RBAC, Management Groups)
* **Azure AD / Entra ID**, ADFS, on\-prem AD
* **Identity federation and application integration**
* **Azure Cost Management and FinOps**


Skilled in **Infrastructure as Code** (Bicep, Terraform, PowerShell), **CI/CD pipelines** (Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions), and event\-driven automation.



Strong stakeholder communication, advisory skills, and ability to guide teams without becoming a bottleneck.

 **What's on offer?**  

* Being part of an international team.
* Fixed\-term contract with the possibility and intention to extend.
* A market\-conform salary, all in line with your knowledge \& experience.
* Hybrid working with 3 days in the office.
* Annual bonus.
* 28 vacation days.
* Collective health insurance.
* Travel allowance of € 0\.23 per KM (with a max of 150KM per day) or 100% public transportation reimbursement.

  



At Fagron, you’ll find plenty of chances to grow — both personally and professionally. We work in an open, dynamic environment where your ideas and talents can shine.



We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Diversity makes us stronger, and we’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels welcome.

 **Ready for the challenge?**  

Take the next step and join us in shaping the future of personalized medicine. Apply today at careers.fagron.com.



 Please note: we do not accept agency referrals for this position.
