title: Operational Buyer
description:
**Sinclair is Hiring! Join Our Team as a Operational Buyer**


We are currently recruiting for **Operational Buyer** at **Almere**. The **Operational Buyer]** will manage day\-to\-day procurement activities, oversee import and export processes, and perform supply chain analysis within a **medical device** environment. This role is responsible for ensuring timely and compliant purchasing of materials and services while maintaining accurate records, optimizing costs, and supporting operational efficiency through data\-driven decision\-making**.**


The ideal candidate will have

* 3–5 years of procurement and logistics experience in a regulated industry (preferably **medical devices**, **pharmaceuticals**, or **healthcare**).
* Experience managing international shipments and understanding of trade compliance.
* Hands\-on experience with ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Microsoft Dynamics).
* Data analysis or reporting tools like Power BI, Tableau is an asset.

 **Hours of work: 40**

 **About Sinclair**


Founded in 1971, Sinclair is a global medical aesthetics organisation, that delivers an extensive product range. With an in\-house commercial infrastructure, including manufacturing and a network of distributors in leading global markets, our products are sold in 55 countries worldwide.


This is a great time to join Sinclair as we continue to increase our product range and expand into new markets and territories.

##### **Sinclair Values:**


**Act with Integrity** Consistently doing the right thing even when it’s the hard choice; 100% Compliance with all rules, standard operating procedures and guidelines  

**Results\-Driven** Make a business impact in all you do, whether sales, efficiency, operational excellence; it should make a meaningful impact  

**Innovation\-Centered** Redefining Aesthetics, we must be pioneering in how we do business; this can be in products, in service models, or strategy  

**One Company, One Goal** Working towards unified mission, we are all Sinclair and be seen by customers as one company in every way


**Own It!** Be Accountable for your decisions, actions and consequences; Be Reliable to your customers and colleagues

##### **What You'll Be Doing:**


Procurement \& Operational Buying  

Import \& Export Compliance  

Data Analysis \& Reporting  

Cross\-functional collaboration

##### **Your skills and experience**

**Essential:**


3–5 years of procurement and logistics experience in a regulated industry (preferably **medical devices**, **pharmaceuticals**, or **healthcare**).


Experience managing international shipments and understanding of trade compliance.


Hands\-on experience with ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, NetSuite, Microsoft Dynamics).


Data analysis or reporting tools like Power BI, Tableau is an asset

**Desirable:**  

Preferably English and Dutch speaker and reader

##### **What We're Looking For:**


Strong ERP proficiency for procurement and inventory modules.


Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, Data Analysis).


Familiarity with data visualization/reporting tools (e.g., Power BI) is a plus.


Excellent organizational and communication skills.


High attention to detail and analytical mindset.


Problem\-solving ability and a proactive approach to process improvement.


Strong collaboration and stakeholder management skills.

##### **Perks \& Benefits:**


Competitive Salary \& Performance Bonuses  

Career Growth \& Learning Opportunities  

Fun Team Events \& Supportive Culture

 **We have a real focus on developing our people, and by fostering an entrepreneurial culture, we encourage flexibility, accountability and autonomy. The company is full of opportunity for those who wish to grasp it. Our people thrive on engagement, development and a varied workload. You will often be included in projects that require you to collaborate cross\-functionally.**


This is an exciting opportunity to join a successful company, with big ambitions for the future.


If this sounds like you and you meet the requirements, please apply, we would love to hear from you.


Please note if we receive a high volume of applications, we may close the role before the closing date noted.


By making this application you give consent for personal information to be used in automated decision\-making processes relating to key job requirements which are stated in this ad.

 **Sinclair does not accept speculative or unsolicited CVs from Recruitment Agencies. Any unsolicited CVs received will be treated as property of Sinclair and Terms \& Conditions associated with the use of such CVs will be considered null and void**

title: Commercial Strategy Senior Associate - Merchant Value & Pricing
description:
**About the Role**  

  

The Strategy team within Delivery Commercial Operations (DCO) enables data\-driven decision\-making and strategic direction for Uber's delivery business. As a Commercial Strategy Senior Associate, you will play a key role in driving deep dives, business optimizations and coordinating large regional programs related to commercial strategy and merchant value generation. You will work within defined models to help identify opportunities for business growth and provide analytical support for forecasting and planning.
  

  

This is an L4 (Senior Associate) level role, ideal for an analytical professional ready to support us in driving commercial strategy, with immediate focus on value generation and merchant pricing.
  

  

**What you'll do*** **Drive Analytics and Deep Dives:** Perform broad analysis and translate into deep dives and executive presentations to support broader strategic initiatives.
* **Maintain Reporting \& Dashboards:** Build cross\-market reporting framework and dashboards to monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and trends related to commercial strategy and revenue generation.
* **Support Strategic Planning:** Assist in the analysis of performance data to support forecasting, planning, and prioritization efforts alongside commercial leaders.
* **Collaborate Cross\-Functionally:** Collaborate with immediate team members and stakeholders in Sales Operations, Finance, and Product to ensure data accuracy and alignment on tactical work.
* **Generate Insights:** Interpret core sales metrics and data sources to provide data summaries and clear updates to the team regarding business performance.
* **Pricing Analytics and Project Management:** Scale and build pricing models and initiatives cross multiple markets.

**What you'll need*** **Analytical Capability \& Data Fluency:** You understand core sales metrics and data sources. You are proficient in SQL and Excel/Google Sheets for data extraction, manipulation, and dashboarding, and you can produce forecast inputs accurately.
* **Business \& Functional Expertise:** You possess a foundational understanding of basic GTM and SaaS concepts and can utilize data tools with support to analyze performance.
* **Strategic Thinking \& Execution:** You can support strategy projects and contribute to tactical execution with direction, connecting your specific tasks to broader team priorities.
* **Operational Excellence:** You follow established planning processes and assist in maintaining reporting tools to ensure reliability.
* **Communication:** You share data and findings clearly in team meetings using structured templates and standard reporting formats.
* **Innovation \& Problem Solving:** You are able to break down basic problems and contribute to brainstorming solutions for immediate business questions.

**Preferred qualifications*** **Experience:** 3\-5 years of experience in analytics, strategy, sales operations, or a related field.
* **Education:** Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Engineering, or a related field.
* **Technical Proficiency:**
	+ **SQL:** Proficiency in SQL for data extraction and manipulation is required.
	+ **Spreadsheets:** advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets.
	+ **Visualization:** Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker) is a plus.
* **Communication:** Ability to communicate technical data clearly to non\-technical stakeholders.

**Why you should join us*** **Impact:** You will be part of a team that accelerates B2B revenue by empowering commercial teams to acquire and grow high\-value Merchant partnerships.
* **Development:** As part of the DCO team, you will operate in an environment that values both innovation and scale, offering opportunities to grow your strategic and analytical skillset.
* **Culture:** Join a data\-driven culture where you will serve as a trusted partner to the business, transforming complex datasets into actionable insights that drive real\-world execution.

title: Sr. Professional, Analytics
description:
**Values \& Innovation**
------------------------


At Under Armour, we are committed to empowering those who strive for more, and the company's values \- Act Sustainably, Celebrate the Wins, Fight on Together, Love Athletes and Stand for Equality \- serve as both a roadmap for our teams and the qualities expected of every teammate.

  

Our Values define and unite us, the beliefs that are the red thread that connects everyone at Under Armour. Our values are rallying cries, reminding us why we're here, and fueling everything we do.

  

Our pursuit of better begins with innovation and with our team's mission of being the best. With us, you get the freedom to go further \- no matter your role. That means developing, delivering, and selling the state\-of\-the\-art products and digital tools that make top performers even better.

  

If you are a current Under Armour teammate, apply to this position on the **Internal Career Site Here.**

**Purpose of Role**
-------------------


The Sr. Professional Analytics is responsible for turning performance data into actionable insights, drive marketplace point\-of\-sale performance visibility, and work on a suite of sustainable and structured dashboards to manage the business and drive growth. You will also provide next\-level analytics services, enabling the business to make fact\-based decisions. This covers both channel specific as well as product, marketing and country specific topics.

---------------

* Deliver high\-quality analytics services by collecting, mining and analyzing data to generate insights
* Collaborate with stakeholders, supporting functional teams in defining goals, selecting appropriate KPIs, monitoring performance and derive trends and opportunities.

* Work productively with a global EDM team as well as within a global Center of Excellence to share best practices and ensure standardization across the company.
* Provide tracking and visibility to monthly and quarterly revenue targets, supporting cross\-functional alignment across functions to meet financial goals.
* Build and maintain standardized dashboards for key milestones and meetings in the go\-to\-market calendar such as quarterly business reviews and seasonal recaps.
* Support in cross\-functional projects whether self\-assigned or assigned to the team, and effectively communicate and present the outcome to the appropriate audiences.
* Manage performance reporting, create \& own dashboards (Tableau interface) summarizing retailer KPIs.

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. Education and/or Experience:

* Proficiency in SQL (Python, R and other programming languages is a plus)
* A passion for delivering data\-driven insights and recommendations to consistently improve consumer satisfaction and steer business performance across the business
* High level of understanding of BI and visualization tools (Tableau, SAP BI, PowerBi or similar)
* Ability to present information in a clear, concise, and complete manner.
* Well\-developed communication skills, comfortable presenting complex topics to stakeholders at various levels of the organization.

**Workplace Location**
----------------------

**Relocation**
--------------

**Benefits \& Perks**
---------------------

* Paid “UA Give Back” Volunteer Days: Work alongside your team to support initiatives in your local community.
* Under Armour Merchandise Discounts
* Health \& fitness benefits, discounts and resources\- We offer teammates across the country programs to promote physical activity and overall well\-being.

title: Business Development Representative (Dutch Speaking)
description:
Do you know the thrill of turning a cold call into a real business opportunity? At WP SEO AI, we’re looking for an experienced BDR who loves the chase, understands the numbers, and knows that consistent activity drives success.

**About WP SEO AI**

WP SEO AI is a B2B SaaS company providing AI\-powered SEO content generation tools that help businesses grow their online presence through smarter content and search strategies.

We have grown from €3M to €10M in recurring yearly revenue in 2025 and are targeting €50M within the next 24 months. We’re a bootstrapped, founder\-led company, which means we don’t rely on external investors. This gives us the freedom to make fast, long\-term decisions independently. Our team of 175\+ people supports over 1700 clients across the Netherlands, Finland, Germany, Belgium, the UK and US while adding around 100 clients each month. We are sales\-driven and continuously strengthening our operations to support our next phase of growth.

Our belief is that AI and advanced algorithms can transform how companies approach their online visibility. That’s why we created WP SEO AI (WordPress Search Engine Optimization Artificial Intelligence): A platform designed to help businesses increase organic traffic, improve search rankings, and reach more customers online.

Our technology combines advanced AI with deep human SEO expertise to automate and optimize content strategies. We deliver practical, data\-driven solutions that create clear business impact and measurable results.

**Why join us?**

We believe success starts with the right people. You’ll join a diverse, ambitious, and supportive team where we learn from mistakes, help each other grow. Our international team includes sales and marketing experts, AI specialists, creative thinkers, and business professionals working closely together. You will be part of a flat organization structure where collaboration, autonomy, and ownership go hand in hand. We keep processes simple and decisions fast, so ideas can move from concept to action quickly.

We foster a culture of trust, openness, and responsibility. You’ll have the freedom to take ownership of your work, share ideas, and see the impact of what you do. We encourage continuous learning, mutual support, and celebrating successes together. Your impact truly matters, whether you're closing deals, shaping strategy or driving innovation.

At WP SEO AI, curiosity leads the way. We ask questions, share knowledge, and continuously improve to empower ourselves and our customers. We take responsibility for both our successes and challenges, act proactively, and maintain a positive, solution\-focused mindset. We hire smart, driven people and give them the space to learn, grow, and succeed.

While we take our work seriously, we also believe enjoying what we do drives better ideas and stronger teams. That’s why we celebrate together with team trips, exciting sales challenges, and unforgettable experiences worldwide.

**The role**

As Business Development Representative, you’ll take full ownership of building our outbound pipeline. You’ll reach out to potential clients who can benefit from our leading AI\-driven SEO platform and play a key role in our international growth. Our solution is perfect for cold calling, relevant for every business that wants to grow online, which means every conversation has potential.

You’ll be part of a driven sales team where performance and energy go hand in hand. Our current targets are 40 meetings per month, and records go well beyond 100, proof of what’s possible with focus and consistency. You’ll speak with business owners and decision\-makers from all kinds of industries, which keeps every day dynamic and rewarding.

**What you'll do**

* Spend most of your day on the phone, turning first contact into real opportunities.
* Reach out to new prospects through calls, email and LinkedIn, with a strong focus on outbound conversations.
* Convert high\-quality leads provided by our RevOps team so you can focus on what you do best: engaging and booking meetings.
* Collaborate closely with Account Executives to ensure a smooth handover and maximise conversions.
* Analyse your performance, share insights and keep improving every week.

**What we are looking for**

* Fluent in Dutch, plus a good command of English (the company’s working language).
* Experience in outbound sales and customer contact.
* Preferably a Bachelor’s degree or higher.
* Interest in Tech and Marketing.
* Positive, proactive mindset with persistence in sales.
* Driven, eager to learn, and always looking for ways to improve.

**What we offer you**

* A dynamic, fast\-changing role at one of Europe’s fastest\-growing scale\-ups, growing by about ten new team members every month and expanding from our Amsterdam headquarters to conquer Europe.
* A culture of empowerment, trust and openness where we focus on personal development and give our colleagues the freedom to make mistakes, learn and grow.
* The opportunity to work with groundbreaking AI technology in SEO and GEO (Generative Engine Optimization).
* A fixed salary of €3,500 per month (excluding vacation pay), plus an uncapped performance\-based incentive, with BDRs earning an average of €1,500 extra per month and top performers consistently above €3,000\.
* Our new office at Rokin in Amsterdam, now home to more than 115 colleagues.
* A growing international team with plenty of company outings, regular trips to Finland, and exciting sales and customer success games that include international business trips for top performers.

Ready to pick up the phone and help us conquer Europe one call at a time? Apply now and let’s grow together.

Soort dienstverband: Fulltime

Salaris: €50\.000,00 \- €100\.000,00 per jaar

title: Director of AI enablement
description:
**Ready to Join the Backstage Crew?**

At Bleckmann, we’ve been delivering on promises since 1862\. As a market leader in supply chain management for fashion and lifestyle brands, we keep the show running behind the scenes — from moving boxes to moving data, from pack \& ship to IT and HR.


But we’re not just logistics experts. We’re The Backstage Crew — a tight\-knit team of 6,500\+ people who make fashion and lifestyle brands shine by doing the work that matters most, out of the spotlight but never out of impact.


Whether you're on the warehouse floor or behind a screen, you’ll find:


* **Strong connections** with colleagues who support and celebrate you.
* **Fast growth** in a company that’s expanding across Europe, the US, and Asia.
* **High energy** in a dynamic environment where no two days are the same.
* **Guided freedom** to take initiative, solve problems your way, and grow your career.

We believe in **entrepreneurship, expertise, excellence,** and **engagement** — and we live these values every day. From repairing returned goods to reducing waste, we help brands extend product lifecycles with sustainability in mind.


So if you’re ready to roll out the red carpet for our clients — and for each other — we’ve got a spot for you.


Behind the scenes is where the real excitement begins. Ready to join us?


**Tasks and responsibilities:**

Purpose of the Role


To lead the strategic enablement and integration of Artificial Intelligence across Bleckmann’s business units, driving innovation, operational efficiency, sales effectiveness and client\-centric solutions. The AI Enablement Director will act as a catalyst for transformation, bringing knowledge into Bleckmann and ensuring AI capabilities are embedded into core processes, decision\-making frameworks, and digital products.


AI Strategy \& Roadmap


* Define and execute Bleckmann’s AI enablement strategy aligned with business goals (business growth, client retention, operational excellence and automation).
* Identify high\-impact use cases across all functions and departments.
* Translate the use cases into an implementation roadmap
* Follow up on the realization of the business cases (project Opex and Capex budgets, realization of savings, …)

Cross\-Functional Enablement


* Partner with IT, Data, Operations, Business development and Client Solutions teams to embed AI into workflows.
* Support departments in identifying and prioritising AI opportunities, including predictive analytics, intelligent automation, generative AI and AI agents.

Governance \& Ethics


* Establish AI governance frameworks, ensuring compliance with data privacy, security, and ethical standards.
* Collaborate with Legal and Risk teams and CISO to assess and mitigate AI\-related risks.

Capability and Enthusiasm Building


* Lead internal training and awareness programs to foster AI literacy.
* Develop toolkits, playbooks, and best practices for AI adoption.
* Ensure the organization is eager to adopt AI as support, e.g. by building an AI community, organizing AI bootcamps, …
* Support the organization with managing change where needed

Innovation \& Vendor Management


* Evaluate and onboard AI platforms, tools, and partners (in collaboration with Procurement, IT, …)
* Stay abreast of emerging technologies and trends to inform strategic decisions.

Performance \& Impact


* Define KPIs to measure AI adoption, ROI, and business impact.
* Report progress to senior leadership and contribute to transformation steering committees.
**Profile:**

Required Experience \& Qualifications


* Proven Track Record: This is a senior leadership role requiring demonstrated success in leading AI enablement and transformation programmes. The ideal candidate has done this before, including the necessary change management — with tangible results in complex, multi\-stakeholder environments.
* Strategic \& Operational Expertise: Ability to translate AI strategy into operational execution across diverse business functions.
* Stakeholder Leadership: Strong influencing skills across senior leadership, technical teams, and operational stakeholders.
* Industry Knowledge: Experience in logistics, supply chain, or operational transformation is highly desirable.
* Technical Acumen: Solid understanding of AI technologies, data platforms, and automation tools. But is also able to explain to ‘non\-technical’ employees and managers.
*At Bleckmann, we are guided by our values: We take a parachute and jump (Entrepreneurship), we unpack our knowledge (Expertise), we raise the bar with every box (Excellence), and we spark energy that connects (Engagement).*

title: Sr Associate Supply Chain (Site Supply Chain Planner)
description:
If you feel like you’re part of something bigger, it’s because you are. At Amgen our shared mission—to serve patients—drives all that we do. It is key to becoming one of the world’s leading biotechnology companies. We are global collaborators who achieve together—researching, manufacturing and delivering ever\-better products that reach over 10 million patients worldwide. It’s time for a career you can be proud of.

**Sr Associate Supply Chain (Site Supply Chain Planner)**


At Amgen Breda, medicines are labelled, assembled, stored and shipped to various countries worldwide. More than 1000 people with 42 nationalities contribute daily to supply chain operations, manufacturing, commercial activities and clinical research.

**Live**

**What you will do**


In this vital operational role, you will be responsible for ensuring a stable and feasible short‑term supply plan. You will manage the Detailed Schedule of production orders and oversee short‑and mid\- term capacity planning, working closely with Production, QA, Warehouse and the Region. Your work will include preparing and owning the frozen/firmed window, checking component availability, and ensuring all materials and capacity are aligned for flawless execution.


You will collaborate with regional teams to safeguard market supply, act promptly on deviations, and contribute to Class A MPS adherence. You will also, facilitate the daily SQDIP meeting and weekly capacity meetings, facilitate scrap activities and follow up on flowstoppers in the day to day processes.


In addition to your core planning responsibilities, you will support product launches by providing planning input. You will also play an active role in operational excellence initiatives, conducting weekly and monthly analyses and proposing improvement actions based on KPI performance.

**Key responsibilities include:**

* Maintaining a feasible, efficient short‑term production schedule and capacity outlook
* Owning and coordinating your lines in terms of feasibility and efficiency, ensuring supply.
* Coordinating cross‑functional alignment for production plans and ensure on time deliveries.
* Providing planning support for product launches and short‑term improvement projects
* Owning KPI reporting and delivering analysis\-driven recommendations

**Win**

**What we expect of you**


We are all different, yet we all use our unique strengths to serve patients. The professional we are looking for is analytical, structured and highly organized, thriving in a dynamic operational environment where planning accuracy and cross‑functional alignment are essential. You bring strong technical know‑how, solid problem‑solving skills and a proactive mindset that supports both day‑to‑day stability and continuous improvement.

**Qualifications**


You bring a background that enables you to navigate complex supply chain environments with confidence. Specifically, you have:

* A Bachelor’s degree or Master degree in Supply Chain, Logistics or Business Administration.
* Experience with SAP, particularly within Plan‑to‑Stock, Inventory Management and Capacity Planning.
* Strong command of MS Office tools (Excel, Access, PowerPoint, Word), Smartsheet, Power Bi and Tableau
* Fluency in Dutch and English, both spoken and written.
* Experience in project management and familiarity with Lean, JIT, Six Sigma, FMEA or RCA methodologies.
* A solid understanding of GMP requirements and experience in pharmaceutical or other regulated industries.
* Experience working in an international, complex organizational environment.

**Core Competencies**


You naturally demonstrate:

* A “can‑do” mentality and strong ownership of your responsibilities.
* Excellent communication and analytical capabilities.
* A structured, detail‑oriented problem‑solving approach.
* The ability to maintain oversight while balancing multiple priorities.
* A proactive, results‑driven and self‑steering working style.

**Additional Profile Requirements:**


Beyond the core profile, success in this role is accelerated by:

* Strong **detailed scheduling capabilities**, ensuring stable and feasible short‑term plans.
* A clear **operational mindset**, comfortable navigating fast‑moving planning cycles.
* Demonstrated **GenAI literacy**, with openness to leveraging AI‑assisted tools.
* Solid **automation skills** and the motivation to optimize planning and data processes.
* An **automation‑thinking mindset**, constantly seeking more efficient, standardized ways of working.
* A continuous improvement mentality, actively contributing to standardization and automated initiatives.
* A high level of **organization, structure and methodical discipline**.
* Exceptional accuracy and consistency in administrative and planning‑related tasks.

**Thrive**

**What you can expect from us**


As we work to develop treatments that take care of others, we also care for our teammates’ growth and well‑being. Amgen is regularly recognized as a Best Place to Work.


You will find:

* Vast opportunities to develop your career across our global organization.
* A diverse and inclusive community that values your ideas and encourages innovation.
* A competitive Total Rewards Plan including salary, bonus structure, 13th month, holiday allowance and collective health insurance.
* A focus on vitality with an on\-site gym, vitality program and healthy restaurant options.

title: Lead Product Designer - Healthcare
description:
**Location**
------------


Amsterdam

**Employment Type**
-------------------


Full time

**Location Type**
-----------------

**Department**
--------------


Design

  

Lead Product Designer \- Healthcare

**Reaktor’s award\-winning tech and design community is looking for a Lead Product Designer to join our Amsterdam office.**

**You understand how to sell creative work — grounding every choice in brand strategy, user insight, and business value.**


At its core, Reaktor is a technology company that helps ambitious organizations become digital leaders.


Reaktor Health Business Unit builds high\-value, safe, and robust digital health systems that measurably reduce administrative burden, enable clinicians to better focus on valuable care, and unlock hybrid care delivery. Our clients include global leaders such as Hologic and Varian as well as regional forward\-looking companies such as Zorg bij jou, Thuisarts, Xpert Clinics, Terveystalo, ZAP Surgical, and Orion Pharma.


Our work increasingly extends to health large language model (LLM) applications and AI in healthcare, where we design and implement compliant, explainable, and scalable AI solutions that support automation of administrative work, assisted care coordination, and enhanced patient engagement.

### **Your daily tasks include:**

* Leading the design of product experiences across digital touchpoints, from strategy to final pixel
* Working side by side with developers, strategists, and product owners to co\-create holistic solutions
* Conceptualizing and prototyping new features and flows to test, validate, and sell ideas
* Bringing a strong visual point of view — from brand systems and typography to motion and transitions
* Presenting creative directions clearly and persuasively to both clients and internal teams
* Participating in workshops and collaborative sessions to uncover insights and shape vision
* Maintaining a balance between high\-level design leadership and hands\-on execution

### **You love solving design problems with craft, logic, and collaboration.**


To thrive in this position, you’ll need:

* 8\+ years of experience in digital product design, preferably within fast\-paced consultancy or client\-facing environments
* The ability to think conceptually and strategically, connecting business goals to design decisions
* Confidence in selling your work through compelling storytelling and rationale
* Immaculate taste and a sharp eye for visual design across layout, typography, and UI systems
* Strong interaction design skills and a clear understanding of user flow logic and responsive behavior
* Fluency in modern design tools (e.g. Figma, Adobe Creative Suite) and familiarity with prototyping or motion tools
* Experience working in interdisciplinary teams with developers, strategists, and copywriters
* Comfort navigating ambiguity and proactively shaping briefs or concepts from scratch
* Curiosity, humility, and a constant appetite for learning and evolving your craft
* Fluent English language skills, with fluent Dutch as a strong plus \- many of our Healthcare clients are based in the Netherlands

**Why join us?**


For two decades, we’ve been developing qualities and capabilities for adapting, reacting, and supporting change – building Reaktor to hold up in a fast\-paced environment while ensuring the well\-being of our people.

**While working at Reaktor, we’ll provide you with:**

* The ability to impact how you work. Together with the client, your team chooses the approach, technologies, and methodologies you think will work best in any given situation
* A community with as much support as your heart desires
* A team that’s not only experienced but considerate as well – they all want you to succeed
* A sustainable work\-life balance and support for your daily life outside of work. (e.g., free moving day, Reaktor car share, sick child care services, office space to use for your private events, etc.)
* An opportunity to grow as a professional. In addition to the day\-to\-day work, we offer internal training courses, community events, and 15\-minute coffee breaks to discuss hot topics in tech and design
* A possibility to take part in more extended academy\-like studies like Cloud Academy
* 300\+ hobby clubs, from winter swimming and running to knitting and archery, that bring people together outside of (and sometimes inside) office hours. Many of these are supported by Reaktor
* Check more benefits \& perks below

**Diversity, equity, and inclusion**


Together, we are committed to making Reaktor an environment that embraces and values all of our employees for the whole of who they are. We encourage applicants from all backgrounds to apply, knowing that studies show that members of underrepresented communities don’t apply for jobs unless they fully satisfy all of the requirements. If you're thinking twice about applying for this reason, we invite you to send us your application anyway. You could be just the person we're looking for.

  

Interested? Drop us a line – We'll be happy to hear from you!

title: DevOps Engineer
description:
We are currently looking for a new medior DevOps engineer for our location in Rotterdam. Not easily convinced? We are pretty confident that we have a lot of exciting things to offer you so let us challenge your curiosity! Important note: we’re looking for candidates who are currently residing in the Netherlands!

**What will you be doing?**

As a DevOps engineer, you will collaborate with 2 DevOps engineers in your team, enabling our product teams through shared developer platform capabilities. You will build and maintain the internal developer platform and standardized self\-service tools that allow our product teams to deploy and manage their own services with minimal friction. You will also help improve CI/CD workflows, strengthen reusable golden paths for common tasks, and support clear documentation and standards so teams can work independently and consistently. Keywords for your responsibilities include optimization and automation. We are constantly working on optimizing our websites, platform, and the infrastructure that supports everything. We expect you to stay updated on the latest developments in AI, security and other DevOps\-related matters and assess whether the latest trends are relevant to our company.

**What success looks like in your first 3\-6 months:**

* Contribute to reducing time\-to\-deploy by improving CI/CD workflows and automation.
* Help reduce toil by building shared developer platform capabilities and clearer golden paths.
* Improve operational visibility by contributing to dashboards, logs, or alerting practices in our cloud environment.
* Support reliability and security improvements by strengthening secure defaults and implementing security scanning.
* Enhance cloud cost awareness by facilitating visibility into usage and spend.

Our ideal candidate is a motivated medior with 2\+ years of experience as a DevOps engineer or a similar role (such as Systems Engineer) and is eager to further advance in this field.

**Must\-have fundamentals**

* Containerization systems (Docker, etc)
* CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions)
* Kubernetes (Google Kubernetes Engine)
* Linux (mainly Ubuntu/Debian)

**Nice\-to\-have experience**

* Infrastructure as Code systems (Terraform)
* GCP (Google Cloud Platform)
* Scripting or programming languages (Bash, PHP, Python, Go)
* LLM integration systems and techniques
* Infrastructure security practices

But most importantly, we value your willingness to continuously expand your knowledge and expertise. You are not expected to master everything we apply from day one.

**About us**

First, a bit about us! Meet TreeHouse: a leading digital enterprise specializing in the real estate market, with a focus on the rental market. We help over 3 million home seekers find a new home monthly in the Netherlands through our labels and high\-traffic platforms Pararius.nl, Huurwoningen.nl, and the CRM package Pararius Office and Leadflow developed for real estate agents. We are also active in the UK with our platform RentaRoof.co.uk!

We are an informal company with about 90 enthusiastic employees. We work in small teams, aiming to switch quickly and collaborate extensively. This approach allows for a lot of freedom in the way we work, and our teams are constantly improving themselves. Our headquarters is in Rotterdam on the 22nd floor of Blaak 555, providing a guaranteed beautiful view of Rotterdam!

Although we are growing significantly as a company, we operate in small teams where we try to switch quickly and engage in a lot of collaboration. This provides a high degree of freedom in the way we work. You should be comfortable with this way of working: communicating with your team members, providing input, engaging in discussions and collaborations with other engineers and product teams, ensuring smooth software deployment. Weekly meetings for an optimal workflow are not uncommon, allowing you the space to shape your own work method.

Are you convinced yet? Alright, a bit more information about our terms and culture::

* You will receive your own laptop to work on.
* There is a daily lunch with plenty of fresh and healthy products.
* Table tennis and gaming (Super smash, mario kart!) are daily activities. Outside working hours, there are regular sporting events planned.
* We have frequent drinks, dinners, and team outings (to counterbalance the sports).
* You are expected to be in the office, we like working together and being physically present.
* Good secondary employment conditions, such as a pension plan where 70% is covered by us.
* A pleasant workplace in Rotterdam, in the fantastic building at Blaak 555, with a stunning view over our beautiful city!

**Excited?**

Does this sound like the ideal job for you? Then we would love to receive your motivation and your recent work experience.

Soort dienstverband: Fulltime

Salaris: €2\.650,00 \- €4\.750,00 per maand

* Bedrijfsfeesten
* Bedrijfsfitness
* Budget voor professionele ontwikkeling
* Lunchkorting
* Pensioen
* Reiskostenvergoeding
* Vrijdagmiddagborrel

description:

---------------


Amazon is looking for an AI Content Expert II to help with annotations, content generation, and data analysis. As part of the Data Team, you will be responsible for delivering high\-quality training data to improve and expand Large Language Models' (LLMs) capabilities.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

* Creating and annotating high\-quality complex training data in multiple modalities (text, image, video) on various topics, including technical or science\-related content
* Writing grammatically correct texts in different styles with various degrees of creativity, stricly adhering to provided guidelines
* Performing audits and quality checks of tasks completed by other specialists, if required
* Making sound judgments and logical decisions when faced with ambiguous or incomplete information while performing tasks
* Diving deep into issues and implementing solutions independently
* Identifying and reporting tooling bugs and suggesting improvements

  

A day in the life  

As an AI Content Expert, you will be responsible for creating training data that are complex in nature and will require you to make informed and high judgement decisions in each case. You will be working closely with scientists and engineers to review and update guidelines, identify tooling improvement opportunities, and engage in conversations regarding the quality of data.  

  

This is a Fixed term contract.
  

  

About the team  

The team works strictly in the office Monday through Friday with an eight\-hour shift. We are constantly looking for ways to improve our capabilities and deliver the best product possible. Diverse team, regular meetings, trainings, and Amazon events throughout the year await you.**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* High\-School or equivalent diploma.
* Proven experience working with written language data, including experience with annotation, and other forms of data markup.
* Strong proficiency in Dutch and English. Candidate must demonstrate excellent writing, reading, and comprehension skills (C1 level in the Common European Framework CEFR scale).
* Strong understanding of Dutch and U.S.\-based culture, society, and norms.
* Strong research skills to gather relevant information, understand complex topics, and synthesize multiple resources; understanding of basic academic integrity, i.e. plagiarism.
* Excellent attention to details and ability to focus for a long period of time
* Comfortable with high\-school level STEM
* Ability to effectively write and evaluate diverse subject matter across various domains
* Ability to adapt writing style to suit various style guidelines and customers.
* Ability to adapt well to fast\-paced environments with changing circumstances, direction, and strategy

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**
----------------------------

* Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field or equivalent professional experience
* Experience with creating complex data for LLM training and evaluation
* Proven experience working with command line interfaces and basic UNIX commands
* Familiarity with common markup languages such as HTML, XML, Markdown
* Familiarity with common standard text formats such as JSON, CSV, RTF
* Working knowledge of Python or another scripting language

  

Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.  

  

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. title: Senior Cloud Engineer (Azure) – Enterprise Environment
description:
**Who we are**  

Since 1990, we’ve grown into a global leader in personalized medicine. Today, 5,000\+ colleagues in 34 countries work together to support over 200,000 customers worldwide.



From compounding and natural products to software, equipment, and genomics — we deliver smart, ready\-to\-use solutions that make healthcare better.



We’re passionate, science\-driven, and always looking for new ways to improve lives.



Explore your future at careers.fagron.com.

 **About the job**  

We are looking for a highly experienced Senior Cloud Engineer (Azure) to take end\-to\-end ownership of our global Azure platform, while actively enabling and supporting other teams in designing and implementing cloud solutions.



You operate in a global, enterprise\-scale environment, ensuring Azure is not only technically robust, but also secure, governed, cost\-efficient and automation\-driven. You act as a trusted advisor for product, application and platform teams, helping them adopt Azure in a compliant and scalable way.

  


**Key responsibilities**


**Azure platform \& architecture**: Design, build and operate a global Azure platform across multiple regions and subscriptions. Define and evolve enterprise\-grade Azure Landing Zones. Own subscription strategy, networking, RBAC and management group design. Drive standardization and automation using Infrastructure as Code (Bicep, Terraform, ARM)


**Cloud governance (ownership)**: Define and enforce governance frameworks, including policies, management groups, RBAC, naming standards, and tagging. Ensure security, compliance, and audit readiness. Embed controls into CI/CD pipelines.


**Cost management \& FinOps:** Lead Azure cost management practices, budgets, alerts, chargebacks, and optimization. Apply AI\-driven cost analytics and anomaly detection.


**Identity \& application integration:** Manage hybrid identity landscapes (Azure AD, Entra ID, ADFS, on\-prem AD). Support secure access patterns and implement Zero Trust principles.


**AI agents \& cloud automation:** Design and implement AI\-driven agents and intelligent automation using Azure OpenAI, Functions, Logic Apps and event\-driven architectures.


**Team collaboration:** Partner with product and DevOps teams, review architectures, and provide practical guidance on governance, security, and cost efficiency. Mentor teams across regions, drive alignment on platform standards, and act as a trusted advisor.

 **Set yourself apart**  

You possess the following qualifications, background and abilities:



 8\+ years in cloud/platform engineering with deep **Azure expertise**.



Hands\-on experience with:


* **Azure Governance** (Policy, RBAC, Management Groups)
* **Azure AD / Entra ID**, ADFS, on\-prem AD
* **Identity federation and application integration**
* **Azure Cost Management and FinOps**


Skilled in **Infrastructure as Code** (Bicep, Terraform, PowerShell), **CI/CD pipelines** (Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions), and event\-driven automation.



Strong stakeholder communication, advisory skills, and ability to guide teams without becoming a bottleneck.

 **What's on offer?**  

* Being part of an international team.
* Fixed\-term contract with the possibility and intention to extend.
* A market\-conform salary, all in line with your knowledge \& experience.
* Hybrid working with 3 days in the office.
* Annual bonus.
* 28 vacation days.
* Collective health insurance.
* Travel allowance of € 0\.23 per KM (with a max of 150KM per day) or 100% public transportation reimbursement.

  



At Fagron, you’ll find plenty of chances to grow — both personally and professionally. We work in an open, dynamic environment where your ideas and talents can shine.



We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer. Diversity makes us stronger, and we’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels welcome.

 **Ready for the challenge?**  

Take the next step and join us in shaping the future of personalized medicine. Apply today at careers.fagron.com.



 Please note: we do not accept agency referrals for this position.

title: Senior Data Engineer
description:
**Senior Data Engineer**


**About Us**
============



FUGA is a subsidiary of Downtown Music Holdings and provides forward\-thinking music distribution, technology, and marketing services to forward\-thinking music companies. Working at the heart of the music industry with more than 800 clients, including Riot Games, Epitaph, Naxos, and Beggars Group, FUGA is the world's leading full\-service B2B music distributor.



FUGA works at the forefront of music distribution technology, connecting its clients with digital service providers like Spotify, Apple Music, and Amazon Music while helping them get the most from their music, whether that's with award\-nominated label services, industry\-leading revenue accounting, or using FUGA's suite of best\-in\-class products.


**JOB DESCRIPTION**
===================



FUGA's Data Platform team is looking for a passionate, dedicated and motivated individual to work in our Amsterdam headquarters. In this role, you'll work with large\-scale music streaming analytics data and contribute to value\-added projects that directly support the business.



The Data Platform team plays a pivotal role in FUGA's data domain, managing and maintaining the DWH, building and maintaining critical data pipelines, and enabling all departments to make reliable, data\-driven decisions.



You'll play a key role in fostering a data\-driven culture by delivering reliable, scalable data engineering solutions. At FUGA, data drives decisions, optimises operations, and delivers value.


**WHAT YOU'LL DO**
==================


* Build, maintain, and own data pipelines with Python, GCP, BigQuery, Airflow, dbt, and Spark
* Design and improve scalable data models for analytics and reporting
* Ensure data quality through cleaning, validation, and transformation
* Monitor and improve pipelines for reliability, performance, and cost efficiency
* Develop components to interact with APIs, databases, and cloud services
* Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and stakeholders to deliver data solutions aligned with business goals
* Write clean, testable code integrated with CI/CD pipelines
* Take ownership of critical data workflows and platform improvements
* Contribute to knowledge\-sharing and best practices

**WHO YOU ARE**
===============


* Solid experience as a Senior Data Engineer working in GCP or AWS
* Strong experience building and maintaining production data pipelines
* Comfortable with SQL for large\-scale data processing
* Confident with Python for ETL and data workflows
* Solid understanding of data modelling for analytics use cases
* Experience with Airflow and containerisation using Docker
* Experience with Spark (PySpark) for large\-scale data processing
* You take ownership of your work and care about quality, reliability, and performance
* You write clean, maintainable code and pay attention to detail
* You collaborate effectively and contribute to a strong engineering culture
* You are proactive, curious, and continuously improving
* You see the benefit of documentation and proactively document systems, pipelines, and decisions

**WHAT SKILLS YOU NEED**
========================


* Degree in Computer Science, Data/Software Engineering, or equivalent experience
* Strong SQL skills, ideally with BigQuery or similar data warehouses
* Python for data processing and ETL
* Understanding of data warehousing concepts and structured/unstructured data

**NICE TO HAVES**
=================


* Experience or interest in the music or entertainment industry
* Familiarity with Linux environments
* Experience with GCP tools beyond BigQuery (e.g. Dataflow, Pub/Sub, Dataform)
* Experience with Kubernetes or container orchestration

title: Senior Data Analyst - Risk
description:
**This is Adyen**



Adyen provides payments, data, and financial products in a single solution for customers like Meta, Uber, H\&M, and Microsoft \- making us the financial technology platform of choice. At Adyen, everything we do is engineered for ambition.



For our teams, we create an environment with opportunities for our people to succeed, backed by the culture and support to ensure they are enabled to truly own their careers. We are motivated individuals who tackle unique technical challenges at scale and solve them as a team. Together, we deliver innovative and ethical solutions that help businesses achieve their ambitions faster.



Enterprise Risk is a function in Adyen's Second Line organization. The function manages a wide range of risk management topics ranging from Financial risk to Technology risk and from Strategic risk to Operational risk. The function has a key role in Adyen's risk management framework and has a strategic and advisory role by independently challenging, overseeing, and advising the rest of the company.


**Senior Data Analyst \- Risk**



We're looking for a proactive, technical, and thorough Data Analyst to join our Enterprise Risk team in Amsterdam. As part of the Enterprise Risk team within Adyen's Second Line, you will work alongside risk experts and data analysts to reimagine risk management through technology, embedding new functionalities that drive automation, optimization, and data\-driven decision\-making. Our mission is to empower Second Line (risk) teams with innovative solutions and actionable insights while building the foundation for an automated risk management landscape.



While financial and risk acumen are a big plus, strong technical skills in data transformation, data governance, and automation are very important for this role. What sets you apart for this role are strong technical skills in data transformation, data analytics, and stakeholder management.


**What you'll do**


* Build and scale; design the foundation of Enterprise Risk's data capabilities and identify growth areas that multiply the effectiveness of the team.
* Develop, optimize and maintain our BI infrastructure on Looker using SQL and Python.
* Create scalable solutions to risk management that ensure data accuracy, consistency, and accessibility.
* Drive the automation of manual risk assessment processes by identifying opportunities for optimization and implementing technical solutions to improve efficiency.
* Partner closely with the different teams in Enterprise Risk to understand their needs, leverage their expertise and capacity, and simplify complexity through smart, user\-focused solutions.
* Liaise with other Data teams in the Second Line and leverage their expertise to apply to Enterprise Risk's scope.
* Champion data governance and data quality by ensuring data integrity, security, and access control through best practices.


**Who you are**


* An experienced data professional with a proven track record of 5\+ years in a (technical) data analyst, business intelligence, or similar role.
* Proficient in Python, SQL, PySpark, and BI tools (e.g. Looker). Experience with big data tools like Spark, Hadoop, Git, and Airflow is a strong advantage.
* You enjoy working in a high\-paced, technology\-driven environment where you may need to shift priorities.
* You work with intention, a strong sense of ownership, and a bias for action.
* You are comfortable talking straight and challenging the status quo, specifically with the ability to interact and collaborate with a variety of internal stakeholders from diverse disciplines and cultures.
* Self\-motivated, proactive, and result driven, always searching for scalable ways to streamline and add value to the business with attention to detail and high quality results.
* You hold a track record of various initiatives and implementation of them.
* An excellent communicator and collaborator, with the ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear and actionable insights for both technical and non\-technical audiences.
* Experience working with risk management, financial data, and banking are a big plus


**Our Diversity, Equity and Inclusion commitments**



Our unique approach is a product of our diverse perspectives. This diversity of backgrounds and cultures is essential in helping us maintain our momentum. Our business and technical challenges are unique, and we need as many different voices as possible to join us in solving them \- voices like yours. No matter who you are or where you're from, we welcome you to be your true self at Adyen.



Studies show that women and members of underrepresented communities apply for jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. Does this sound like you? If so, Adyen encourages you to reconsider and apply. We look forward to your application!


**What's next?**



Ensuring a smooth and enjoyable candidate experience is critical for us. We aim to get back to you regarding your application within 5 business days. Our interview process tends to take about 4 weeks to complete, but may fluctuate depending on the role. Learn more about our hiring process here. Don't be afraid to let us know if you need more flexibility.



This role is based out of our Amsterdam office. We are an office\-first company and value in\-person collaboration; we do not offer remote\-only roles.

description:

* Hybrid working model.
* Must be Dutch speaking and open to travel.

Do you get energy from working with data and building smart solutions? Do you want to work on challenging projects where you can fully utilize your analytical and technical skills, without having to worry about commercial responsibilities? Then this Data Engineer opportunity may be a great fit.

You will work alongside a driven team on innovative data solutions that have a direct impact and deliver value to clients and partners. You also get the freedom to choose projects that match your interests, whether that is building new solutions or optimizing existing ones.

**Who are we?**

A dynamic and fast\-growing organization specializing in IT consultancy. With teams of experts in Cloud, Data, Security, and Software Development, the organization strives for excellence and customer satisfaction. There is a strong focus on supporting consultants and creating a people\-first work environment.

**Our culture**

* **We connect.**

People come first. Consultants receive personal coaching from the beginning, with attention to growth, support, and long\-term development. Collaboration and learning from colleagues are central to the culture.

* **We play.**

The organization values enjoying the work as much as delivering strong results. Team activities and social events are part of the culture, with a range of options to suit different interests.

* **We grow.**

Consultants have a strong voice in choosing the projects they work on. There is a substantial training budget and regular in\-house knowledge\-sharing sessions. Compensation is structured to reward consultants fairly, while commercial negotiations are handled centrally.

**What are you going to do?**

Imagine joining a team full of talent and ambition. Together with internal stakeholders, a challenging project is matched to your interests and strengths. This could involve designing a data architecture for a mobility platform, optimizing energy usage through smart data solutions, or building a real\-time analytics tool for a financial institution. Each project offers the chance to develop your skills further.

The work is organized in short, efficient sprints, both at client locations and on internal consultancy projects. You handle data processing and integration, and work closely with colleagues and partners to monitor progress. With your experience and expertise, you advise clients on how data can be used more intelligently and efficiently to improve business processes.

**What are you bringing?**

* A completed technical higher professional or university education
* At least five years of relevant work experience
* Fluent spoken and written Dutch at C1 level
* Strong expertise in programming and SQL, with experience in data platforms and cloud technologies
* Experience with ETL/ELT processes
* Expertise in big data solutions
* Familiarity with data modeling techniques such as Kimball
* Experience with CI/CD or DevOps principles and tooling
* A strong willingness to learn and share knowledge with colleagues and clients
* The ability to advise and guide others in designing and implementing scalable data processes
* Excellent communication skills and the ability to make and keep clear agreements

**It is a bonus if you are familiar with:**

* Dashboarding and BI tools
* Data workflow tools such as dbt
* Container technologies and managed container services
* Infrastructure\-as\-code tooling

**You like this:**

* **Data processing**

Working with data comes naturally to you. Whether you are setting up your own projects in your spare time or trying out new tools and techniques, you enjoy continuously learning and experimenting.

* **Problem solving**

You have a passion for solving complex data problems. Analyzing large datasets and devising creative, efficient solutions for technical challenges gives you energy.

**You do not like this:**

* **Monotonous routines**

You do not simply follow the beaten path. You enjoy trying new approaches and coming up with fresh ideas that make the work smarter and more efficient for yourself, your team, and the client.

* **Poor data infrastructure**

Poorly structured databases or outdated pipelines without clear documentation are frustrating to you. You quickly see where improvements are possible and address them thoroughly.

title: Senior Cost Management Lead
description:

Their commitment, collective expertise, and unique capabilities are the engine room behind SBM Offshore’s True. Blue. Transition. \- shaping the future of energy, and beyond.  

  

**About Us:**  

SBM Offshore is a global leader in deepwater ocean infrastructure, delivering floating production solutions across the full asset lifecycle—from design and construction to installation and operation. Supported by a global team of more than 8,000 professionals, the Company operates a long\-term, asset\-backed business model that delivers high\-availability assets and predictable cash flows. SBM Offshore combines engineering expertise, operational reliability, and selective innovation to support safe, efficient, and lower\-carbon energy production, while extending its capabilities into new opportunities across the blue economy.

In such cases the Lead Cost Engineer will report directly to the Project Control Manager and the Department Head only for the duration of their assignment on the project.
Responsibilities

Education

Experience

Functional Competencies
Cost Control and performance management execution
Budgeting and forecasting
Project budget set\-up
Project Cost Management
Contractual and commercial
Analytics and reporting
Cash Management
Invoice management

SBM Offshore N.V. is a listed holding company that is headquartered in Amsterdam. It holds direct and indirect interests in other companies that collectively with SBM Offshore N.V. form the SBM Offshore Group (“the Company”). For further information, please visit our website at www.sbmoffshore.com. The companies in which SBM Offshore N.V., directly and indirectly, owns investments are separate entities.

SBM Offshore is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. SBM Offshore does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, non\-disqualifying physical or mental disability, national origin, veteran status, or any other basis covered by appropriate law. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need.

title: Director, European Strategic Communications
description:
Anduril Industries is a defense technology company with a mission to transform U.S. and allied military capabilities with advanced technology. By bringing the expertise, technology, and business model of the 21st century's most innovative companies to the defense industry, Anduril is changing how military systems are designed, built and sold. Anduril's family of systems is powered by Lattice OS, an AI\-powered operating system that turns thousands of data streams into a realtime, 3D command and control center. As the world enters an era of strategic competition, Anduril is committed to bringing cutting\-edge autonomy, AI, computer vision, sensor fusion, and networking technology to the military in months, not years.

### **ABOUT THE TEAM**


Anduril's global communications team is responsible for shaping how the world understands modern defense technology—and why it matters. We're seeking a Director of Communications, Europe, to build and lead Anduril's external communications presence across the continent.  

  

This is a senior individual contributor role based in Amsterdam, reporting to the VP of Global Communications, with a dotted line to Anduril's European General Manager. You will be Anduril's first dedicated communications hire in Europe, responsible for standing up a regional program from scratch, building relationships with media and institutions, and developing the narratives that will define Anduril's reputation across the world's most consequential defense markets.  

  

This is a builder role. You'll set strategy and execute it yourself, establish agency and contractor networks as the business grows, and work shoulder\-to\-shoulder with business development, policy, and senior leadership to earn Anduril a place in Europe's defense conversation. The ideal candidate has deep roots across multiple European markets, understands how defense, technology, and industrial policy intersect at the national level, and knows how to build presence and credibility in environments where trust is earned slowly and reputation matters enormously.

### **WHAT YOU'LL DO**


* Build Anduril's European communications program from the ground up, developing strategy, establishing infrastructure, and executing across markets with varying levels of maturity and priority.
* Drive media relations across defense, technology, business, and policy outlets in priority European markets, building the relationships and pipelines that will sustain long\-term coverage.
* Develop and execute market\-specific narrative strategies that connect Anduril's mission and capabilities to each country's defense priorities, industrial ambitions, and security context.
* Work closely with Anduril's European General Manager and business development teams to support market entry, customer engagement, partnerships, and key events.
* Build and manage a network of regional agencies and contractors as European operations expand.
* Partner with global communications, policy, and marketing teams to adapt core messaging for European audiences without losing coherence or accuracy.
* Engage think tanks, research institutions, and policy communities to build credibility and shape the broader discourse around defense innovation and allied cooperation.
* Own metrics and reporting frameworks to track progress and demonstrate impact across markets.

#### **QUALIFICATIONS**


* 10\+ years of experience in communications, public affairs, or media relations, ideally with meaningful time spent in defense, national security, or dual\-use technology sectors.
* Fluency in the European defense and political landscape across multiple countries, how decisions get made, who shapes opinion, and where the conversation is heading.
* A track record of building communications programs in new markets, not just inheriting them.
* Experience managing agencies, contractors, and regional communications networks across borders.
* Exceptional writing and strategic thinking, with the ability to develop narratives that land across cultures and audiences.
* Comfortable working autonomously in an early\-stage market environment, with the judgment to make calls without a lot of process around you.

#### **You'll excel in this role if you:**


* Have built something before, a market presence, a regional program, a communications function, and know what that actually requires.
* Can hold complexity: different countries, different threat perceptions, different industrial interests, all requiring distinct approaches under a coherent strategy.
* Know Europe's defense ecosystem personally, the journalists, the institutions, the officials who shape how these conversations evolve.
* Are energized by the moment Europe is in, and want to help shape how one of the most important defense technology companies in the world shows up in it.


The salary range for this role is an estimate based on a wide range of compensation factors, inclusive of base salary only. Actual salary offer may vary based on (but not limited to) work experience, education and/or training, critical skills, and/or business considerations. Highly competitive equity grants are included in the majority of full time offers; and are considered part of Anduril's total compensation package. Additionally, Anduril offers top\-tier benefits for full\-time employees, including:


**Healthcare Benefits**


* **US Roles:** Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision plans at little to no cost to you.
* **UK \& AUS Roles:** We cover full cost of medical insurance premiums for you and your dependents.
* **IE Roles:** We offer an annual contribution toward your private health insurance for you and your dependents.


**Additional Benefits**


* **Income Protection**: Anduril covers life and disability insurance for all employees.
* **Generous time off**: Highly competitive PTO plans with a holiday hiatus in December. Caregiver \& Wellness Leave is available to care for family members, bond with a new baby, or address your own medical needs.
* **Family Planning \& Parenting Support:** Coverage for fertility treatments (e.g., IVF, preservation), adoption, and gestational carriers, along with resources to support you and your partner from planning to parenting.
* **Mental Health Resources:** Access free mental health resources 24/7, including therapy and life coaching. Additional work\-life services, such as legal and financial support, are also available.
* **Professional Development:** Annual reimbursement for professional development
* **Commuter Benefits:** Company\-funded commuter benefits based on your region.
* **Relocation Assistance:** Available depending on role eligibility.


**Retirement Savings Plan**


* **US Roles:** Traditional 401(k), Roth, and after\-tax (mega backdoor Roth) options.
* **UK \& IE Roles:** Pension plan with employer match.
* **AUS Roles:** Superannuation plan.


The recruiter assigned to this role can share more information about the specific compensation and benefit details associated with this role during the hiring process.

  



### **Protecting Yourself from Recruitment Scams**



Anduril is committed to maintaining the integrity of our Talent acquisition process and the security of our candidates. We've observed a rise in sophisticated phishing and fraudulent schemes where individuals impersonate Anduril representatives, luring job seekers with false interviews or job offers. These scammers often attempt to extract payment or sensitive personal information.



To ensure your safety and help you navigate your job search with confidence, please keep the following critical points in mind:


* **No Financial Requests:** Anduril will never solicit payment or demand personal financial details (such as banking information, credit card numbers, or social security numbers) at any stage of our hiring process. Our legitimate recruitment is entirely free for candidates.
* **Please always verify communications:**
	+ Direct from Anduril: If you receive an email from one of our recruiters, it will *only* come from an @anduril.com address.
	+ Via Agency Partner: If contacted by a recruiting agency for an Anduril role, their email will clearly identify their agency. If you suspect any suspicious activity, please verify the agency's authenticity by reaching out to contact@anduril.com.
* **Exercise Caution with Unsolicited Outreach:** If you receive any communication that appears suspicious, contains grammatical errors, or makes unusual requests, do not engage. Always confirm the sender's email domain is @anduril.com before providing any personal information or clicking on links.
* **What to Do If You Suspect Fraud:** Should you encounter any questionable or fraudulent outreach claiming to be from Anduril, please report it immediately to contact@anduril.com. Your proactive caution is invaluable in protecting your personal information and upholding the security and trustworthiness of our recruitment efforts.
### **Data Privacy**



To view Anduril's candidate data privacy policy, please visit https://anduril.com/applicant\-privacy\-notice/.

title: AI Engineer
description:
**About Code for Good** 




  





**At Code for Good, we’re building the best version of tomorrow.** 




 We connect data, people and AI technology to help manufacturing companies grow into a smarter future. Our team designs and develops AI\-powered modules within the Code for Good Platform, a system that brings together data, vision, and machine learning to create real\-world impact.
 



 Across manufacturing organizations, our modules support every department from production and quality control to finance and logistics, adding layers of intelligence that make work more efficient, decisions smarter, and operations more insightful. Based in the city center of Eindhoven, we work on projects that matter, with technology as the driving force.
 



  





**The Role** 




 The way software is built is changing fast. AI is no longer just a feature, it’s becoming the way we write code itself. We’re looking for an AI Engineer who understands this shift and wants to be at the center of it.
 



 You’ll join our AI Platform team and build applications using Next.js, with AI woven into everything you do. Python plays a key role too, especially when working alongside our Data Science team on model integration and data processing. No two projects look the same. One week you might be connecting a document extraction flow to an ERP, the next you’re building a computer vision pipeline using NVIDIA DeepStream or developing a real\-time classification system. What ties it all together: you’re someone who figures out how to turn an idea into a working product, fast.
 



 This is a hands\-on position in a growing team. You’ll have real ownership over what you build and the freedom to shape how we deliver AI\-powered solutions to our clients.
 



  





**You Will** 



* Build applications using Next.js that put AI to work in real business contexts
* Integrate with third\-party platforms, APIs, and external systems
* Work closely with our Data Science team: they build the models, you build the applications around them
* Think critically about new problems and move quickly from idea to working product



  





**Who You Are** 




 You’re a critical thinker who breaks down unfamiliar problems quickly, thinks in solutions, and doesn’t wait to be told what to do.
 



  





**Must haves** 



* 2 to 5 years of experience in software development
* Strong proficiency in TypeScript/JavaScript and experience with Next.js or React
* Working knowledge of Python
* Experience integrating APIs and third\-party services



  





**Nice to have** 



* Experience with AI services or LLM APIs
* Familiarity with Azure or another major cloud platform
* Frontend development experience



  





**You as a person** 



* Solution\-oriented and quick on your feet
* Clear communicator across technical and non\-technical teams
* Genuinely curious about AI and where it’s heading



  





**What We Offer** 



* Real ownership and freedom to shape our projects and technology
* An open culture where your ideas turn into action quickly
* Compensation between €3\.500 and €5\.500 per month, depending on experience
* Growth opportunities as we build and scale together
* A friendly work environment in the center of Eindhoven

title: Senior Engineer - AI Powered Resource Management Solutions
description:
**About Transformers Group** 




 Transformers Group is a Netherlands\-based startup building an AI\-powered Resource Management System for the aviation industry. We have developed a scalable, open\-architecture platform designed to serve multiple operational use cases. Our current focus is stand and gate planning, with expansion into check\-in counter allocation, bus planning, baggage handling, and additional modules planned from next quarter onward.
 



  





 We work at the forefront of technology, experimenting with the latest developments in Operations Research and AI to build solutions that outperform legacy incumbents. We are a challenger in an industry long overdue for innovation.
 



  





 We are hiring to accelerate the introduction of new modules.
 



  





**The Role** 




 As Senior Engineer, you will join a experienced engineering team to build out the platform foundation and develop new modules from the ground up. You will actively work with airports and airlines to understand their operations, and you will develop deep knowledge of the aviation industry. We believe in developers that understand the whole stack. Our stack consists of : Django, CP\-SAT (Google OR\-Tools), React, Redux Toolkit, Kubernetes. 
 



  





**Profile** 




 You have held a senior or lead engineering role and take full ownership of your work. You have an "I got this" mentality: you drive things forward independently and take responsibility for your technical decisions.
 



  





 You are genuinely curious and intrinsically motivated to explore new technologies. You are the kind of engineer who naturally stays ahead of the curve — not because it is required, but because it is who you are. At the same time, you know how to apply the right technology in a professional context without overcomplicating. You understand the balance between effort and value.
 



  





 You have a proven ability to automate workflows and continuously improve efficiency. We are an AI\-powered engineering company — not only in what we build, but in how we build. Automated testing, automated changelogs, automated user documentation, and AI\-assisted programming are standard practice in our team.
 



  





 Quality is non\-negotiable. All code undergoes peer review, supported by AI tooling but always validated by a colleague.
 



  





**Stack** 




 Django, CP\-SAT (Google OR\-Tools), React, Redux Toolkit, Kubernetes. We believe in owning the whole stack end to end to be as effective as you can in creating features. For this particular role, solid experience on the frontend (e.g. complex state management, realtime connections etc.) is a plus.
 



  





**Why Join Us?** 




 \- Competitive salary based on a 40\-hour workweek, in the range 5000\-7000 gross monthly.
 



 \- Macbook M4
 



 \- Hybrid work setup: 2 days in our Amersfoort office, other days remote.
 



 \- The opportunity to work with cutting\-edge technologies and push the boundaries of what's possible. Ride the latest wave of AI and experiment with new tools.
 



  





**Please note: We do not support Visa sponsorship or relocation at this phase.**
