title: Data Engineer
description:
**Help shape the future of food with AI**

**At Blue Radix, we build technology that tackles one of the biggest challenges out there: feeding a growing population with fewer resources. We are the global leader in autonomous greenhouse management, using advanced algorithms to help growers produce more with less.**

**Our in\-house platform acts as a digital brain for greenhouses worldwide. Built by a team of data, tech, and crop experts, it enables smarter, more sustainable food production at scale.**

**Working at Blue Radix means real impact. You take ownership, help shape our direction, and see your work directly applied in the real world. You’ll join a diverse, international team that combines deep expertise with a no\-nonsense, high\-performance mindset.**

**If you want to work on technology that matters and help solve a global problem with real\-world impact you’re in the right place.**

### **Your role:**

  

At Blue Radix we are looking for a Data Engineer to develop and extend our fast\-expanding data infrastructure.


In this role you are responsible for building and managing the infrastructure, new technical implementations, and components that will handle data and logic. You will support other development teams and data scientists with technical questions. Every day, you work in close cooperation with architects, data scientists, product and application developers to create the best solutions possible. You will create operational processes that run fully autonomously, always reliable, and scalable for global operations.

  

As a Data Engineer you are an important linking pin between the Product Development team and the development team. Your work includes:

* Collaborate closely with our business analysts and backend engineers to implement stable algorithms and ETL pipelines and develop data solutions based on known designs within the current data infrastructure
* Work in teams with end\-to\-end technical responsibility for AI\-driven products and their features
* Design, develop and extend data models for optimal data retrieval, storage, and business logic to meet business requirements
* Analyze large data sets to solve incidents, identify gaps and inconsistencies, and provide data insights
* Ensure that monitoring and solving of issues can be done in an efficient way by maintaining dashboards and logging

### **What are you bringing along?**


You have been working as a Data Engineer for at least three years. During these years, you have developed a solid skill set:

* A bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, or other related technical fields
* Programming of back\-end applications (Python) and a solid understanding of cloud data technologies, like data lakes, time\-series technologies, and APIs.
* You know how to work with Git and CI/CD, ensuring automated tests and code quality checks.
* Experience in designing and deploying high performance systems with reliable monitoring and logging practices, with tools like Datadog and Dagster .
* Extensive experience with cloud platforms like AWS or Azure. Azure experience is a strong plus.
* You are used to use AI\-assited coding tools like Claude, Cursor or CoPilot.
* You are a colleague with a positive attitude who enjoys working in a team. You can work independently, you can master new tooling quickly, you are proactive, and you have a pragmatic approach

### **Why work at Blue Radix?**


You will become part of a team of talented, skilled, and educated colleagues developing cutting\-edge solutions. You fit in perfectly if you are looking for:

* The opportunity to create a world that is future proof, whilst expanding your know\-how, fulfilling your ambitions, and evolving new initiatives. Develop yourself by joining high quality personal skills training, visit conferences or follow courses with your team.
* We enjoy to spend time together during lunch, drinks and events to get to know each other and have a good time. The diversity in our team provides a great source of amazing stories, experiences and insights.
* Always something exciting to work on with our broad range of activities ranging from energy optimisation to climate control algorithms using advanced technology.
* The chance to be part of an exciting journey in a growing company with an international customer base.
* When you work at Blue Radix, you contribute directly to our success. You are involved in the direction of the strategy; you are given professional freedom and you take your responsibility.
* A good balance between working at home and meeting colleagues at the office.
* A great location to work in the Van Nelle factory (World Heritage Location) with awesome coffee.

**Up for the challenge?**  

Send us your resume and a motivation letter, written by you, not ChatGPT. We love AI, but this time, we need a human!

title: Systems Analyst
description:
**About ElevenLabs**
--------------------


ElevenLabs is an AI research and product company transforming how we interact with technology.


We launched in January 2023 with the first human\-like AI voice model. Today, we serve millions of users and thousands of businesses \- from fast\-growing startups to large enterprises like Deutsche Telekom and Meta. Our investors are some of the world's most prominent, including Andreessen Horowitz, ICONIQ Growth and Sequoia. We've raised $781M in funding and our last valuation was $11B \- multiples of 11, always.  

  

We have expanded from voice into three main platforms:

* ElevenAgents enables businesses to deliver seamless and intelligent customer experiences, with the integrations, testing, monitoring, and reliability necessary to deploy voice and chat agents at scale.
* ElevenCreative empowers creators and marketers to generate and edit speech, music, image, and video across 70\+ languages.
* ElevenAPI gives developers access to our leading AI audio foundational models.


Everything we do is the result of the creativity and commitment of our team \- builders doing the best work of their lives. We are researchers, engineers, and operators. IOI medalists and ex\-founders. If you want to work hard and create lasting positive impact, we want to hear from you.

**How we work**
---------------

* **High\-velocity:** Rapid experimentation, lean autonomous teams, and minimal bureaucracy.
* **Impact not job titles:** We don’t have job titles. Instead, it’s about the impact you have. No task is above or beneath you.
* **AI first:** We use AI to move faster with higher\-quality results. We do this across the whole company—from engineering to growth to operations.
* **Excellence everywhere:** Everything we do should match the quality of our AI models.
* **Global team:** We prioritize your talent, not your location.

**What we offer**
-----------------

* **Innovative culture:** You’ll be part of a generational opportunity to define the trajectory of AI, surrounded by a team pushing the boundaries of what’s possible.
* **Growth paths:** Joining ElevenLabs means joining a dynamic team with countless opportunities to drive impact \- beyond your immediate role and responsibilities.
* **Learning \& development**: ElevenLabs proactively supports professional development through an annual discretionary stipend.
* **Social travel**: We also provide an annual discretionary stipend to meet up with colleagues each year, however you choose.
* **Annual company offsite:** Each year, we bring the entire team together in a new location \- past offsites have included Croatia and Italy.
* **Co\-working**: If you’re not located near one of our main hubs, we offer a monthly co\-working stipend.

**About the role**
------------------


We're looking for a RevOps Systems Analyst to help build a modern, AI\-powered revenue operations stack. This isn't a traditional Salesforce admin role. We're building a GTM tech stack where AI is a first\-class citizen, automations are genuinely intelligent, and our CRM is a living system that reflects how our business actually works.


You'll function as a Salesforce admin and backend developer in one. You'll own platform administration, build complex automations via Flows and Apex, and develop and maintain custom services and integrations, including Salesforce MCP servers and AI tooling that connect our CRM to the broader systems our team runs on. For larger engineering efforts, you'll work shoulder\-to\-shoulder with our engineering team, scoping and shipping together.


In this role, you will:

* Administer our Salesforce org: object model, data architecture, user configuration, and platform health.
* Build and maintain automations via Salesforce Flows, Apex, and custom services that power our GTM processes end to end.
* Develop and maintain Salesforce MCP servers and AI tooling that extend CRM functionality into AI\-powered workflows.
* Design and maintain SQL\-based data pipelines that feed dashboards and revenue reporting across the org.
* Translate ambiguous business problems into clean technical scope, from stakeholder conversation to implemented solution.

**Requirements**
----------------

* Salesforce administration experience with deep familiarity across the object model, Flows, data loading tools, and platform configuration.
* Strong Apex development skills: you can build, debug, and maintain custom logic, triggers, and integrations without leaning on a separate engineering team.
* SQL proficiency: you can decompose ambiguous questions into answerable queries and are comfortable with complex joins, aggregations, and pipeline logic.
* Systems mindset: you can hold an end\-to\-end process in your head, trace a field from its origin through every downstream dependency, and anticipate what breaks when something changes.
* Excited by modern tooling: you're drawn to building AI\-native systems and are curious about where Salesforce, MCP, and AI agents intersect.

**Bonus:**

* Familiarity with dbt or modern data transformation tooling.
* Experience with BI tools such as Sigma or Looker.

**Location**
------------


This role is remote and can be executed globally. If you prefer, you can work from our offices in Bangalore, Dublin, London, New York, San Francisco, Tokyo, and Warsaw.

title: Lead Data and Cloud Architect
description:
At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence. We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go. Join EY and help to build a better working world.

**Confidence in your impact today \| Freedom to build tomorrow**
----------------------------------------------------------------

  

We are looking for a Lead Data \& Cloud Architect who will play a pivotal role in strengthening our data engineering and AI‑ready capabilities. Your expertise will ensure continuity, architectural excellence, and the design of future‑proof solutions across complex enterprise environments.

**Your new job**
----------------

  

* Design modern cloud‑based data architectures across AWS or GCP (Azure optional).
* Develop robust data‑warehousing, data‑modelling and scalable platform designs.
* Define AI‑ready data platform components including ingestion, orchestration, storage and compute layers.
* Provide architectural leadership, oversight and governance across delivery teams.
* Translate business requirements into actionable and scalable architectural solutions.
* Engage senior stakeholders and influence strategic technical decisions.
* Drive architectural patterns, standards and best practices across teams.
* Coach engineers and junior architects, raising the technical bar of the wider team.

**Your new team \| About your colleagues and the office**
---------------------------------------------------------

  

Our Amsterdam office is located at the Zuidas: a bustling area full of companies. In our Cross Towers, we welcome up to 1,500 people every day. For a breathtaking view of the city, head to the 22nd floor. Collaboration takes on a new meaning in our Wavespace, a high‑tech environment that connects clients and colleagues virtually across the globe.

**Your skills and strengths \| About your unique contribution**
---------------------------------------------------------------

  

* 8\+ years of experience in data architecture, cloud engineering or analytics platform design.
* Expertise in AWS or GCP (Azure is a plus).
* Strong background in enterprise‑scale data‑warehousing and data modelling.
* Experience with lakehouse, streaming, and ELT/ETL architectures.
* Ability to influence both technical and non‑technical senior stakeholders.
* Comfortable working in ambiguity, solving problems without predefined paths.
* Hands‑on mindset while maintaining strategic architectural oversight.
* Proactive, resilient and energised by complex enterprise challenges.

**Your benefits \| About your perks and work enjoyment**
--------------------------------------------------------

* A competitive salary that reflects your role and responsibilities, depending on experience and qualifications. Plus, a solid pension plan for the future. In addition to your salary, you’ll receive holiday allowance and an annual bonus (based on performance and development).
* You’ll receive a 32\- 40 hours contract.
* A laptop and phone for both work and personal use, and a monthly internet allowance.
* 34 vacation days to recharge and unwind, plus the option to buy extra days.
* With the mobility budget, you decide how you travel. Choose a lease car, a discounted public transport subscription, or arrange transport easily via our app.
* At EY, we care about a healthy work\-life balance. Hybrid working makes this easier. Whether you're working from the office, at a client, or from home, we’ll make sure you have a well\-equipped home office.
* The opportunity to work up to 20 days per year from abroad.
* The option to swap 3 national holidays for non\-national ones.
* You’ll have the space to grow both personally and professionally. Whether you want to deepen your expertise, develop your personality, or improve your leadership style, we offer a broad range of trainings and courses.
* Your well\-being is our priority. We believe that when you feel good, you perform better. That’s why you’ll have access to the OpenUp wellbeing platform (including for your family). You can easily connect with psychologists and lifestyle experts within 36 hours and take part in wellbeing sessions.
* A strong focus on teaming and various internal networks where you can join all kinds of social activities. A positive work atmosphere and good collaboration with colleagues contribute to your overall happiness at work.
* All this within an environment where our core values of integrity, inclusiveness, and respect are central. We think it’s important that you can be yourself and fully develop into the best version of yourself. Curious about all our benefits? Click here.

**Ready to shape your future with us? About your application process**
----------------------------------------------------------------------

1\. For this vacancy, we only ask for your CV. This way, it’s quick and easy to apply.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

2\. First interview: Before the interview, we’ll ask you to complete an online assessment. Not a standard questionnaire, but a game\-based assessment focusing on both your cognitive skills and behavioral preferences. We’ll discuss the results during the interview, which will include a colleague from the relevant department. This mutual introduction is a chance for us to get to know you, and for you to ask any questions you have.
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

3\. Second interview: If we see a match and you’re still enthusiastic, we’ll invite you for a second interview. We’ll dive deeper into the role and other relevant topics. Sometimes, a business case is part of the process. A final interview may also follow.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

4\. We’re a match. You’ll receive an offer and we’ll perform a pre\-employment screening. Then your new adventure begins. Welcome to EY!
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  



**EY****\|****Building a better working world**
-----------------------------------------------

  



EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Enabled by data, AI and advanced technology, EY teams help clients shape the future with confidence and develop answers for the most pressing issues of today and tomorrow.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

EY teams work across a full spectrum of services in assurance, consulting, tax, strategy and transactions. Fueled by sector insights, a globally connected, multi\-disciplinary network and diverse ecosystem partners, EY teams can provide services in more than 150 countries and territories.
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

title: Senior AI/ML Engineer (Spectrum) New
description:
Software engineers and AI agents alike suffer from the same problem: finding that one person or place that will answer their tough, specific question. Many solutions promise to solve this with similarity search in vector databases. Unfortunately, finding the answer is often a puzzle with pieces to be collected across a myriad of contradictory sources and cannot be solved without surgical search and careful reasoning.

  



Spectrum collects data from an organization's code, docs, and issues, and organizes knowledge in a unified ontology that AI agents can efficiently search through and reason over. We aim to revolutionize the semantic layer space for software\-building organizations and move beyond specs that fall out of sync with code, introducing a living spec – one that's extracted from the whole system and used to keep it aligned. Spectrum is meant to be the single source of truth for all product and architectural knowledge.

  



A resident of JetBrains' startup incubator, Spectrum enjoys startup speed and autonomy, and is backed by 25 years of developer tooling expertise. We are looking for a Senior AI/ML Engineer to build and evolve the ML\-powered systems at the heart of our product.


**Your responsibilities will include:**
=======================================


* Designing and building the ML/LLM solution for data ingestion, knowledge extraction and retrieval, and subsequent reasoning.
* Creating datasets, metrics, and pipelines that drive measurable improvements across the system.
* Architecting and improving AI agents, which will involve prompt engineering, model selection, and inference optimization.
* Contributing to our MLOps practices, including orchestration, observability, and experiment tracking.

**We expect you to have:**
==========================


* At least four years of experience building ML/AI systems in production environments, with hands\-on LLM experience.
* A deep understanding of the LLM ecosystem, including model architectures and fine\-tuning approaches.
* Hands\-on experience with:
* + Prompt engineering and LLM pipeline design, including evaluation.
	+ Agentic frameworks such as LangGraph, Pydantic AI, or an equivalent.
	+ Retrieval\-augmented generation (RAG) patterns and vector databases.
	+ Deploying and scaling LLM\-powered applications using APIs (e.g. OpenAI or Anthropic) or open\-source models.
* Strong Python skills – Kotlin knowledge would be a plus.
* Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to diverse audiences.
* Proficiency in English, both written and verbal.

**Our ideal candidate would also have:**
========================================


* Experience with ontologies, knowledge graphs, or graph\-based reasoning.
* Experience in early\-stage startups – you enjoy the zero\-to\-one phase.
* A background in code analysis, developer tools, or software engineering research.
* Experience with multi\-agent systems or complex agentic workflows.
* Actively contributed to relevant open\-source projects or publications.

**What we offer:**
==================


* A competitive salary and JetBrains benefits.
* A generous runway and corporate resources with startup autonomy.
* A motivated team that moves fast, with short feedback loops, rapid prototyping, and lots of ownership.
* Opportunities to publish research at top venues in collaboration with JetBrains Research.


\#LI\-DNI

**We are an equal opportunity employer**  

  

We know great ideas can come from anyone, anywhere. That’s why we do our best to create an open and inclusive workplace – one that welcomes everyone regardless of their background, identity, religion, age, accessibility needs, or orientation.


*We process the data provided in your job application in accordance with the* *Recruitment Privacy Policy.*

description:

**About Us:** At Booking.com, data drives our decisions. Technology is at our core. And innovation is everywhere. But our company is more than datasets, lines of code or A/B tests. We’re the thrill of the first night in a new place. The excitement of the next morning. The friends you encounter. The journeys you take. The sights you see. And the memories you make. Through our products, partners and people, we make it easier for everyone to experience the world.

 **About the team:** The Digital Media Analytics team in Growth Marketing unites analysts, data scientists, and engineers to measure, report, and optimize paid media across channels like YouTube, Facebook, and Criteo using an incrementality\-first approach with holdout/control tests. They translate causal results into automated pipelines and clear dashboards for daily decisions, and run an experimentation program that standardizes hypotheses, KPIs, power/duration, and effort scoring for transparent planning and robust readouts.

 **Role description:** As a Senior Data Scientist in the Digital Media Analytics Team, you focus on scalable solutions and frameworks to the company’s most persistent challenges in decision making. You provide support to experimenters and business leaders across the company, investigate problems and define clear solutions and requirements for their implementation. You will build proofs of concept and prototypes, and work closely with the engineering teams to support our product vision. The ability to write high quality, reproducible code, dig into technical product details for feasible and robust solutions is crucial to make your work impactfully landed in the Experimentation Tool, and enable solid decision making adoptable by a wide range of experimenters and leadership!

  

Senior Data Scientist defines the strategy and vision in the area, driving them from ideation to full productionisation. They autonomously prioritize and solve impactful strategic business problems using suitable data analysis and modeling methods. Senior Data Scientists handle key projects within their team, delivering work that influences business decisions across multiple products. They design and interpret quantitative experiments, driving long\-term strategic business decisions. They independently manage a range of stakeholders, including senior managers and director roles, and maintain effective communication. As subject matter experts in a specific area of data science, they play a vital role as a go\-to authority for senior business stakeholders, influence business decisions within their wider area, and contribute significantly to the community through coaching, mentoring, and community projects.

 **Key Job Responsibilities and Duties**

* Responsible for defining the strategy and vision for generating impact in the department by leveraging data science solutions and driving them from ideation to prototyping to full productionisation.
* Responsible for proactively identifying and autonomously prioritizing impactful strategic business problems, translating them into data stories and solving them effectively by applying suitable statistical data analysis and modeling methods. Leverages input from product stakeholders in all of the above to determine the right technical solutions to deliver customer value. May seek guidance from the manager on complex business problems.
* Responsible for designing and interpreting quantitative experiments to objectively drive long\-term strategic business decision making.
* Responsible for autonomously identifying and managing a broad range of stakeholders, from operational team members to directors . Maintains effective communication, addresses stakeholder needs, and conveys complex analysis results clearly. Continues to guide less experienced colleagues in all of the above, but may seek guidance from the manager in complex situations with senior stakeholders.
* Responsible for exploring and modifying existing internal and external Data Science approaches. Makes these solutions reusable by designing for easy adoption and proactively educating and upskilling peers on how to effectively utilize these solutions in their work.
* Responsible for incorporating scalability, reproducibility and long term orientation in the work they do. Regularly sharing best practices with peers in their area.
* Responsible for acting as a force multiplier for peers in their area by actively helping them with their craft through means of coaching, mentoring and setting a good example.
* Responsible for contributing significantly to the community of their area by thinking of and implementing ways to make other data professionals more effective (ie. group trainings, community projects), actively participating in community strengthening activities (ie. recruitment) and connecting their area to the rest of the business.
* Responsible for proactively collaborating with peers in related crafts (ie. Data Engineering) on all projects owned by their team, anticipating data needs to maintain smooth execution of projects.
* Responsible for having significant deep knowledge about the operational, tactical and strategic goals of their area (ie. track / vertical) and be knowledgeable of the goals of their wider area (ie. Business Unit).

**Role qualifications and requirements**

* A minimum of 8 years of professional experience as a Data Scientist, preferably in software, product or e\-commerce environments
* Fluent in Pyspark and general Python
* Strong working knowledge of statistical and hypothesis testing
* Strong commercial awareness with an ability to understand the business implications of a certain solution while being able to communicate that to stakeholders from various levels
* Experience working closely with product teams (not just PMs, but directly with developers/engineers)
* Willingness to dig deep into problems and behind the scenes data to support troubleshooting/bug fixing
* Solution oriented and result driven, strong bias for actions instead of perfect solutions and willingness to work on the most pressing challenges even when they don’t sound advanced enough.
* Able to brainstorm, peer review and drive research topics benefit Experimentation and Decision Making in both short and long term
* Excellent English communication skills, both written and verbal; the ability to convey your message to team members and other stakeholders, clearly and gently.
* Achieve impacts as a team player, and maintain solid relationships with technical and non technical stakeholders.
* Understand the complexity of experimentation from both craft and product perspectives, and how to effectively drive scalable solutions for continuous improvements.


The following skills are assets for this role:

* Knowledgeable about statistical properties of (good) metrics, and ability to scalably test them
* Causal inference knowledge beyond A/B testing
* Understand the gist of scripts written in R
* Experience building libraries/modules in python
* Familiarity with apis using is good, building/maintaining is great
* Good understanding of fundamental machine learning concepts
* Strong knowledge of reporting/visualization techniques (e.g., Tableau, Graphite, Power BI, etc.)
* The senior might be required at times to provide reporting and dashboarding

**Benefits \& Perks \- Global Impact, Personal Relevance:**

  

Booking.com’s Total Rewards Philosophy is not only about compensation but also about benefits. We offer a competitive compensation and benefits package, as well unique\-to\-Booking.com benefits which include:

* Annual paid time off and generous paid leave scheme including: parent, grandparent, bereavement, and care leave
* Hybrid working including flexible working arrangements, and up to 20 days per year working from abroad (home country)

title: Postdoctoral Researcher - Proteomics
description:
As a pioneer in proteomics, the BioMS group is a key collaborative partner for many. We seek an experienced researcher who will promote the group, execute and manage a diversity of projects in collaboration with our external partners, and act as an ambassador towards the mass spectrometry community on our behalf. Join an expert team performing cutting\-edge research, ranging from technological development and fundamental research to translational research with a direct therapeutical impact.

**Your job**
------------


The primary objective of this position is to perform experimental proteomics work, including sample preparation and data analysis, and to establish and manage a service infrastructure within the BioMS group, enabling fee\-for\-service access for external collaborators. You will independently design, plan and perform full projects, followed by the analysis, interpretation and reporting of data and results. You will pro\-actively promote proteomics services and acquire new collaborative projects.


The projects will vary in duration, workload, topic and methodology, depending on the request of the collaborator. They will be carried out with both academic and industry partners. You will independently plan and perform projects in a broad area of quantitative proteomics, including plasma proteomics, glycoproteomics and PTM analysis. You will work in a collaborative environment within the BioMS group, liaising with the PIs about the incoming projects and making use of the group’s expertise and technology to execute the projects effectively.


While many of your projects will be initiated by collaborators, there is space and need to take the lead in shaping their direction and to add your own ideas and creativity to each project. You will also assist collaborators with data analysis and structuring results into a paper or other type of output.


In addition to executing the projects, you will maintain the relationships with collaborators and manage the regulatory (IP rights, data safety etc.) and financial aspects of the project. You will collaborate with support staff in the group and within the department, including the Research Support Office (RSO) and financial and legal controllers, to ensure successful execution of projects.

**Your qualities**
------------------

* A PhD in quantitative mass spectrometry\-based proteomics and/or structural proteomics.
* Experience in operating and handling a diversity of mass spectrometers independently.
* Strong social skills and communication skills in English.
* Postdoctoral experience in mass spectrometry–based proteomics and/or experience working in an industrial environment is a major advantage.
* Keen interest in instrumentation and fundamentals of mass spectrometry, as well as skills in data analysis, statistics, and/or visualization is an advantage.

**Our offer**
-------------

* a position for one year, with the possibility of an extension for one year upon a positive evaluation;
* a gross monthly salary, depending on qualifications and experience, between € 3\.546 and €6\.433 in the case of full\-time employment (salary scale 10,11 under the Collective Labour Agreement for Dutch Universities (CAO NU));
* 8% holiday pay and 8\.3% year\-end bonus;
* a pension scheme, partially paid parental leave and flexible terms of employment based on the CAO NU.


In addition to the terms of employment laid down in the CAO NU, Utrecht University also offers a range of its own schemes for employees. This includes arrangements for professional development, various types of leave, and options for sports and cultural activities. You can also tailor your employment conditions through our Terms of Employment Options Model. In this way, we encourage you to keep investing in your personal and professional development. For more information, please visit Working at Utrecht University.

**About us**
------------


A better future for everyone. This ambition motivates our scientists in executing their leading research and inspiring teaching. At Utrecht University, the various disciplines collaborate intensively towards major strategic themes. Our focus is on Dynamics of Youth, Institutions for Open Societies, Life Sciences and Pathways to Sustainability. Sharing science, shaping tomorrow.


Working at the Faculty of Science means bringing together inspiring people across disciplines and with a variety of perspectives and backgrounds. The Faculty has six departments: Biology, Pharmaceutical Sciences, Information \& Computing Sciences, Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. Together, we work on excellent research and inspiring education. We do so, driven by curiosity and supported by outstanding infrastructure. Visit us on LinkedIn and discover how you can become part of our community.


The Utrecht Institute for Pharmaceutical Sciences (UIPS) is the research institute of the department of Pharmaceutical Sciences of Utrecht University. UIPS focuses on processes around discovery, development, and use of drugs and medical nutrition using molecular and technological principles from the Natural and Life Sciences. This research is well positioned in the Faculty of Science to work on basic scientific problems and to translate new findings into potential solutions to urgent societal medical needs, which are addressed in the Faculties of Medicine and Veterinary Medicine. Every year around 400 peer\-reviewed articles are published and around 30 PhD candidates defend their thesis. UIPS is partner in several open\-source innovation networks.


You will be embedded in the division of Biomolecular Mass Spectrometry and Proteomics. The research in this division focuses on the use of mass spectrometry based enabling technologies to deeply understand the inner workings of cells at the molecular level. Mass spectrometry enables the characterization of molecules that are present in cells and allows thereby the identification, quantification and characterization of proteins and other biomolecules that work together and are involved in all cellular processes. The group houses an excellent infrastructure that includes more than a dozen state\-of\-the\-art mass spectrometers, combined with cell culture facilities and access to microscopes etc. The group is world\-renown for mass spectrometry\-based proteomics and structural biology, and applies these technologies to study amongst others cancer, auto\-immune disorders, immunology, infections, and infectious diseases. Additionally, the group is on the forefront of developing new mass spectrometry based technologies in chemical proteomics, structural biology and the analysis of protein post\-translational modifications.

**More information**
--------------------


For more information, please contact Celine Miedema (Research Project Manager) att.c.miedema@uu.nl.


Do you have a question about the application procedure? Please send an email to science.recruitment@uu.nl.

**Apply now**
-------------


As Utrecht University, we want to be a home for everyone. We value staff with diverse backgrounds, perspectives and identities, including cultural, religious or ethnic background, gender, sexual orientation, disability or age. We strive to create a safe and inclusive environment in which everyone can flourish and contribute.


Knowledge security screening can be part of the selection procedures of academic staff. We do this, among other things, to prevent the unwanted transfer of sensitive knowledge and technology.


If you are enthusiastic about this position, just apply via the "Apply now" button! Please enclose:

* your letter of motivation;
* your Curriculum vitae;
* minimum of 2 references (include name, phone number and email address);
* a copy of your PhD certificate.

  

If this specific opportunity isn’t for you, but you know someone else who may be interested, please forward this vacancy to them.


Some connections are fundamental – Be one of them  

\#FundamentalConnection

title: Security Systems & Reporting Analyst
description:
Led by an experienced management team and supported by a strong investor group, including large and experienced institutions and strategic partners, EdgeConneX offers a dynamic, fast\-paced work environment where we are bringing flexibility, proximity, power, and connectivity to some of the world's key businesses. With major offices in Herndon, Denver, and Amsterdam, we have a global footprint and a unified team of employees committed to providing a premier customer experience and delivering the full spectrum of data center solutions, from core to edge, like no other data center provider can do.



Focused on driving innovation and helping our customers define and deliver their own unique vision for the Edge, at any scale, in any market worldwide, for any requirement, we are building tomorrow's data center infrastructure, today for some of the world's most demanding Network, Content, and Cloud customers.


Location: This role can be based in Amsterdam, Dublin or Warsaw.



The **Security Systems \& Reporting Analyst** plays a key role in supporting EdgeConneX's global security operations, with responsibility for analyzing and interpreting security\-related data across facilities, systems, and regions. The role supports the protection of people, physical assets, and information through accurate reporting, data integrity, and effective coordination. Through disciplined execution and attention to detail, the role contributes to maintaining a secure, reliable, and consistent operating environment for customers, employees, and partners worldwide.


**Primary Responsibilities**


* Analyze security\-related data across global data center operations to identify trends, gaps, and areas for improvement.
* Develop and maintain security reporting, dashboards, and metrics to support operational decisions and leadership visibility.
* Ensure the accuracy, integrity, and consistency of security data through regular validation, reconciliation, and quality checks.
* Act as a key coordination point between Global Security, regional teams, operations, IT, and other internal stakeholders.
* Assist with internal and external security audits, including evidence collection, reporting, and follow\-up actions.
* Partner with security integrators and vendors to support system performance, reporting, and continuous improvement.
* Contribute to maintaining a secure, professional, and consistent security experience for customers, employees, and partners, while upholding EdgeConneX's commitment to excellence.


**Knowledge, Skills \& Abilities**


* Proven ability to design, build, and maintain security dashboards and reports using data visualization tools, with Power BI strongly preferred.
* Advanced Excel skills, including pivot tables, lookups, data validation, and handling large datasets.
* Working experience writing and using SQL to query, join, and validate data from multiple sources.
* Experience working with structured databases and understanding how data is stored, maintained, and extracted for reporting.
* Hands\-on experience administering or supporting physical security systems such as LenelS2 and Genetec.
* Ability to manage and administer security data and system records, including access control data, site information, and user permissions.
* Experience coordinating or supporting projects, including tracking actions, dependencies, and deliverables across teams.
* Ability to reconcile data across systems and regions, identify discrepancies, and drive resolution with regional stakeholders.
* Comfortable operating independently in a global environment, working across time zones with limited supervision.
* High attention to detail, with a strong focus on data accuracy, consistency, and quality.


**Experience/Education**


* 3\+ years' experience in a security systems, reporting, data analyst, or similar role.
* Demonstrated experience building dashboards and recurring reports using Power BI or comparable tools.
* Strong Excel experience supporting operational or security reporting.
* Practical SQL experience querying operational or security datasets.
* Experience supporting or administering S2 access control systems.
* Experience supporting global or multi\-site security operations.
* Experience supporting audits, customer reviews, or compliance activities through data and system evidence.
* Experience in data centers, critical infrastructure, or controlled environments strongly preferred.

Grow your career in an international Finance team that values diversity, expertise, and development. Working with us means joining an organization that spans across borders, providing access to a wide range of finance functions, while offering opportunities for professional growth and continuous learning.


The Cost Accounting Analyst is responsible for reliable setup and maintenance of a dataset over 9,500 active materials across two legal entities. You’ll play a key role in improving costing models, supporting product launches, and shaping financial performance through data\-driven analysis and continuous optimization. You will make a valuable contribution to the Supply Chain “Control Tower” function VLOG has within AbbVie.  

  

You will be part of a collaborative team of 3 analysts working with the Finance Lead Direct Cost. You will support the Finance Lead and/or the Controller directly and indirectly by making a substantial contribution to the VLOG Finance business plan and strategic initiatives. You are aware of and work within the legal and company regulations and actively contribute to the governance processes and culture.

**Responsibilities**

* Primary contact for master data set\-up and costing both ad\-hoc and in annual cycles.
* Partner with global teams to support product launches, ensuring end\-to\-end financial readiness.

* Optimize and/or improve financial systems/models and costing, identifying trends, risks, and opportunities including Internal Control Framework.
* Act as a key finance partner to Supply Chain, providing insights on cost drivers, shipments, and spend.
* Drive process improvements and automation, leveraging tools like Power BI, Excel, and SAP.
* Contribute to global projects and system implementations, helping shape the future of data\-driven finance.
* Support analysis of purchasing activities across Third Party and Internal Manufacturing or Supply Chain.

  

**Qualifications** **Job Qualifications regarding education and work experience**

* Master or Bachelor in business economics, accountancy (CPA/CIMA) or Supply Chain.

* Experience with Excel and data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI).

 As a Product Support Engineer focused on autonomous driving, you'll sit at the intersection of field operations and engineering: reviewing real\-world issues, digging into logs and code, and driving fixes that improve the experience for customers and service technicians alike. You won't just report problems; you'll root\-cause them, propose solutions, and implement improvements yourself.


The ideal candidate is a hands\-on engineer with strong analytical instincts, someone who checks the technical details, gets into the code, and earns the trust of development teams through the quality of their work.

 **What You'll Do*** Review and triage field cases: analyze vehicle logs and diagnostic data to root\-cause issues across hardware and software boundaries
* Own the diagnostic strategy for autonomous driving features in Europe: define what needs to be detected, design the diagnostic approach, and implement it in production code (alerts, service routines, tooling).
* Analyze fleet metrics to identify trends, edge cases, and areas for improvement
* Support vehicle\-level testing and validation of fixes and new features
* Present insights and recommendations to leadership and cross\-functional teams
* Act as the technical bridge between Customer Support and development teams focused on autonomous driving, advocating for service and field needs with engineering leadership
  

**What You'll Bring*** Strong engineering fundamentals with the ability to reason across hardware and software boundaries, comfortable with Linux, and communications over CAN and Ethernet
* Software development skills in C\+\+, Python, and/or JavaScript, able to review, debug, and contribute to production codebases, not just write scripts
* Understanding of driver assistance and autonomous driving systems (perception, vehicle controls)
* Data analysis skills (SQL, data visualization, statistical reasoning)
* Clear written and verbal communication in English. Able to distill complex technical findings into actionable recommendations for engineering, service, and leadership audiences
* A collaborative but independent working style, comfortable digging into problems autonomously, questioning assumptions, and coordinating across hardware, software, field service, and customer support teams


Nice to have:

* Experience with camera\-based perception systems
* Experience with fleet\-scale data analysis


Application process:


Along with your CV, include 3 "Evidence of Excellence" items. Each should describe a specific technical challenge you tackled and the measurable outcome it produced. This is your chance to show us the work that defines you as an engineer \- we weigh these heavily in our review.

  

Format:


"At \[company], while working on \[short product description], did XYZ, resulting in \[product impact]."  

Keep each item to 2\-3 sentences. Be detailed but accessible, avoiding company\-specific acronyms or jargon that wouldn't be clear to an outside reviewer.

  

Examples:


The examples below are from other engineering roles to illustrate the format and level of detail we're looking for \- your evidence should reflect your own experience and doesn't need to relate to this role specifically.

* At \[company], while working on a LiDAR labeling tool, owned the backend MVP and designed the pipeline for generating object tracks in driving scenes, resulting in an MVP that was 10 times faster than the equivalent tool offered by an external vendor and a pipeline design that led to 2 patents.
* At \[company], while working on the navigation platform, designed and delivered a routing engine to serve alternate routes. As Tech Lead and sole developer for most of the project, analyzed the existing implementation, identified bottlenecks, and improved performance by 10x. This allowed the company to move away from a third\-party provider, saving several million USD per year.
* At \[company], while working on a data visualization service, identified a missing caching layer \- raw images were being re\-converted on every request. Designed and built a custom caching mechanism, resulting in a 50% latency improvement on cache hits.

title: SEO / AIO Lead
description:
### **Description**

 ***About the team:****We’re a passionate bunch on a mission to make Vintage Cash Cow and ArcaVindi, the go\-to place for giving pre\-loved items a new lease of life. As we scale rapidly across the UK and Europe, AI\-optimised discovery (AIO) and SEO are becoming increasingly critical growth levers, helping customers find us earlier, more often, and with higher intent.* *Organic search and AI\-driven discovery at Vintage Cash Cow are built on a clear underlying foundation of demand, with significant untapped potential. This role exists to unlock the next phase of growth by turning underperforming non\-brand organic demand, content, and technical foundations into a scalable, high\-impact acquisition engine.* ***About the role:***
  

*As our* ***SEO / AIO Lead****, you will own the hands\-on execution and strategic evolution of SEO and AI\-optimised discovery across Vintage Cash Cow and ArcaVindi. Reporting into the Growth Director, you will drive technical SEO, AIO optimisation, prioritisation, and content strategy, working closely with Product, Tech, Data, and Content to deliver measurable, incremental growth.*  


  

*This is a foundational build role. We are looking for someone who has launched or rebooted SEO in environments without mature legacy processes, and is comfortable creating structure where none exists. You will define standards, build documentation, establish reporting frameworks, and embed SEO best practice into product and content workflows from the ground up.*  


  

*This role is designed for a self\-sufficient operator who thrives in ambiguity and operates end\-to\-end: identifying opportunity, setting direction, building process, and translating strategy into action at pace. You will have clear ownership, autonomy, and accountability for SEO / AIO performance, with the mandate to influence roadmaps and priorities across teams.*  


  

*Your work will directly impact non\-brand traffic, acquisition efficiency, conversion quality, and long\-term growth. As the business continues to scale, this role has significant opportunity to expand in scope and influence.*  


  

*This is a rare opportunity to build and scale the SEO / AIO engine within a high\-growth consumer brand, not simply optimise it.*  


  

**First phase focus:**  

* Audit current SEO and AIO performance across technical foundations, content, and non\-brand demand
* Identify priority opportunities where Vintage Cash Cow already ranks but underperforms on clicks, visibility, or AI surfaces
* Establish a clear **SEO / AIO prioritisation framework** aligned to business impact
* Partner closely with Product and Tech through major platform and CMS changes to ensure SEO best practice is embedded

Define clear success metrics across traffic, rankings, CTR, and downstream performance  
* 

**Driving Excellence in SEO \& AIO**  

This role is accountable for elevating SEO and AI\-optimised discovery into a high\-impact, scalable growth channel for Vintage Cash Cow.
  

The SEO / AIO Lead will focus on:* Building a clear, prioritised SEO / AIO roadmap aligned to commercial impact
* Ensuring technical and content foundations are robust, scalable, and future\-proof
* Embedding SEO and AIO best practice into product, platform, and content workflows
* Adapting our organic acquisition strategy as search and AI\-driven discovery evolve
* Driving continuous improvement through testing, learning, and performance insight

Success in this role is measured not just by rankings, but by incremental non\-brand growth, acquisition efficiency, and contribution to wider business objectives. **Key Goals \& Objectives:*** Drive measurable growth in non\-brand organic traffic, leads and AI\-driven discovery
* Improve visibility, CTR, and performance of existing keyword and content foundations
* Ensure SEO best practice is embedded into product, platform, and content workflows
* Establish SEO / AIO as a core, scalable lead and revenue growth lever at Vintage Cash Cow
* Support incremental revenue and acquisition efficiency through scalable organic growth

### **Key Responsibilities**

 **SEO / AIO Strategy \& Prioritisation*** Own the SEO / AIO strategy and roadmap across the UK and international markets, plus Multi\-language technical setup, aligned to growth goals and customer needs.
* Build and maintain a clear prioritisation framework that balances technical foundations, content opportunities, and commercial impact.
* Identify high\-impact opportunities across non\-brand demand (rankings, CTR, content gaps) and translate them into an executable plan.
* Define success metrics and ensure stakeholders are aligned on what “good” looks like (traffic, visibility, rankings, CTR, conversions, and incremental value).

  

**Technical SEO \& Platform Partnership*** Lead technical SEO delivery across crawlability, indexation, internal linking, site architecture, redirects, structured data/schema, metadata, and page performance fundamentals.
* Partner closely with Product and Engineering to embed SEO requirements into releases, QA, and platform decisions.
* Support and guide SEO best practice during platform changes (including migrations), ensuring risks are managed and improvements are shipped.
* Maintain technical hygiene through audits, monitoring, and issue triage (e.g. index bloat, duplication, thin content, canonicalisation).

  

**Content Strategy, Optimisation \& E\-E\-A\-T*** Partner with Content to develop a scalable SEO / AIO content strategy (pillars, clusters, templates) mapped to user intent and business priorities.
* Optimise existing and new content for performance: including on\-page structure (H1–H3\), internal linking, metadata, entity coverage, FAQs, and AI\-readiness.
* Help establish consistent expert\-led signals (author profiles, expert attribution, review processes) to strengthen trust and E\-E\-A\-T across priority topics.
* Work with stakeholders to ensure content is accurate, brand\-aligned, and built for long\-term compounding organic growth.

  

**AIO Optimisation \& AI Discovery*** Ensure content and technical foundations are optimised for emerging AI discovery surfaces (LLMs, AI summaries/answers, rich results, Answer Engine Optimisation) alongside traditional SEO.
* Track shifts in search and AI behaviour, identify risks and opportunities, and translate insights into practical changes across content and technical foundations.
* Champion best practices for AIO across the business, ensuring we evolve with the landscape.

  

**Performance Measurement \& Continuous Improvement*** Establish reporting and monitoring for SEO / AIO performance, surfacing opportunities, risks, and wins clearly and regularly.
* Build a testing and optimisation cadence to improve rankings, CTR, engagement, and conversion quality over time.
* Quantify incremental impact where possible (especially non\-brand growth), and communicate progress in a stakeholder\-friendly way.
* Continuously improve ways of working (templates, checklists, workflows, QA) to scale output without compromising quality.

  

**Cross\-functional Leadership \& Ways of Working*** Collaborate with Product, Engineering, Data, CRM, Brand, and Performance Marketing to ensure SEO / AIO is integrated into the broader growth system.
* Act as the internal subject\-matter expert for SEO / AIO, raising standards and capability across teams through guidance and best practice.
* Establish lightweight governance (standards, documentation, QA checks) so SEO / AIO execution is scalable, consistent, and reliable.

  

### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise**

  

Strong hands\-on SEO experience (typically 5–10\+ years), including experience building or rebooting SEO within a high\-growth consumer or marketplace environment.  

* This role is ideal for someone who:
	+ Has built SEO foundations from scratch or successfully reset underperforming channels
	+ Enjoys operating independently and thrives in ambiguity
	+ Is commercially focused — thinking beyond rankings to revenue, conversion, and LTV
	
	Wants ownership over performance outcomes, not just execution  
	+

Key capabilities:* Proven ability to independently own technical SEO strategy, prioritisation, and delivery
* Deep expertise across on\-page, technical SEO, and content optimisation
* Strong understanding of emerging AI\-driven discovery (e.g. AI Overviews, generative search) and how SEO strategy must adapt
* Hands\-on experience setting up, managing, and extracting insight from SEO tool stacks (e.g. SEMrush, Ahrefs, Moz), including tracking frameworks and reporting
* Strong analytical capability — comfortable turning data into clear commercial priorities
* Experience partnering closely with Product and Engineering teams to ship SEO improvements
* Solid understanding of schema, structured data, and advanced SERP features
* Experience working with modern CMS platforms (e.g. Webflow)
* Experience operating SEO across multiple markets and multi\-languages technical setup

  

  

### **About Vintage Cash Cow \& Arcavindi**


**Vintage Cash Cow and Arcavindi, exist to create a world where everything has value and nothing is wasted, on a global scale.**  

  

Together, we form the UK and European operations of the Vintage Group, united by one purpose, one mission, and one set of values.  

  

**Vintage Cash Cow** is our UK operation and the foundation of our business. It’s where our model was built, tested, and proven, making it easy for customers to sell multiple pre\-loved valuables in one simple, trusted journey. **Arcavindi** is our European operation. Built on the same proven model, it takes what works in the UK and adapts it for new markets across Europe, allowing us to scale our impact internationally.  

  

While our brands reflect different markets, **we are one business, working as one team**. Our people collaborate across borders, share ownership of outcomes, and bring the same care, fairness, and common sense to everything we do.  

  

Behind the scenes, we are building the **world’s largest international trading platform for pre\-loved items**, powered by expert people, smart data, and a shared belief in the circular economy.  

  

Every item we buy is rehomed, reused, or responsibly recycled — keeping valuable materials in play and out of landfill, and ensuring we always treat customers and their treasures with care and respect.  

**Equal Opportunities**  

At Vintage Trading Limited, we’re committed to creating an inclusive environment where everyone feels heard, respected, and able to bring their authentic self to work. We believe that diversity fuels innovation, creativity, and success.  

  

We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences, and we work hard to ensure equitable opportunities for all. If you're excited about this role but don’t meet every requirement, we still encourage you to apply, your unique skills and experiences might be exactly what we need now or in the future.  

  

If you need any adjustments or accommodations during the hiring process, just let us know and we’ll do our best to support you.  

  

Your personal data will be handled in accordance with our Privacy Notice.

title: Polymer Scientist - Senior Scientist I
description:
**Job Summary**


Does the idea of exploring polymer properties and turning insights into innovative applications inspire you?  

  

IFF is a global leader in flavors, fragrances, food ingredients, and bioscience solutions. Our innovations power everyday products through sustainable, cutting\-edge science.  

  

At our Leiden site—home to 37 scientists from 12 nationalities—we have a proud history rooted in the early days of modern biotechnology. Today, we continue to lead the industry in sustainable bio\-solutions.  

  

Our R\&D team designs microbial cell factories to create engineered enzymes used in food \& beverages, home \& personal care, animal nutrition, grain processing, biopolymers, and more. We drive innovation from idea to production, using generative AI, advanced biomolecular engineering, high throughput screening, and full\-scale testing at IFF manufacturing plants.  

  

IFF Health \& Biosciences is currently developing a new family of polysaccharides with broad industrial potential. This work spans biocatalysis, polymer process design, and application development, requiring strong cross functional collaboration.
We are looking for an enthusiastic Polymer Scientist to join our Designed Enzymatic Biomaterials (DEB) platform within the R\&D team in Leiden. In this role, you will apply your expertise in polymer and material science to explore physical polymer properties and translate insights into innovative applications and product development. You will also collaborate closely with cross‑functional partners, including process development.


Success in this role requires strong hands‑on laboratory skills, a proactive mindset, and the ability to connect scientific findings to business needs—such as cost‑in‑use considerations and competitive product or process evaluations.


This Leiden based role involves close interaction with international IFF teams. Occasional travel to R\&D sites in Europe and North America may be required, along with participation in online meetings outside standard working hours.

**Where You’ll Make a Difference**

* Focus on understanding the fundamental material science properties of the polysaccharides and their chemical derivatives, and how these properties can add value in customer applications
* Identify new opportunities for polysaccharides in diverse industrial applications
* Develop and optimize lab workflows, be involved in laboratory and customer pilot trials
* Contribute to lab management, by developing and building laboratory capabilities
* Assure digitalization of generated data
* Interact with internal and external cross\-functional scientific teams
* Read and apply relevant scientific literature
* Present research activities at internal and external meetings

**What Makes You the Right Fit**

* PhD in Material Science, Physical Chemistry or similar discipline with 1\-3 years of industrial experience
* Strong hands\-on lab skills with excellent attention to detail and recordkeeping
* Proven record in polymer innovation: tuning ideas into projects and projects into products
* Proven problem\-solving capabilities and ability to work independently
* Effective in fast\-growing, dynamic, and evolving environments
* Highly motivated, curious, and proactive in scientific work
* Excellent interpersonal and communication skills; strong team player
* Adaptable, eager to learn, and fluent in spoken and written English

**How You Would Stand Out**

* Experience with water\-soluble polymer formulation/characterization or biodegradable polymer design is a plus
* Knowledge of polysaccharide material processing is advantageous
* Knowledge of the fabric care and personal care polymers space is a plus

**Why Choose Us?**

* Join a dynamic, innovative, and multicultural team of highly skilled scientists
* Grow professionally and build your career in biotechnology, in an energetic, inclusive, and multicultural environment
* Attractive salary and attractive compensation and benefits, such as:
* Individual Choice Budget of 8% of the annual base salary, employee choice to be paid in monthly instalments or to exchange for additional time off up to 15 extra days per year
* 25 vacation days and 3 bridge days, of which 2 of them are chosen by the employer
* Annual Incentive Plan


We are a global leader in taste, scent, and nutrition, offering our customers a broader range of natural solutions and accelerating our growth strategy. At IFF, we believe that your uniqueness unleashes our potential. We value the diverse mosaic of the ethnicity, national origin, race, age, sex, or veteran status. We strive for an inclusive workplace that allows each of our colleagues to bring their authentic self to work regardless of their religion, gender identity \& expression, sexual orientation, or disability.


Visit IFF.com/careers/workplace\-diversity\-and\-inclusion to learn more

title: AI Evangelist
description:

* Define and monitor KPIs for adoption, effectiveness and ROI.

Apply through the ***“apply now”*** button on our website.

title: Data Scientist Finance/Controlling
description:
Data Scientist Finance/Controlling**At a glance**
---------------


Join our Analytics \& Innovation team as a Data Scientist and help Finance Controlling move faster with sharper insights and smarter processes. You’ll lead end\-to\-end data science projects, sourcing and modelling data in Databricks and building AI/ML solutions from first idea through production. You thrive in a supportive, collaborative team where knowledge sharing is part of the daily rhythm, and you bring strong programming skills (Python, PySpark, SQL), clear communication, and a hands\-on, independent mindset. In return, we offer a competitive salary, generous vacation, a personal development budget, and flexible hybrid working options.


**Your job**
------------


As a Senior Data Scientist, you take a leading role in data science initiatives that contribute to process optimisation and the generation of strategic insights for Finance. You design, develop and maintain AI and ML models. You initiate innovation projects, make model choices, and are co\-responsible for bringing new models into production. Your work sets standards for quality and helps develop the team further.


In your role, you will

* Unlock, model and structure internal and external data in our Databricks environment.
* Design, develop and maintain advanced models and applications using AI and ML techniques, from experimentation through production deployment.
* Perform complex analyses and research, make well\-founded model choices, explore new methodologies, and develop and operationalise use cases through to production.
* Safeguard the quality of models and analyses, mentor junior data scientists, and promote knowledge sharing and collaboration within the team and stakeholders.
**Working environment**
-----------------------


Analytics \& Innovation is part of Finance Controlling and forms a multidisciplinary, self\-organising team. Our mission is to make the Controlling and Finance organisation more data\-driven. We focus on three areas:

* Data insights: we create new insights for Controllers and the Business they support. We identify new data sources, combine Finance and Business data and apply (advanced) analytics techniques and models. In doing so, we support the transformation from expert\-based to model\-based decision\-making.
* Process optimisation: we continuously look for opportunities to improve existing processes, both within Controlling and Finance more broadly. We explore how new technologies such as GenAI/LLMs can support the work of our colleagues.
* People \& skills: we advise senior management on data opportunities, inspire and inform colleagues about new technologies and contribute to improving analytics skills across the organisation.
**Your profile**
----------------


The ideal candidate holds a master’s degree (WO) and has at least six years of relevant experience. They are skilled in Python, PySpark, SQL, and Databricks, with a proven record of independently initiating and leading complex data science projects. Their strong analytical skills and conceptual thinking enable them to turn complex analyses into actionable insights. With a proactive, solution\-focused attitude and a deep sense of responsibility, they are dedicated to continuous learning and staying up to date with the latest developments in data science and technology.


This candidate also dedicates themselves to mentoring junior team members, offering guidance and constructive feedback to support their growth and encourage knowledge sharing within the group. Collaboration is central to their approach, as they actively help build a high\-performing team environment and consistently value teamwork.

**We are offering**
-------------------

* An attractive salary, including holiday allowance and an 11% benefits budget
* Five weeks of vacation per year, with the option to purchase additional days
* Personal Development Budget of €1,000 per year (up to €3,000 if saved)
* Flexible hybrid working (in consultation)
* Free nationwide public transport annual pass
* Excellent pension scheme
**Interested?**
---------------


We look forward to receiving your application. Want to know more about the role or the team? Don’t hesitate to get in touch, we’re happy to tell you more.

**Your Future: Inclusive, Innovative, Sustainable**
---------------------------------------------------


At ABN AMRO, we believe in "**Banking for better, for generations to come.**" Equal opportunities for everyone are a crucial foundation, as we strive for an inclusive culture where all employees feel seen, heard, and valued. Our vision of **being a personal bank in the digital age** aligns perfectly with the demand for surprising insights and innovative solutions, born from a diverse interplay of cultures and experiences. We focus on customer experience, sustainability, and building a future\-proof bank, conducting annual reviews to ensure equal pay for equal work. Join a bank that embraces ingenuity and ambition, and make an impact with us for a better future.

 *If you want to apply for the B\-Able or Reboot program, make sure to mention it both in the title and the content of your resume.*

External recruitment agencies need to have a signed agreement with ABN AMRO BANK N.V., executed by a Talent Acquisition Specialist, when submitting a resume to a vacancy. No unsolicited services or offers, please.

title: (Senior) Consultant Digital HR
description:
At EY, we’re all in to shape your future with confidence.


We’ll help you succeed in a globally connected powerhouse of diverse teams and take your career wherever you want it to go.


Join EY and help to build a better working world.

 **Confidence in your impact today. Freedom to build tomorrow.**

  

At EY, you can be sure that you’re making a difference – for your client, for society, for your colleagues and for your own development. At the same time, you’ll have the freedom to work in a way that fits your life. Whether you choose to specialize, broaden your expertise or grow into leadership, you’re in the driver’s seat. Together with ambitious colleagues in an open and trusted culture, you aim for the best every day. Big on opportunity, friendly in collaboration. EY provides the resources you need to shape your career and make an impact in your field.

 **Your new job \| About your main responsibilities**

  

As a Senior Consultant Digital HR, you’ll be part of the People Consulting team. In this role, you’ll be responsible for:

* Guiding clients through digital HR transformations
* Working alongside clients and leading project workstreams from planning through implementation with your team.
* Analyzing the customer requirements concerning the design of HR systems and challenging the customer where necessary.
* Analyzing and optimizing HR processes.
* Applying change management and project management practices.
* Preparing and facilitating client workshops that bring HR technology to life.
* Contributing to generating profitable revenue by identifying additional engagement opportunities and building lasting client relationships.
* Influencing and persuading clients and stakeholders to gain support for project\-related initiatives and decisions.
* Building and maintaining your knowledge of HR technology and solutions on the market.
* Contributing to the continuous improvement and development of our products and services.
 **Yo****ur new team \| About your colleagues and the office**

 **Your team**


The People Consulting department consists of 40 professionals who have at least one thing in common. We have a human\-centric mindset and put humans at the center of our engagements to make a lasting impact for our clients. We closely work together across our competences to make a lasting client impact. The competencies within our team are Change Experience, HR Transformation, Leadership \& Culture, Digital HR en Talent \& Skills, AI \& Adoption and Organization \& Workforce Transformation.

 **Your office**


Our Amsterdam office is located at the Zuidas: a bustling area full of companies. In our Cross Towers, we welcome up to 1,500 people every day. For a breathtaking view of the city, head to the 22nd floor. Collaboration takes on a new meaning in our Wavespace, a high\-tech environment that connects clients and colleagues virtually across the globe.

 **Your skills and strengths \| About your unique contribution**

* A relevant master’s degree, e.g. in Business Administration, Change Management, Human Resources or a related field.
* Relevant working experience of 3 to 5 years within the HR field in a consulting/project environment with a strong affinity for HR technology, digital transformation and AI.
* Demonstrable experience in working with HR technology or implementing HR technology.
* Knowledge of the AI landscape (e.g., GenAI, automation, digital tools) and enthusiasm for guiding people and organizations in leveraging these technologies responsibly.
* Experience and/or curiosity in how to deploy AI within people related processes
* Skilled in managing stakeholders and client relationships in dynamic and complex environments.
* Resilient in stressful situations and able to perform well under pressure.
* Analytical problem\-solving skills, with the ability to provide input to insightful, practical, and sustainable solutions for our clients.
* A true team player with the ability to quickly and effectively integrate into new teams.
* Strong oral and written communication skills in English. Fluency in Dutch is preferred.
 **Your benefits \| About your perks and work enjoyment**

  

A competitive salary that reflects your role and responsibilities, depending on experience and qualifications. Plus, a solid pension plan for the future. In addition to your salary, you’ll receive holiday allowance and an annual bonus (based on performance and development).

* You’ll receive a fixed/temporary contract for 40 hours.
* A laptop and phone for both work and personal use, and a monthly internet allowance.
* 29 vacation days to recharge and unwind, plus the option to buy extra days.
* With the mobility budget, you decide how you travel. Choose a lease car, a discounted public transport subscription, or arrange transport easily via our app.
* At EY, we care about a healthy work\-life balance. Hybrid working makes this easier. Whether you're working from the office, at a client, or from home, we’ll make sure you have a well\-equipped home office.
* The opportunity to work up to 20 days per year from abroad.
* The option to swap 3 national holidays for non\-national ones.
* You’ll have the space to grow both personally and professionally. Whether you want to deepen your expertise, develop your personality, or improve your leadership style, we offer a broad range of trainings and courses.
* Your well\-being is our priority. We believe that when you feel good, you perform better. That’s why you’ll have access to the OpenUp wellbeing platform (including for your family). You can easily connect with psychologists and lifestyle experts within 36 hours and take part in wellbeing sessions.
* A strong focus on teaming and various internal networks where you can join all kinds of social activities. A positive work atmosphere and good collaboration with colleagues contribute to your overall happiness at work.
  

All this within an environment where our core values of integrity, inclusiveness, and respect are central. We think it’s important that you can be yourself and fully develop into the best version of yourself. Curious about all our benefits? Click here.

 **Ready to shape your future with us?** **\| About your application process**

  

  

For this vacancy, we ask you to upload a motivation letter along with your CV. This gives you the opportunity to show us your drive, motivation, and personality. At EY, we support the use of AI tools like ChatGPT, as long as your own tone and unique personality remain visible.

  

Our first contact: After your application, our recruiter will let you know the next steps within 5 business days.


First interview: Before the interview, we’ll ask you to complete an online assessment. Not a standard questionnaire, but a game\-based assessment focusing on both your cognitive skills and behavioral preferences. We’ll discuss the results during the interview, which will include a colleague from the relevant department. This mutual introduction is a chance for us to get to know you, and for you to ask any questions you have.


Second interview: If we see a match and you’re still enthusiastic, we’ll invite you for a second interview. We’ll dive deeper into the role and other relevant topics. Sometimes, a business case is part of the process. A final interview may also follow.


We’re a match. You’ll receive an offer and we’ll perform a pre\-employment screening. Then your new adventure begins. Welcome to EY!

  

Research shows that women are less likely to apply if they don’t meet all the criteria. Please feel free to apply even if you don’t meet everything.

EY is building a better working world by creating new value for clients, people, society and the planet, while building trust in capital markets.
